actions.description: List the Adjust accounts this brand can query.
actions.long_description: Returns the Adjust accounts granted to the current brand (account_id + name). START HERE for any Adjust task: every other adjust action requires one of these account_ids. Do NOT ask the user for an account id — this action is the source of truth. Adjust supports multi-account, so one connected token can expose more than one account; each is pinned per request by its real account id.

actions.description: List the Adjust attribution/analytics tools available for an account.
actions.long_description: Returns the live Adjust MCP tool catalog for one granted account, each annotated with `requires_review`. The default response is a slim catalog without input schemas. Pass `tool` to fetch one exact tool's full input schema before calling `adjust/query`. Tools with `requires_review=true` (anything not on our read-only allowlist) must go through `adjust/request_change`, not `adjust/query`. Data is live from Adjust (realtime, no cached pipeline); Adjust MCP is Early Access, so treat results as best-effort.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Adjust account id (from adjust/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Adjust tool name; when provided, returns that tool's full input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.project_id, data.tools[].{name, description, requires_review[, input_schema when tool is provided]}

actions.description: Run a read-only Adjust tool (aggregated performance, cohorts, events, SKAN, app metadata).
actions.long_description: Forwards a read-only Adjust tool call for one granted account and returns live data (realtime — there is no cached sync pipeline). The hosted MCP exposes aggregated attribution/performance reporting (installs, cost, revenue, ROAS, retention by network/campaign/date), cohort and event aggregates, SKAN (iOS) data, and app metadata. Get the account_id from `adjust/list_accounts` (never ask the user for it). Only allowlisted read tools run immediately; anything else returns `requires_review` — call `adjust/request_change`. The response echoes which account was queried.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Adjust account id (from adjust/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Adjust tool name from adjust/list_tools.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured, truncated}

actions.description: Request a non-allowlisted Adjust tool (or a future write) via human approval.
actions.long_description: Submits an Adjust tool call that is not on our read-only allowlist for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the call is forwarded to Adjust only after a human approves. Use this for anything `adjust/query` rejected as requires_review. Note: account/org-wide listings (e.g. listing all accounts or users) are hard-hidden and never available, even here.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Adjust account id (from adjust/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Adjust tool name.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured}

actions.description: Create or append to a Google account-level negative-keyword shared set (atomic); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new SharedSet of type NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS plus N SharedCriterion rows in one atomic mutate, OR append keywords to an existing shared set.

Use when:
- The user wants a reusable account-wide negative-keyword list (omit shared_set_id).
- The user wants to add keywords to an existing shared set (pass shared_set_id).
- A list of brand-safety terms must be excluded across many campaigns.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to add negatives to one campaign only. Use action:ads/add_campaign_negative_keywords.
- The platform is Meta.

Workflow:
1. (If appending) Find the shared_set_id via GAQL: gaql_search(resource_name='shared_set', dimensions=['shared_set.id','shared_set.name'], filters={'shared_set.type': 'NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS'}).
2. Build the keyword list with text + match_type.
3. (Optional) Choose a list_name for a new shared set (ignored when shared_set_id is set).
4. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
5. (If new set) Attach the new set to campaigns via the Google Ads UI (no L1.5 attach handler).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- When creating: raw.results contains the sharedSetResult followed by N sharedCriterionResult entries.
- When appending: raw.results contains N sharedCriterionResult entries only.

Side effects:
- Atomic mutate that creates SharedCriterion rows (and optionally a SharedSet).
- Criteria are reversible via action:ads/remove_account_negative_keywords (list first to collect resource_names); deleting the shared set itself is not exposed (Google Ads UI only).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: The agent is not told that a newly created shared set is inert until manually attached to campaigns — the long_description mentions UI attach as a workflow step, but does not warn that NO campaigns are excluded until then. An agent may report success and believe the negatives are live. Also note: keywords item shape is documented as {text, match_type} but the docs do not state that plain strings are also accepted (defaulting to BROAD) — minor; agents will likely send objects. Capability gap: CampaignSharedSet attachment: creating a new SharedSet does not attach it to any campaign. Per the official guide, a negative-keyword shared set has no effect until linked to campaigns via CampaignSharedSet/CampaignSharedSetService. The capability docs disclose this ("Attach the new set to campaigns via the Google Ads UI; no L1.5 attach handler"), so the created set is inert until a human attaches it. This is the one capability dimension that is unreachable through the agent.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: keywords
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {text, match_type} entries. match_type one of EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD.

input_params.name: shared_set_id
input_params.description: ID of an existing shared set to append to. Find via gaql_search on shared_set resource (filter shared_set.type = NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS). Omit to create a new shared set.

input_params.name: list_name
input_params.description: Optional display name for a new shared set. Default "Account negative keywords". Ignored when shared_set_id is provided.

actions.output_shape: {"success": true, "message": "Appended 5 keyword(s) to shared set 123456", "results": [{"sharedCriterionResult": {...}}]}  / new set: {"success": true, "message": "Created account negative keyword set with 5 keyword(s)", "results": [{"sharedSetResult": {...}}, {"sharedCriterionResult": {...}}]}

actions.description: Add negative keywords to a Google ad group (batch); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Batch-add ad-group-level negative keywords to a Google ad group in one mutate. Negatives prevent the ad group from serving on the given queries.

Use when:
- The user wants to block specific search terms within a single ad group.
- A search_terms report shows wasted spend on terms irrelevant to this ad group but potentially valid for other ad groups in the same campaign.

Do not use when:
- The user wants campaign-wide negatives. Use action:ads/add_campaign_negative_keywords.
- The user wants account-wide negatives. Use action:ads/add_account_negative_keywords.
- The user wants positive keywords. Use action:ads/add_keywords.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not expose keyword negatives.

Workflow:
1. Inspect search terms via action:ads/query_single_dimension.
2. Build the keyword list with text + match_type.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count added.

Side effects:
- Per-keyword ad_group_criterion_operation.create with negative=True, all in one mutate.
- match_type validated against {EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD}.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_ad_group_negative_keywords (list first to collect resource_names).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: keywords accepts three shapes (list of dicts, JSON-array string, or list of bare strings). Capability docs mention 'list (or JSON string) of {text, match_type}' but do not state that bare-string entries are accepted and silently default match_type=BROAD, nor that entries with empty/missing text are silently dropped (no error). An agent passing malformed entries could submit fewer keywords than intended without warning. Also note: match_type default behavior (omitted -> BROAD) is not stated in the capability docs; an agent expecting EXACT to be the default, or expecting an error on omission, would be surprised. Capability gap: No remove/L1.5 delete handler for ad-group negatives — once added, the agent cannot undo via this capability set (capability docs explicitly say 'no L1.5 remove handler exposed; reversible via the Google Ads UI'). This is a one-directional capability gap, not a bug.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id from action:ads/query_single_dimension(dimension='adgroup').

input_params.name: keywords
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {text, match_type} entries. match_type one of EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD.

actions.description: Add an audience criterion to an ad group (ENABLED); supports USER_LIST, USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_INTENT, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Attach an audience criterion on a Google ad group. Supports multiple criterion types: USER_LIST (default), USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_INTENT, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE.

Use when:
- The user wants to add an audience targeting layer to an existing ad group.
- A copy workflow needs to replicate ad_group_criterion audience settings.

Do not use when:
- The target is a campaign. Use action:ads/add_campaign_audience_targeting.
- The platform is Meta. Meta uses targeting inside the ad set spec.

Workflow:
1. Identify the audience resource_id via gaql_search on ad_group_criterion or campaign_criterion.
2. Call this action with criterion_type and resource_id.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via gaql_search on ad_group_criterion.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.

Side effects:
- Creates an ad_group_criterion with status=ENABLED.
- Sets bid_modifier if provided.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_audience_targeting (by criterion resource_name).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: criterion_type valid values are listed, but the docs do not tell the agent how to obtain the resource_id for non-USER_LIST types (USER_INTEREST category ids, CUSTOM_INTENT/customInterests ids, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE ids, COMBINED_AUDIENCE ids). The Workflow only mentions gaql_search on ad_group_criterion/campaign_criterion (a copy flow), not how to discover a fresh user-interest/custom-audience id. The agent must guess the lookup path for those types. Also note: No mention of campaign-type restrictions (USER_INTEREST not on search-only, display-only USER_LIST not on search/shopping, CUSTOM_INTENT positive-only). The agent can pick a criterion_type that the parent campaign rejects and only learn at mutate time. Capability gap: negative / exclusion audience targeting: the official AdGroupCriterion.negative field is not exposed in handler/docs/whitelist, so the agent cannot exclude an audience (audience exclusion) at the ad-group level — a whole targeting dimension is unreachable via this action.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id from action:ads/gaql_search.

input_params.name: criterion_type
input_params.description: Audience criterion type: USER_LIST (default), USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_INTENT, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE.

input_params.name: resource_id
input_params.description: Audience resource id. For USER_LIST: user list id; for USER_INTEREST: user interest category id; etc. Legacy alias: user_list_id.

input_params.name: bid_modifier
input_params.description: Optional bid multiplier for this audience (e.g. 1.2 for +20%).

actions.description: Add Google callout assets to a campaign (batch, atomic); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Batch-create Callout assets AND CampaignAsset link rows for a Google campaign in one atomic mutate. Callouts are short marketing phrases shown below the ad.

Use when:
- The user wants to add callout extensions like 'Free shipping' or '24/7 support'.

Do not use when:
- The user wants clickable extensions. Use action:ads/add_sitelinks.
- The user wants structured headers / values. Use action:ads/add_structured_snippets.
- The platform is Meta.

Workflow:
1. Build the callouts list, each with non-empty callout_text or text.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count of callouts added.

Side effects:
- Same model as add_sitelinks: assets + campaign_asset links in one atomic mutate.
- Raises ValueError if any entry has neither callout_text nor text.
- Reversible: detach the campaign links via action:ads/remove_campaign_assets (field_type=CALLOUT); the assets stay in the account for re-attachment.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: The 25-char (12 double-width) limit is stated in the callouts param description but there is no local pre-validation; the agent only learns of an over-length failure from a raw Google API error at mutate time. Docs could state the failure mode explicitly. Also note: callouts[].name is documented as accepted but its semantics are unclear to the agent — it is the internal Asset.name (a label), NOT a user-visible serving field. An agent might think it changes ad rendering. Capability gap: start_date / end_date — callout date scheduling (official optional CalloutAsset fields) cannot be set; callouts always serve continuously.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: callouts
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {callout_text|text, name?} entries. Each entry must have non-empty callout text (max 25 chars per callout).

actions.description: Append ChatGPT Ads ad units to a campaign.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Append ChatGPT Ads ad units to a campaign while keeping existing campaign ad units.

Use when:
- The user approved additional ad units for an existing campaign.
- Existing campaign ad units should remain attached.

Do not use when:
- The desired final set should exactly match a new list. Use replace_campaign_ad_units.
- The ad_unit_ids are unknown. Create or inspect ad units first.

Workflow:
1. Resolve campaign_id and ad_unit_ids.
2. Submit the append operation for HITL review.
3. Read the campaign back to confirm the final binding.

Side effects:
- Mutates the campaign's attached ad units by adding ids.
- Does not activate the campaign.

Output semantics:
- raw is the Gravity response for the ad-unit binding mutation.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: chatgpt_ads only

actions.description: Add an audience criterion to a campaign; supports USER_LIST, USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Attach an audience criterion on a Google campaign. Supports multiple criterion types: USER_LIST (default), USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE.

Use when:
- The user wants to add campaign-level audience targeting.
- A copy workflow needs to replicate campaign_criterion audience settings.

Do not use when:
- The target is an ad group. Use action:ads/add_audience_targeting.
- The platform is Meta. Meta uses targeting inside the ad set spec.

Workflow:
1. Query source campaign's audience criteria via gaql_search on campaign_criterion.
2. For each criterion, call this action with the matching criterion_type and resource_id.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_criterion.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.

Side effects:
- Creates a campaign_criterion of the specified type.
- Sets bid_modifier if provided.
- Additive — does not remove existing audience criteria.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_campaign_audience_targeting (by criterion resource_name).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from list_campaigns or gaql_search.

input_params.name: criterion_type
input_params.description: Google only: audience criterion type: USER_LIST (default), USER_INTEREST, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, COMBINED_AUDIENCE. CUSTOM_INTENT is ad-group-only. NOTE: positive campaign-level USER_LIST works only on SEARCH campaigns; for Display/PMax use negative=true. resource_id must match the chosen criterion_type (user list id for USER_LIST, user_interest_category id for USER_INTEREST via gaql_search on user_interest, custom_audience id for CUSTOM_AUDIENCE, combined_audience id for COMBINED_AUDIENCE). Source: constant enum; resource_id source depends on type (create_user_list output for USER_LIST, gaql_search for others).

input_params.name: resource_id
input_params.description: Audience resource id. For USER_LIST: user list id; for USER_INTEREST: user interest category id; etc. Legacy alias: user_list_id.

input_params.name: negative
input_params.description: Google only: set true to create an EXCLUDED (negative) audience criterion instead of a positive one. Required for campaign-level USER_LIST on Display/PMax campaigns (positive campaign-level user_list is Search-only) and for any audience exclusion. Cannot be combined with bid_modifier (the API rejects bid modifiers on negative criteria). Default false (positive ENABLED). Source: agent decision / user intent (exclude vs include); constant boolean.

input_params.name: bid_modifier
input_params.description: Google only: optional bid multiplier for this audience (e.g. 1.2 for +20%). Only valid on POSITIVE criteria -- must be omitted when negative=true (the API rejects bid modifiers on negative criteria). Source: agent decision / user-provided multiplier.

actions.description: Add negative keywords to a Google campaign (batch); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Batch-add campaign-level negative keywords to a Google campaign in one mutate. Negatives prevent the campaign from serving on the given queries.

Use when:
- The user wants to block specific search terms across the whole campaign.
- A search_terms report shows wasted spend on terms that should be excluded.

Do not use when:
- The user wants account-wide negatives (a new shared set). Use action:ads/add_account_negative_keywords.
- The user wants positive keywords. Use action:ads/add_keywords.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not expose keyword negatives.

Workflow:
1. Inspect search terms via action:ads/query_single_dimension.
2. Build the keyword list with text + match_type.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count added.

Side effects:
- Per-keyword campaign_criterion_operation.create with negative=True, all in one mutate.
- match_type validated against {EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD}.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_campaign_negative_keywords (list first to collect resource_names).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Capability docs phrase each keyword entry as '{text, match_type}' implying match_type is mandatory per entry, but the implementation defaults a missing match_type to BROAD (utils.py:68). The agent is not told that match_type is optional / defaults to BROAD, nor that a bare string is accepted. Also note: Capability docs do not state the official 80-char / 10-word text limit, so the agent may submit over-long negatives that fail the whole atomic batch with no local hint. Capability gap: No client-side enforcement of the official 80-character / 10-word keyword.text limit, so KEYWORD_HAS_TOO_MANY_WORDS / over-length errors only surface from the Google API mid-batch (the mutate is atomic, so one bad keyword fails the whole batch).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: keywords
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {text, match_type} entries. match_type one of EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD.

actions.description: Review-gated add of a conversion_group rule.
actions.long_description: Adds a rule to an Admin View conversion_group after human approval. The server validates conversion_action_id/account_id against the current brand's granted ad accounts at approval execution time.

input_params.name: _summary

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

actions.description: Add keywords to a Google ad group (batch, ENABLED); review-gated, reversible per keyword.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Batch-add positive keywords to a Google ad group in one mutate. Created with status=ENABLED because criteria are not lifecycle resources.

Use when:
- The user wants to add new search keywords with explicit match types.
- A workflow needs to expand an ad group's targeting after checking existing keywords via GAQL.

Do not use when:
- The user wants negative keywords. Use action:ads/add_campaign_negative_keywords or action:ads/add_account_negative_keywords.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not use keyword targeting; use ad set targeting.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/gaql_search to choose ad_group_id.
2. Call action:ads/gaql_search to avoid duplicates.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count added; raw.results contains one entry per keyword.
- Returns success=false with 'No valid keywords provided.' if the list parses empty.

Side effects:
- Batch of ad_group_criterion_operation.create entries in one mutate.
- Status=ENABLED; the keyword starts matching once the parent ad group is enabled.
- Reversible per keyword via action:ads/remove_keyword (terminal REMOVED).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id from action:ads/gaql_search.

input_params.name: keywords
input_params.description: List of {text, match_type} objects (or a JSON string). match_type one of EXACT, PHRASE, BROAD.

input_params.name: cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: Google only: optional per-criterion CPC bid override applied to every keyword in this batch, in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Now actually applied on create. Source: user-provided / agent-chosen bid amount converted to micros.

input_params.name: final_urls
input_params.description: Google only: optional keyword-level landing-page override (list of full https URLs) applied to every keyword in this batch; overrides the ad-level final URL for these criteria. Source: user-provided landing page URL(s).

input_params.name: final_mobile_urls
input_params.description: Google only: optional keyword-level mobile landing-page override (list of full https URLs) applied to every keyword in this batch. Source: user-provided mobile landing page URL(s).

actions.description: Add Google sitelink assets to a campaign (batch, atomic); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Batch-create Sitelink assets AND CampaignAsset link rows for a Google campaign in one atomic mutate.

Use when:
- The user wants to add sitelink extensions (extra links shown below the ad).

Do not use when:
- The user wants callouts (short marketing phrases). Use action:ads/add_callouts.
- The user wants structured snippet headers. Use action:ads/add_structured_snippets.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not use sitelink extensions.

Workflow:
1. Build the sitelink list with link_text and final_urls per entry.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count of sitelinks added.
- raw.results contains assetResult entries (the new assets) followed by campaignAssetResult entries (the campaign links).

Side effects:
- Creates N Asset rows (SITELINK type) AND N CampaignAsset link rows in one atomic mutate. Uses temp resource names for in-batch linking.
- Reversible: detach the campaign links via action:ads/remove_campaign_assets (field_type=SITELINK); the assets stay in the account for re-attachment.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: sitelinks
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {link_text|text, final_urls|urls, name?} entries. link_text is the visible link label.

actions.description: Add a Google structured snippet asset to a campaign; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create one structured snippet asset (header + values list) and link it to a Google campaign.

Use when:
- The user wants to highlight a list of services, brands, models, etc. under a structured header.

Do not use when:
- The user wants sitelink URLs. Use action:ads/add_sitelinks.
- The user wants callouts. Use action:ads/add_callouts.
- The platform is Meta.

Workflow:
1. Choose a header from Google's predefined list (e.g. Services, Types, Brands).
2. Build the values list.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.results contains exactly one assetResult and one campaignAssetResult.

Side effects:
- Creates one structured-snippet Asset and one CampaignAsset link in one mutate.
- Reversible: detach the campaign link via action:ads/remove_campaign_assets (field_type=STRUCTURED_SNIPPET); the asset stays in the account for re-attachment.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: header
input_params.description: Google only: header from Google's predefined, locale-specific structured-snippet header set. Canonical English headers include: Amenities, Brands, Courses, Degree programs, Destinations, Featured hotels, Insurance coverage, Models, Neighborhoods, Service catalog, Shows, Styles, Types. NOTE 'Services'/'Programs' are NOT canonical (use 'Service catalog' / 'Degree programs'). Header is language-dependent: on non-English accounts use that locale's header strings or Google rejects it. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent from Google's predefined header list (https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/data/structured-snippet-headers).

input_params.name: values
input_params.description: Google only: list (or JSON string) of 3 to 10 value strings matching the header, each 1-25 characters. Fewer than 3, more than 10, or any value over 25 chars is rejected (the asset+link mutate is atomic and fails wholesale on a single bad value). Source: user-provided / agent-composed snippet items consistent with the chosen header.

actions.description: Attach a Google label to an ad group; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create an ad_group_label join row so a label appears on a Google ad group.

Use when:
- The user wants to tag an ad group with an existing label_id.

Do not use when:
- The label does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_label first.
- The user wants to label a campaign. Use action:ads/apply_campaign_label.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/apply_label.

Workflow:
1. Look up label_id via action:ads/create_label or gaql_search.
2. Choose ad_group_id via action:ads/gaql_search.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.

Side effects:
- Creates an ad_group_label join row via mutate.create.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_ad_group_label.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: customer_id is required by the handler but never surfaced to the agent as a parameter; the capability doc only mentions account_id. This is correct by design (account_id->customer_id mapping is internal) but an agent reading only the handler would not know account_id is the controllable source. Low impact. Also note: The doc workflow says 'Choose ad_group_id via action:ads/gaql_search' but does not give an example GAQL (e.g. SELECT ad_group.id, ad_group.name FROM ad_group). Minor — agent must construct the query. Capability gap: No remove/detach handler for ad_group_label (official AdGroupLabelOperation supports remove). Docs explicitly note 'no L1.5 remove handler exposed; reversible via Google Ads UI' — detaching a label via the agent is impossible. This is a deliberate scope limit, not a break of the apply happy-path.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id from action:ads/gaql_search.

input_params.name: label_id
input_params.description: Label id from action:ads/create_label.

actions.description: Attach a Google label to a campaign; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a campaign_label join row so a label appears on a Google campaign.

Use when:
- The user wants to tag a campaign with an existing label_id.

Do not use when:
- The label does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_label first.
- The user wants to label an ad group. Use action:ads/apply_ad_group_label.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/apply_label (different semantics — by name).

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/create_label or look up label_id via gaql_search.
2. Call action:ads/list_campaigns to choose campaign_id.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_label.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.

Side effects:
- Creates a campaign_label join row via mutate.create.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_campaign_label.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: The capability doc tells the agent to obtain label_id via 'action:ads/create_label or gaql_search', but does not give the exact GAQL (e.g. SELECT label.id, label.name FROM label) nor warn that create_label is review-gated (HITL via _batch/submit), so in a single turn the label_id may not yet exist when apply_campaign_label is attempted — a sequencing ambiguity the agent could mishandle. Also note: No mention of the non-idempotent behavior: re-applying an existing (campaign,label) pair errors. The agent is not told to verify absence first, so a retry after a partial success could surface a confusing duplicate error. Capability gap: No L1.5 remove handler is exposed (capability docs explicitly note 'no L1.5 remove handler exposed'; removal only via Google Ads UI). Removing a campaign label is a normal CampaignLabelService remove operation, so the unlabel dimension is unreachable through the agent. P1-ish but documented as intentional.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: label_id
input_params.description: Label id from action:ads/create_label.

actions.description: Review-gated onboarding action that creates missing conversion groups and applies selected rules/goals/manual campaigns from templates.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Apply user-approved conversion group setup recommendations from suggest_conversion_group_setup. This is the default write path for onboarding: it can create missing Admin View groups and add explicitly selected rules, goal links, or manual campaign include/exclude entries in one human approval.

Safety semantics:
- Existing groups are not overwritten by default; precise updates still use update_conversion_group with expected_version.
- Every rule target is validated against the current brand's granted ad accounts.
- The action is review-gated and initially returns pending_review_id.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: templates
input_params.description: Template objects. Each requires slug/template_slug and may include name, description, derived_metrics, metadata, rules[], goal_ids[], manual_campaigns[].

actions.description: Apply a Meta ad label (by NAME) to a campaign/adset/ad/creative; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Apply a Meta ad label to an object (campaign, ad set, ad, or creative). Unlike Google, Meta resolves the label by NAME (case-sensitive), not by id.

Use when:
- The user wants to tag an existing object with a previously-created label.

Do not use when:
- The label does not exist. Use action:ads/create_ad_label first.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/apply_campaign_label or apply_ad_group_label.

Workflow:
1. Look up the exact label name via action:ads/list_ad_labels.
2. Identify object_id (campaign / adset / ad / creative id).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.

Side effects:
- POSTs {object_id}/adlabels with body adlabels=[{name: label_name}] (JSON-stringified).
- If label_name does not exist as a label, Meta returns an error.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_label.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: create_ad_label long_description says 'raw.id is the new ad label id (note: Meta apply_label uses NAME, not id)'. The agent gets an id back from create but must supply name to apply — the docs flag this but it is an easy footgun if the agent stores only the id. Also note: Docs do not state that label resolution is account-scoped: if the same display name exists in multiple labels (Meta does allow duplicate-named labels) the by-name resolution behavior is ambiguous and the agent cannot disambiguate (only id could). Capability gap: execution_options=['validate_only'] — a native dry-run that would let the HITL review/_batch flow validate the label application without mutating; absent from handler, allowlist, and capability docs.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: object_id
input_params.description: Target object id (campaign / ad set / ad / creative id) to label.

input_params.name: label_name
input_params.description: Label name (case-sensitive) from action:ads/list_ad_labels. Meta resolves by name, not id.

actions.description: Toggle archive state for one ChatGPT Ads ad unit; review-gated risk action.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Toggle archive state for one ChatGPT Ads ad unit after explicit user confirmation.

Use when:
- The current ad-unit state has been verified and the user wants to archive it.
- The user understands Gravity's endpoint is a toggle, not a one-way delete.

Do not use when:
- The current state is unknown. Read dashboard/campaign detail first.
- The intent is campaign removal. Campaign archive is not exposed because it is also a toggle.

Workflow:
1. Verify the target ad_unit_id and current state.
2. Present the toggle risk in the HITL summary.
3. Submit archive_ad_unit only after explicit approval.

Side effects:
- Calls Gravity's ad-unit archive toggle endpoint.
- Repeating the same action may unarchive the ad unit.

Output semantics:
- resource_id is the ad_unit_id when available or the requested id fallback.
- raw preserves the Gravity response.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: chatgpt_ads only

actions.description: Archive a Meta lead form (status=ARCHIVED); review-gated. Meta has no lead-form delete; collected leads remain readable.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Retire a lead form that should no longer be attached to new ads. Meta's Graph API does not support deleting lead forms — archiving is the only retirement path; ARCHIVED hides the form from ad-creation flows.

Use when:
- The user wants to clean up outdated or test lead forms on a Page.
- A form was created with wrong questions and must not be attached to new ads (create a corrected form via action:ads/create_lead_form first, then archive the old one).

Do not use when:
- The user wants the collected leads deleted. Archiving does NOT delete leads; they remain readable via action:ads/get_leads.
- The user wants to stop ads that use the form. Archiving does not pause delivery; pause or remove the ads instead (action:ads/update_ad / action:ads/remove_ad).
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Get page_id via action:ads/get_account_pages and confirm form_id via action:ads/list_lead_forms.
2. Check no running ad still relies on the form (action:ads/list_ads).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_lead_forms (status=ARCHIVED).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.

Side effects:
- POSTs status=ARCHIVED to {form_id} using the Page access token resolved for page_id (same page-token path as create_lead_form; requires pages_show_list / leadgen page access). When no page token can be resolved, the action fails with a permission error — the Meta credential itself stays valid; reconnect Meta with page access.
- ARCHIVED hides the form from ad-creation flows; it cannot be attached to new ads.
- Collected leads are NOT deleted and remain readable via action:ads/get_leads.
- Not reversible via this surface: Meta itself allows setting status back to ACTIVE (Meta UI / a Graph status write), but no unarchive action is exposed here — treat archive as terminal within this surface.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: page_id
input_params.description: Facebook Page id the form belongs to, from action:ads/get_account_pages. Used to resolve the Page access token required for lead-form writes.

input_params.name: form_id
input_params.description: Lead form id to archive, from action:ads/list_lead_forms or action:ads/create_lead_form (raw.id).

actions.description: Review-gated link of a platform conversion goal to a conversion group.
actions.long_description: Links a platform conversion goal to an Admin View conversion group after human approval. The group then inherits the goal's campaigns (auto source) in list_conversion_groups.groups[].campaigns. Requires expected_version.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

input_params.name: goal_id
input_params.description: Internal goal id from list_conversion_goals.

actions.description: Bulk-create Meta ad creatives in one review-gated request; executes items sequentially.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create multiple Meta ad creatives as one human-approved CLI/agent operation. This is a single-layer bulk action: it does not create a campaign tree or resolve cross-layer dependencies by itself.

Use when:
- The user has already prepared several Meta ad creatives with the same account_id.
- The operation is one hierarchy layer at a time, with all parent ids already known.
- The user wants one approval record and ordered per-item results.

Do not use when:
- The user needs a full campaign -> ad set -> ad tree in one request. Create each layer separately and feed returned ids into the next layer.
- The user only needs one object. Use action:ads/create_ad_creative instead.
- The platform is Google or the account_id is unknown.

Workflow:
1. Prepare a JSON items array. Every item must include unique client_ref.
2. Put fields accepted by action:ads/create_ad_creative either at the top level as shared defaults or on individual items as overrides.
3. Submit this action for review. Approval runs asynchronously for CLI bulk flows; poll the review for final item results.

Side effects:
- After approval, creates Meta ad creatives in the target ad account.
- Execution is sequential and non-transactional; already-created platform objects remain even if a later item fails.
- Review rejection creates no platform-side objects.

Output semantics:
- items[] preserves input order and includes index, client_ref, success, stage, created id field when available, and raw/error details.
- The operation is not transactional: earlier successes are not rolled back if a later item fails.
- No resume/idempotency in v1; use client_ref to map outputs and build a new retry file for failed items.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be meta.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id.

input_params.name: items
input_params.description: Non-empty array of item objects. Each item requires unique client_ref and may include fields accepted by action:ads/create_ad_creative.

actions.description: Review-gated clear of a manual campaign include/exclude on a conversion group.
actions.long_description: Removes a manual include or exclude for a campaign on an Admin View conversion group after human approval. Idempotent. Requires expected_version and the INTERNAL campaign_id.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

input_params.description: Internal campaign id.

actions.description: Create an ad on Google (RSA) or Meta in PAUSED state; review-gated, reversible via remove_ad.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new ad. On Google this is a Responsive Search Ad (RSA) requiring at least 3 headlines and 2 descriptions. On Meta it uses an existing creative_id. Meta lead-gen creatives are semantically preflighted before the create call. Always force-paused at create.

Use when:
- The user wants to add an ad to an ad group (Google) or ad set (Meta).
- A workflow needs to register a new RSA or creative-based ad.

Do not use when:
- The Meta creative does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_ad_creative first.
- The Meta creative is an inline lead-gen spec with lead_gen_form_id. Use create_ad_creative first and pass creative_id so the handler can validate the existing creative, adset, campaign, page, and form before creating the ad.
- The user wants to update an existing ad's URLs or creative. Use action:ads/update_ad.
- The Google RSA's headlines or descriptions need to change later. RSA creative fields are immutable; use remove_ad then create_ad.

Workflow:
1. (Google) Choose ad_group_id via action:ads/gaql_search; build headlines (>=3) and descriptions (>=2). (Meta) Choose adset_id via list_adsets and creative_id via list_ads or create_ad_creative.
2. (Meta lead-gen) Confirm the creative uses the same page as the lead form; the handler then checks destination_type=ON_AD, optimization_goal=LEAD_GENERATION, campaign objective OUTCOME_LEADS/LEAD_GENERATION, and ACTIVE form ownership.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_ads.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.resource_id is the new ad's numeric id; ad_group_ad_resource_name is the composite path needed for later remove_ad.
- Meta raw.id is the new ad id.
- Google returns {success: false, error: '...'} (no platform call) on RSA validation failure (fewer than 3 headlines or 2 descriptions).

- Meta lead-gen semantic failures return success=false with META_LEADGEN_* error_code before the create-ad write.

Side effects:
- Creates an AdGroupAd (Google) or Ad (Meta).
- Status is force-paused on both platforms regardless of caller input.
- No immediate spend; activation requires update_ad with status=ENABLED/ACTIVE.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_ad (Google REMOVED terminal; Meta archives).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google or meta

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Meta: ad set id from action:ads/list_adsets. Google: the dispatcher renames this to ad_group_id, so pass the Google ad group id.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Meta only (required): the ad name. Required by the registry and by Meta's Ad node create contract. Source: user-provided ad name.

input_params.name: final_urls
input_params.description: Google only (required): landing-page URLs for the RSA. Schema required flag now matches the registry's required_extras_by_platform=google. Source: user-provided landing-page URL(s).

input_params.name: headlines
input_params.description: Google only (required): 3-15 RSA headlines, each <=30 chars. Each item may be a plain string OR an object {text, pinned_field} where pinned_field is HEADLINE_1|HEADLINE_2|HEADLINE_3 to lock the asset into a fixed slot. Source: agent-generated ad copy; pinned_field is a constant enum chosen for brand/legal lines.

input_params.name: descriptions
input_params.description: Google only (required): 2-4 RSA descriptions, each <=90 chars. Each item may be a plain string OR {text, pinned_field} with pinned_field DESCRIPTION_1|DESCRIPTION_2. Source: agent-generated ad copy; pinned_field is a constant enum.

input_params.name: path1
input_params.description: Google only: first display-URL path segment shown after the domain (max 15 chars). Pure cosmetic display URL, independent of final_urls. Source: user-provided / agent-composed short keyword string.

input_params.name: path2
input_params.description: Google only: second display-URL path segment (max 15 chars). Requires path1 to be set; rejected otherwise. Source: user-provided / agent-composed short keyword string.

input_params.name: tracking_url_template
input_params.description: Google only: ad-level click-tracking URL template (ValueTrack/{lpurl} macros). Attaches tracking without changing final_urls. Source: user-provided tracking template, often from an ad-tech/analytics vendor.

input_params.name: final_url_suffix
input_params.description: Google only: query-string suffix appended to the final URL after redirects (ValueTrack params). Source: user-provided URL suffix string.

input_params.name: creative_id
input_params.description: Meta only: ID of a pre-created AdCreative to attach to this ad. Required UNLESS you pass an inline 'creative' spec instead. One of creative_id or creative must be provided. Source: output of create_ad_creative (the returned creative id).

input_params.name: creative
input_params.description: Meta only: inline AdCreative spec used INSTEAD of creative_id, e.g. {"object_story_spec": {"page_id": "...", "link_data": {...}}}. Provide either this or creative_id, not both. If both are sent the inline spec is ignored. Inline lead-gen creatives containing lead_gen_form_id are rejected; create the creative first and pass creative_id. Source: constructed by the agent from page_id + an uploaded image_hash (output of upload_image) / video_id.

input_params.name: tracking_specs
input_params.description: Meta only: list of conversion-tracking spec objects, e.g. [{"action.type": ["offsite_conversion"], "fb_pixel": ["<PIXEL_ID>"]}]. Source: pixel id from the account's tracking setup.

input_params.name: bid_amount
input_params.description: Meta only: ad-level bid override in the account currency's minor units (cents). Usually managed at the ad-set level; only set when an ad-specific bid cap is required. Source: user-provided bid cap (minor units).

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Meta only: ignored at create time. The handler always force-sets status=PAUSED so newly created ads are review-gated and never spend until explicitly activated via update_ad. Source: constant (always PAUSED at create).

actions.output_shape: {"success": true, "message": "Responsive Search Ad created successfully", "resource_id": "123", "results": [{"adGroupAdResult": {"resourceName": "customers/X/adGroupAds/AG~AD"}}]}  // Meta: {"id": "23..."}

actions.description: Create a Meta ad creative (image / video / carousel); review-gated, reversible via UI.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Build a Meta ad creative wired to a Page, with one of image_hash / image_url / video_id / child_attachments (carousel). The creative is then attached to ads.

Use when:
- The user wants to create a new ad creative for an existing media asset.
- A workflow needs a creative_id for action:ads/create_ad.

Do not use when:
- The user wants only an inline preview without persisting a creative. Use action:ads/generate_ad_preview.
- The image / video hasn't been uploaded. Use action:ads/upload_image or action:ads/upload_video first.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/create_ad directly.

Workflow:
1. Upload media via action:ads/upload_image or upload_video to get image_hash / video_id.
2. Get a page_id via action:ads/get_account_pages.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Wire the new creative_id into action:ads/create_ad.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new creative id.
- Returns success=false with 'create_ad_creative requires image_hash, image_url, video_id, or child_attachments' if no media identifier was provided.

Side effects:
- Builds object_story_spec from the supplied media:
  - video_id -> video_data (title=headline, image_url=thumbnail_url, image_hash=thumbnail_image_hash)
  - child_attachments -> link_data with child_attachments JSON list (carousel)
  - else -> link_data with image_hash OR picture=image_url
- For lead-gen image creatives, link_data.link and call_to_action.value.link must remain the external landing-page URL; do not use a Facebook Page short link or create_ad may fail with 'lead ad creative did not use external URL'.
- Public URLs (image_url, thumbnail_url) are validated for https, reachability, no IP literals.
- Removable via action:ads/remove_ad_creative (irreversible hard delete; fails while ads still reference the creative).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Creative display name.

input_params.name: page_id
input_params.description: Facebook Page id from action:ads/get_account_pages.

input_params.name: image_hash
input_params.description: Image hash from action:ads/upload_image.image_hash.

input_params.name: image_url
input_params.description: Public https image URL (alternative to image_hash).

input_params.name: video_id
input_params.description: Video id from action:ads/upload_video.id.

input_params.name: thumbnail_url
input_params.description: Public https thumbnail URL (used with video creatives).

input_params.name: thumbnail_image_hash
input_params.description: Meta image hash used as the thumbnail for video creatives. Prefer this when action:ads/upload_image already produced an image_hash for the video thumbnail.

input_params.name: child_attachments
input_params.description: Carousel child attachments list (alternative to single image/video).

input_params.name: message
input_params.description: Meta only: primary text of the ad. Required for carousel (child_attachments) creatives per AdCreativeLinkData. Source: user-provided ad copy.

input_params.name: headline
input_params.description: Headline shown above the link description.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Meta only: description below the headline. For image/carousel it maps to link_data.description; for video creatives it maps to AdCreativeVideoData.link_description (the field name differs). Source: user-provided ad copy.

input_params.name: link
input_params.description: Meta only: destination URL. Effectively required for single-image and carousel creatives (Graph rejects with error 100 'link is required' otherwise). For image lead-form creatives this MUST be an external landing-page URL; the handler keeps it on link_data.link and call_to_action.value.link so create_ad(creative_id) remains valid for ON_AD lead ads. For video creatives the link only takes effect through the call_to_action and is ignored unless a CTA is built. Source: user-provided landing-page URL.

input_params.name: caption
input_params.description: Meta only: caption (display URL) shown in the link attachment for image/carousel link_data creatives. Source: user-provided display URL string.

input_params.name: url_tags
input_params.description: Meta only: query-string appended to the creative's link URLs for click tracking (e.g. 'utm_source=meta&utm_id=...'). Source: user-provided UTM / tracking template.

input_params.name: lead_gen_form_id
input_params.description: Meta only: Lead Ad instant-form id; placed in call_to_action.value.lead_gen_form_id so the creative opens the form. Do not place this id directly on link_data or video_data; Meta rejects those story-data fields. When supplied without call_to_action_type the handler defaults the CTA type to SIGN_UP. For image creatives also provide link as the external landing-page URL; do not use a Facebook Page short link. Source: raw.id output of action:ads/create_lead_form.

input_params.name: object_story_id
input_params.description: Meta only: id of an existing published Page post (format <page_id>_<post_id>) to boost. Mutually exclusive with building object_story_spec; when provided, image_hash/video_id/child_attachments/link/message are ignored. Source: user-provided existing Page post id, or a post id surfaced by a Page-content read.

input_params.name: call_to_action_type
input_params.description: Meta only: CTA button. The documented set (LEARN_MORE, SHOP_NOW, SIGN_UP, DOWNLOAD, GET_QUOTE, CONTACT_US, SUBSCRIBE, APPLY_NOW, BOOK_TRAVEL) is a subset of Meta's enum; the chosen value must be compatible with the campaign objective or Meta rejects the creative. For video creatives the destination URL only takes effect when a CTA is present. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: instagram_user_id
input_params.description: Optional Instagram user id for cross-posting. Must be the ad-account-connected IG identity from action:ads/get_connected_instagram_accounts — a public IG @handle or the Facebook Page id is rejected by the ads API.

input_params.name: instagram_actor_id
input_params.description: Optional Instagram actor id (legacy alias).

input_params.name: asset_feed_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: dynamic creative (Advantage+ creative) spec. When supplied, Meta auto-combines multiple assets into optimized variations, and the handler builds a minimal object_story_spec (page identity only) alongside it. Shape: {images:[{hash}] and/or videos:[{video_id,thumbnail_hash}], bodies:[{text}], titles:[{text}], descriptions:[{text}], link_urls:[{website_url}], call_to_action_types:[...], ad_formats:[...]}. REQUIRED sub-keys: at least one of images/videos, AND ad_formats (e.g. ['SINGLE_IMAGE'], ['CAROUSEL'], ['SINGLE_VIDEO']) — the handler rejects the call otherwise. Mutually exclusive with the single-asset fields (image_hash / image_url / video_id / child_attachments / object_story_id): put those assets inside the asset_feed_spec arrays. Top-level copy fields (message / headline / link / description / caption) are ignored on this path — put copy in bodies / titles / descriptions / link_urls instead. Top-level call_to_action_type and lead_gen_form_id are REJECTED (not silently dropped) — put call_to_action_types (and the lead form) inside the asset_feed_spec. Prerequisite: the ad set that runs this creative must be dynamic-creative-enabled (see update_adset.is_dynamic_creative). Source: user-provided per Meta Advantage+ creative / asset_feed_spec reference.

actions.description: Create a Google Ads ad group in PAUSED state; review-gated, reversible via remove_ad_group.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new ad group as a SEARCH_STANDARD child of a campaign. Always force-paused so keywords and ads can be attached before activating.

Use when:
- The user wants to add a new ad group to a Google campaign.
- A workflow needs an ad_group_id to host keywords and ads.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Meta uses ad sets; use action:ads/create_adset.
- The user wants to clone an existing ad group. There is no clone action; inspect existing ad groups via action:ads/gaql_search, then create_ad_group with the same parameters.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_campaigns to choose a campaign_id.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. After approval, call action:ads/add_keywords or create_ad to populate.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_id is the new ad group's numeric id.

Side effects:
- Creates an AdGroup with type_=SEARCH_STANDARD.
- Force-paused at create time (status=PAUSED); no immediate spend.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_ad_group (terminal REMOVED).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Parent campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Ad group display name.

input_params.name: cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: Default CPC bid in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000).

input_params.name: type
input_params.description: Google only: AdGroup type enum, immutable after create. Defaults to SEARCH_STANDARD when omitted. Must match the parent campaign's channel or the platform rejects it. Source: constant enum value chosen by the agent based on the parent campaign type from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Google only: ad-group-level target CPA override in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Only effective when the parent campaign uses TargetCpa or MaximizeConversions with a target CPA. Source: user-provided target CPA budget converted to micros.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Google only: ad-group-level target ROAS override as a ratio (e.g. 4.0 = 400%). Only effective when the parent campaign uses TargetRoas or MaximizeConversionValue with a target ROAS. Source: user-provided target return-on-ad-spend ratio.

input_params.name: tracking_url_template
input_params.description: Google only: ad-group-level tracking URL template for click measurement. Source: user-provided tracking URL template (often from a third-party click tracker).

input_params.name: final_url_suffix
input_params.description: Google only: ad-group-level suffix appended to the final URL when parallel tracking is enabled. Source: user-provided URL suffix string.

actions.description: Create a Meta ad label on an account; review-gated, reversible via UI.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a Meta ad label for tagging campaigns, ad sets, ads, or creatives.

Use when:
- The user wants a new Meta-side tag (e.g. by quarter, theme).

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/create_label.
- The user wants to apply an existing label. Use action:ads/apply_label.

Workflow:
1. Choose name.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Apply via action:ads/apply_label.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new ad label id (note: Meta apply_label uses NAME, not id).

Side effects:
- POSTs {account_id}/adlabels.
- Reversible only via the Meta UI.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: account_id is marked required=True in the capability docs but its example/description is shared with Google ('For Google, digits only without dashes') even though create_ad_label is Meta-only; minor confusion, harmless because the action is gated to meta. Also note: Capability docs do not state that account_id may be omitted (executor auto-resolves from the active/owned account). Not a functional gap, but the agent is told it is required, which is the safe instruction. Capability gap: None material. The ad-account adlabels create edge has no additional commonly-needed official parameters (no description/color/reference fields on the modern AdLabel create). The action is contract-complete for its scope.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Ad label display name (case-sensitive; used as the key when applying).

actions.description: Create a Meta automated ad rule (PAUSE / CHANGE_BUDGET / NOTIFY); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a scheduled automated rule that evaluates conditions against scoped objects and runs an execution (PAUSE, CHANGE_BUDGET, NOTIFY).

Use when:
- The user wants to automate pausing ads above a spend threshold.
- A workflow needs scheduled budget adjustments based on metrics.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Google has automated rules but they are not exposed here.
- The user wants one-off mutations. Use the matching action:ads/update_* directly.

Workflow:
1. Build evaluation_spec (e.g. {evaluation_type, filters: [{field, operator, value}]}).
2. Build execution_spec (e.g. {execution_type: PAUSE, execution_options: [...]}).
3. Optionally build schedule_spec.
4. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new rule_id.

Side effects:
- POSTs {account_id}/adrules_library; the rule begins running on its schedule.
- The rule can pause ads or change budgets automatically once scheduled — review carefully.
- Reversible only via the Meta UI.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Ad rule display name.

input_params.name: evaluation_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: evaluation spec. Shape {evaluation_type: SCHEDULE|TRIGGER, filters: [{field, operator, value}]}. MUST include a scoping filter on field 'entity_type' (e.g. {field:'entity_type', operator:'EQUAL', value:'AD'|'ADSET'|'CAMPAIGN'}) plus metric filters; metric thresholds should set a window via a 'time_preset' filter (e.g. LAST_7_DAYS). For TRIGGER use a trigger{type,field,value,operator} block. Source: agent-constructed from user intent (entity scope + thresholds).

input_params.name: execution_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: execution spec. Shape {execution_type: PAUSE|UNPAUSE|CHANGE_BUDGET|NOTIFICATION|PING_ENDPOINT, execution_options: [...]}. NOTE the enum value is NOTIFICATION (not NOTIFY). execution_options carries the action params: for NOTIFICATION a user_ids list; for CHANGE_BUDGET a change_spec {amount, unit, limit}; optionally an action_frequency. Source: agent-constructed from user intent (which action + its options).

input_params.name: schedule_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: schedule spec; REQUIRED when evaluation_spec.evaluation_type=SCHEDULE, otherwise optional. Shape {schedule_type: DAILY|HOURLY|SEMI_HOURLY|CUSTOM, schedule: [...]} where CUSTOM needs explicit time windows. Source: agent-constructed from user cadence.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Meta only: ad rule lifecycle status. Valid values are ENABLED, DISABLED, DELETED, HAS_ISSUES (the ad-rule enum, NOT the generic ACTIVE/PAUSED). Omit to default to ENABLED. Use DISABLED for an off/paused rule. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

actions.description: Create a ChatGPT Ads ad unit draft; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create one ChatGPT Ads ad unit before creating a campaign. Gravity campaigns require at least one ad_unit_id.

Use when:
- The user has approved ChatGPT Ads copy and landing page inputs.
- A deployment flow needs an ad_unit_id for action:ads/create_campaign.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google or Meta. Use their creative/ad actions instead.
- The user only needs a media plan draft. Use the ChatGPT Ads media-plan workflow.

Workflow:
1. Prepare description, landing_page, static_ad_text, and static_cta.
2. Submit create_ad_unit for HITL review.
3. Use the returned resource_id/id in create_campaign.ad_unit_ids.

Side effects:
- Creates a draft ad unit in the advertiser dashboard.
- Rejects upstream server-managed fields like ad_text_mode, source, status, review_status, review_reason, metadata, and generate_static_text before calling the dashboard API.
- Does not create or activate a campaign and does not start spend by itself.

Output semantics:
- resource_id is the ad unit id when the dashboard response includes id.
- raw preserves the Gravity response for later campaign binding.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: chatgpt_ads only

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads advertiser account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional ad unit name.

input_params.name: headline
input_params.description: Optional ad unit headline.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Required ad unit description.

input_params.name: landing_page
input_params.description: Required ad unit landing page URL.

input_params.name: static_ad_text
input_params.description: Required static ad body text.
input_params.example: Meet the shoes made from nature.

input_params.name: static_cta
input_params.description: Required static call to action.

input_params.name: brand_name
input_params.description: Advertised brand name.

input_params.name: favicon_url
input_params.description: Optional favicon URL.

input_params.name: image_url
input_params.description: Optional image URL.

input_params.name: utm_params
input_params.description: Optional UTM parameter list.

actions.description: Create a Meta ad set in PAUSED state; review-gated, reversible via remove_adset.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new Meta ad set inside a campaign, with billing, optimization, targeting, and budget. Force-paused at create so ads can be attached before activating.

Use when:
- The user wants to add a new ad set to a Meta campaign.
- A workflow needs an adset_id to host ads.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/create_ad_group.
- The campaign already has a matching ad set. Reuse via action:ads/list_adsets.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_campaigns to choose a campaign_id.
2. Build the targeting spec (geo_locations, age_min/max, interests, ...).
3. Call action:ads/estimate_audience_size to validate reach if needed.
4. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
5. After approval, call action:ads/create_ad with the new adset_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API response: {id: '...'}; the envelope's resource_id matches.
- budget_daily_micros is converted to daily_budget in cents (int(micros/10_000)) by the dispatcher.

Meta lead-gen / ON_AD notes:
- For Campaign Budget Optimization campaigns, set is_cbo_adset=true and omit ad-set budget fields; budget stays on the campaign.
- destination_type=ON_AD + optimization_goal=LEAD_GENERATION uses an instant form flow. The handler preserves manual targeting by defaulting targeting.targeting_automation.advantage_audience to 0 when omitted.
- If targeting.instagram_positions contains explore_home, Meta also requires explore; the handler auto-adds explore when it is missing.

Side effects:
- Creates an AdSet under the campaign.
- Force-paused at create (status=PAUSED), overwriting any caller value.
- No immediate spend; activation requires a later action:ads/update_adset with status=ACTIVE.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_adset (archives; can un-archive to PAUSED).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Parent Meta campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Ad set display name.

input_params.name: billing_event
input_params.description: One of IMPRESSIONS, LINK_CLICKS, APP_INSTALLS, OFFER_CLAIMS, PAGE_LIKES, POST_ENGAGEMENT, THRUPLAY, VIDEO_VIEWS.

input_params.name: optimization_goal
input_params.description: One of REACH, IMPRESSIONS, LINK_CLICKS, OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS, LEAD_GENERATION, LANDING_PAGE_VIEWS, VALUE, APP_INSTALLS, THRUPLAY, POST_ENGAGEMENT.

input_params.name: targeting
input_params.description: Meta targeting spec object. Typical fields: geo_locations, age_min, age_max, interests, behaviors, flexible_spec, publisher_platforms, facebook_positions. For Instagram placements, explore_home requires explore; the handler auto-adds explore when missing. For ON_AD lead generation ad sets, targeting_automation.advantage_audience defaults to 0 if omitted.

input_params.name: budget_daily_micros
input_params.description: Meta only: daily budget in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000); converted to daily_budget cents. CONDITIONAL: required for non-CBO ad sets (or use lifetime_budget); MUST be omitted for CBO campaigns (set is_cbo_adset=true instead). Source: user-provided budget amount.

input_params.name: lifetime_budget
input_params.description: Lifetime budget in cents (Meta native unit). Alternative to budget_daily_micros.

input_params.name: bid_strategy
input_params.description: Meta only: ad-set bid strategy. One of LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP, LOWEST_COST_WITH_BID_CAP, COST_CAP, LOWEST_COST_WITH_MIN_ROAS. Cap strategies (LOWEST_COST_WITH_BID_CAP/COST_CAP) require bid_amount. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent based on the user's bidding intent.

input_params.name: is_cbo_adset
input_params.description: Meta only: handler control flag (not a Graph field). Set true when the parent campaign uses Campaign Budget Optimization (Advantage Campaign Budget); the ad-set budget is then omitted and budget stays on the campaign. Source: derived from the parent campaign's budget setting (list_campaigns shows daily_budget/lifetime_budget on the campaign => CBO). For lead-gen ON_AD ad sets under a CBO campaign, keep this true and do not try to move budget to the ad set.

input_params.name: dsa_beneficiary
input_params.description: Meta only: EU DSA beneficiary -- the brand/person/org that benefits from the ad. Mandatory for ad sets targeting EU-regulated countries, else the ad cannot be published. Source: user-provided beneficiary name (the advertising brand).

input_params.name: dsa_payor
input_params.description: Meta only: EU DSA payer -- who pays for the ad. Required alongside dsa_beneficiary for EU-regulated targeting. Source: user-provided payer name.

input_params.name: destination_type
input_params.description: Meta only: conversion destination. Common values WEBSITE, MESSENGER, WHATSAPP, INSTAGRAM_DIRECT, APP, ON_AD. Needed for messaging/lead/app destinations. Source: constant enum chosen from the campaign objective / user intent.

input_params.name: is_dynamic_creative
input_params.description: Meta only: enable Dynamic Creative on the ad set (required for Advantage+/dynamic-creative ad sets). Source: user intent (dynamic creative on/off).

input_params.name: pacing_type
input_params.description: Meta only: pacing control, e.g. ["standard"] (default) or ["no_pacing"]. List of strings. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: attribution_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: conversion attribution windows, list of {event_type, window_days} objects (e.g. [{"event_type":"CLICK_THROUGH","window_days":7}]). Source: constant config chosen by the agent for the conversion window.

input_params.name: bid_amount
input_params.description: Bid in cents (when optimization requires a manual bid).

input_params.name: promoted_object
input_params.description: Promoted object spec, e.g. {pixel_id, custom_event_type} for conversion optimization, or {page_id} for engagement campaigns.

input_params.name: start_time
input_params.description: ISO-8601 ad set start time.

input_params.name: end_time
input_params.description: ISO-8601 ad set end time.

actions.description: Create a reusable Google portfolio Target CPA or Target ROAS strategy.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a reusable Google portfolio Target CPA or Target ROAS strategy.

Use when:
- You need a reusable Google Target CPA or Target ROAS portfolio strategy.

Do not use when:
- You need a campaign-specific standard bidding strategy; use action:ads/update_campaign.

Workflow:
1. Read the current strategy or campaign state when changing an existing resource.
2. For write operations, submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify the resulting state with action:ads/get_bidding_strategy.

Output semantics:
- Returns the created, read, updated, listed, or removed Google portfolio strategy result.

Side effects:
- Creates, updates, or removes a Google portfolio strategy. Removing a strategy is irreversible and requires it to be unused.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Portfolio bidding strategy name; optional when updating.

input_params.name: bidding_strategy_type
input_params.description: Portfolio strategy type.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Required when bidding_strategy_type is TARGET_CPA.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Required when bidding_strategy_type is TARGET_ROAS.

actions.description: Create a campaign on Google, Meta, or ChatGPT Ads in PAUSED state; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new advertising campaign shell. Google, Meta, and ChatGPT Ads creation paths force the campaign into a paused/non-active state so the agent can finish setup and review before spend begins.

Use when:
- The user asks to launch a new campaign with a chosen objective and budget.
- A workflow needs a fresh campaign to host ad groups, ad sets, or ChatGPT Ads ad units.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to enable spend immediately. Create paused first, then use action:ads/update_campaign only after explicit activation approval.
- The user wants a Google Performance Max campaign. Use action:ads/create_pmax_campaign.
- The campaign already exists. Use action:ads/update_campaign instead.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. For Google choose campaign_type and budget_daily_micros; for Meta choose objective; for ChatGPT Ads create ad units first and pass landing_page, campaign_objective, budget, and a non-empty ad_unit_ids list.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. After approval, call action:ads/list_campaigns or action:ads/get_campaign to confirm the paused/non-active state.
