Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.resource_id is the new campaign's numeric id. Meta raw.id is the new campaign id. ChatGPT Ads raw.campaign_id or raw.id is surfaced as resource_id.

Side effects:
- Creates a Campaign on the platform; Google also creates a dedicated CampaignBudget named 'Budget for {name}' in the same atomic mutate.
- Status is force-paused/non-active regardless of any status the caller supplies.
- ChatGPT Ads create rejects upstream-removed fields like keywords, network_type, and network_config before calling the dashboard API.
- No immediate spend; spend begins only after a later explicit activation update.
- remove_campaign is available only for Google and Meta. ChatGPT Ads campaign archive is not exposed because Gravity's endpoint is a toggle.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google, meta, or chatgpt_ads

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Campaign display name; must be unique within the account.

input_params.name: campaign_type
input_params.description: Google only (required): campaign type. One of SEARCH, DISPLAY, SHOPPING, VIDEO. Use create_pmax_campaign for PERFORMANCE_MAX; unknown values default to SEARCH with a warning. Backend (registry/executor) rejects the call with HTTP 400 if omitted for Google. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent based on user intent.

input_params.name: budget_daily_micros
input_params.description: Google only (required) / Meta: daily budget in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Backend rejects with HTTP 400 if omitted for Google. For Meta this is the ONLY campaign-level daily budget input (becomes daily_budget in cents) and becomes mandatory when bid_strategy is supplied at the campaign level; use lifetime_budget instead for a fixed-duration budget. Source: user-provided budget amount converted to micros.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Google only: target cost-per-action in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Required companion when bidding_strategy=TARGET_CPA (the handler errors without it). Optional target when bidding_strategy=MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS — Google's standard tCPA form since standalone TargetCpa strategies were deprecated. Any other strategy rejects it with a clear error. Source: user-provided target CPA converted to micros.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Google only: target return on ad spend as a ratio (e.g. 4.0 = 400%). Required companion when bidding_strategy=TARGET_ROAS (the handler errors without it). Optional target when bidding_strategy=MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE — Google's standard tROAS form since standalone TargetRoas strategies were deprecated. Any other strategy rejects it with a clear error. Source: user-provided target ROAS ratio.

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Google only: campaign start date in YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to start-today when omitted. Source: user-provided date.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Google only: campaign end date in YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to no-end when omitted. Source: user-provided date.

input_params.name: network_settings
input_params.description: Google only: object controlling serving networks. Keys (booleans): target_google_search, target_search_network, target_content_network, target_partner_search_network. Omitted keys keep platform defaults. Source: user/agent-provided serving preferences (constant booleans).

input_params.name: bidding_strategy
input_params.description: Google only: one of MANUAL_CPC, MAXIMIZE_CLICKS, MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS, MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE, TARGET_CPA, TARGET_ROAS, TARGET_SPEND. Default: TARGET_SPEND.

input_params.name: max_cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: Google only: max CPC bid in micros; applies only to MAXIMIZE_CLICKS and TARGET_SPEND. Ignored for other strategies (warning emitted).

input_params.name: objective
input_params.description: Meta only (required): campaign objective. One of OUTCOME_TRAFFIC, OUTCOME_AWARENESS, OUTCOME_LEADS, OUTCOME_SALES, OUTCOME_ENGAGEMENT, OUTCOME_APP_PROMOTION. Required for Meta even though the shared spec previously marked it optional. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent from the user's campaign goal.

input_params.name: special_ad_categories
input_params.description: Meta only: special category list. Defaults to []. Values include EMPLOYMENT, HOUSING, CREDIT, ISSUES_ELECTIONS_POLITICS, ONLINE_GAMBLING_AND_GAMING.

input_params.name: buying_type
input_params.description: Meta only: AUCTION (default) or RESERVED.

input_params.name: bid_strategy
input_params.description: Meta only: LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP, LOWEST_COST_WITH_BID_CAP, COST_CAP, LOWEST_COST_WITH_MIN_ROAS. For Meta campaigns with campaign-level budget (CBO / Advantage Campaign Budget), the handler defaults to LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP when omitted so the downstream ad set does not inherit a cap strategy that requires bid_amount. Only choose a cap strategy when the user also provides the required bid/cost cap.

input_params.name: spend_cap
input_params.description: Meta only: lifetime account-level spend cap for the campaign in account-currency cents (NOT dollars, NOT micros). Source: user-provided cap converted to cents.

input_params.name: special_ad_category_country
input_params.description: Meta only: list of ISO country codes; REQUIRED whenever special_ad_categories contains a regulated category (HOUSING, EMPLOYMENT, CREDIT, ISSUES_ELECTIONS_POLITICS, FINANCIAL_PRODUCTS_SERVICES). Omit for non-regulated campaigns. Source: user-provided target country/countries of the regulated campaign.

input_params.name: lifetime_budget
input_params.description: Meta only: campaign-level lifetime budget in account-currency cents (CBO/Advantage Campaign Budget). Mutually exclusive with the daily budget derived from budget_daily_micros. Use for fixed-duration/scheduled campaigns (pair with stop_time). Source: user-provided total spend cap converted to cents (1 USD = 100).

input_params.name: is_adset_budget_sharing_enabled
input_params.description: Meta only: when no campaign-level budget is set, Meta requires this flag to be explicit (each ad set keeps its own budget). The handler defaults it to false for budget-less campaigns; pass true to let ad sets share budget. Source: user-provided campaign-budget-optimization choice.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Meta/Google: force-set to PAUSED by create_campaign regardless of caller value; spend begins only after a later update_campaign sets ACTIVE (Meta) / ENABLED (Google). Source: constant; not effective on create (handler overrides to PAUSED).

input_params.name: start_time
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO-8601 scheduled start time.

input_params.name: stop_time
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO-8601 scheduled stop time.

input_params.name: landing_page
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads only (required): final landing page URL for the campaign. Use the UTM-tagged URL prepared by the ChatGPT Ads media plan.

input_params.name: daily_budget
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads only (required): dashboard daily budget in dollars. Prefer budget_daily_micros for cross-platform payloads; either field satisfies the ChatGPT Ads required budget.

input_params.name: is_active
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads only: accepted by the upstream create API, but Soku forces new campaigns to non-active by sending status=paused and is_active=false.

input_params.name: campaign_objective
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads only (required): campaign objective. Current Gravity dashboard values are clicks or conversions.

input_params.name: ad_unit_ids
input_params.description: ChatGPT Ads only (required): non-empty ad unit id list. Create ad units first with action:ads/create_ad_unit, then pass the returned ids.

actions.description: Create an ENABLED Google Ads conversion action for attribution; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Register a new Google Ads conversion action for attribution. Created with status=ENABLED because conversion actions are not lifecycle resources.

Use when:
- The user wants to track a new website / call / lead conversion type.
- A workflow needs a conversion_action to feed into bidding strategies.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/create_pixel and action:ads/send_conversion_event.
- The action already exists and only its name / status / category / value settings change. Use action:ads/update_conversion_action.
- The user wants to delete a conversion action. Use action:ads/remove_conversion_action (permanent).

Workflow:
1. Choose type and category from the Google enums below.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_conversion_actions.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_id is the new conversion_action's numeric id.

Side effects:
- Creates a ConversionAction with status=ENABLED.
- Reversal requires action:ads/remove_conversion_action, which is permanent — the removed action can never be re-enabled.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Conversion action display name; must be unique within the account.

input_params.name: type
input_params.description: Google only: one of AD_CALL, CLICK_TO_CALL, UPLOAD_CLICKS, WEBPAGE, WEBSITE_CALL. NOTE: WEBSITE_CALL and AD_CALL additionally require default_value (always_use_default_value is forced true). Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: category
input_params.description: Google only: conversion category. One of DEFAULT, PAGE_VIEW, PURCHASE, SIGNUP, DOWNLOAD, ADD_TO_CART, BEGIN_CHECKOUT, SUBSCRIBE_PAID, PHONE_CALL_LEAD, IMPORTED_LEAD, SUBMIT_LEAD_FORM, BOOK_APPOINTMENT, REQUEST_QUOTE, GET_DIRECTIONS, OUTBOUND_CLICK, CONTACT. DOWNLOAD is only valid with type=GOOGLE_PLAY_DOWNLOAD (not currently a supported type) so do not pair DOWNLOAD with the supported types; prefer DEFAULT/PURCHASE/SIGNUP/PHONE_CALL_LEAD etc. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: default_value
input_params.description: Google only: default monetary value recorded per conversion when always_use_default_value is true. REQUIRED for type=WEBSITE_CALL and type=AD_CALL. Nested into value_settings.default_value. Source: user-provided / derived from the business value of the conversion (e.g. average call value).

input_params.name: default_currency_code
input_params.description: Google only: ISO 4217 currency code for default_value (value_settings.default_currency_code). If omitted, the account currency is used. Source: user-provided / account default currency.

input_params.name: always_use_default_value
input_params.description: Google only: when true, every conversion is recorded with default_value instead of a transaction-specific value (value_settings.always_use_default_value). MUST be true for type=WEBSITE_CALL and type=AD_CALL (handler defaults it to true for those types; passing false errors). Source: user-provided; forced true for call conversion types.

input_params.name: counting_type
input_params.description: Google only: how conversions are counted. ONE_PER_CLICK (typical for leads) or MANY_PER_CLICK (typical for ecommerce/purchases). Defaults to Google's per-type default if omitted. Source: constant enum, chosen by conversion intent (lead vs purchase).

input_params.name: primary_for_goal
input_params.description: Google only: whether this conversion action feeds bidding/goals. Google defaults this to true when omitted. Source: user-provided.

input_params.name: view_through_lookback_window_days
input_params.description: Google only: number of days a view-through conversion can be attributed back to an impression (1-30 typical). Source: user-provided.

actions.description: Review-gated create of an Admin View conversion_group.
actions.long_description: Creates a brand-scoped Admin View conversion_group after human approval. Do not include scope, brand_id, owner_admin_user_id, color, sort_order, or level; those are not agent-facing fields.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Conversion group display name.

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Stable conversion group slug.

input_params.name: metadata
input_params.description: Customer-defined [{key, value, description}] metadata fields.

actions.description: Create a Meta custom audience; review-gated, reversible via Meta UI.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a Meta custom audience of a chosen subtype (CUSTOM, WEBSITE, APP, etc.) for later retargeting.

Use when:
- The user wants to build a custom audience from pixel events, an uploaded list, app activity, or engagement signals.

Do not use when:
- The user wants a Lookalike audience. Use action:ads/create_lookalike_audience.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/create_user_list.

Workflow:
1. Choose subtype and optional rule / pixel_id / customer_file_source.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. List or use the new audience_id via action:ads/list_custom_audiences or in action:ads/create_lookalike_audience as source_audience_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new audience id.

Side effects:
- POSTs to {account_id}/customaudiences. Audience may populate asynchronously.
- Metadata (name/description) is editable via action:ads/update_custom_audience; removable via action:ads/remove_custom_audience (irreversible hard delete).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Audience display name.

input_params.name: subtype
input_params.description: One of CUSTOM, WEBSITE, APP, OFFLINE_CONVERSION, CLAIM, PARTNER, MANAGED, VIDEO, LOOKALIKE, ENGAGEMENT, BAG_OF_ACCOUNTS, STUDY_RULE_AUDIENCE, FOX. Default CUSTOM.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Audience description.

input_params.name: customer_file_source
input_params.description: Meta only: source of the uploaded data for CUSTOM (customer-file) audiences ONLY: USER_PROVIDED_ONLY (default), PARTNER_PROVIDED_ONLY, BOTH_USER_AND_PARTNER_PROVIDED. Ignored/omitted by the handler for rule-based subtypes (WEBSITE/ENGAGEMENT/etc.). Source: constant enum; choose based on how you obtained the user list.

input_params.name: pixel_id
input_params.description: Pixel id for WEBSITE-subtype audiences.

input_params.name: rule
input_params.description: Meta only: flexible-rule JSON for rule-based subtypes (WEBSITE/ENGAGEMENT/VIDEO/APP/OFFLINE_CONVERSION). Shape: {inclusions:{operator,rules:[{event_sources:[{id,type}],retention_seconds,filter:{operator,filters:[{field,operator,value}]}}]}, exclusions:{...}}. Required for these subtypes unless WEBSITE supplies pixel_id; the handler now rejects rule-based audiences with no rule/pixel_id. Source: agent-constructed per Meta flexible-rule schema using pixel/event-source ids from ads.list_pixels.

input_params.name: retention_days
input_params.description: Meta only: days a user stays in the audience. Non-purchase website/rule audiences cap at 180; purchase-event website/app audiences may go up to 730 (raised by Meta 2026-05). Exceeding the applicable cap is rejected by Meta. Source: user/agent decision within the cap above.

input_params.name: is_value_based
input_params.description: Meta only: whether this audience carries a per-user value, so it can later seed a value-based lookalike. Only meaningful for customer-file (CUSTOM) audiences whose uploaded list includes a value column. Source: user/agent decision; set true only when you intend to upload value-weighted user data and later build a value-based lookalike from this audience id.

input_params.name: prefill
input_params.description: Meta only: for rule-based (e.g. WEBSITE) audiences, backfill matching historical pixel/event data captured before the audience was created instead of only collecting from now on. Source: user/agent decision; true to include pre-existing pixel history, false (default) to start fresh.

input_params.name: opt_out_link
input_params.description: Meta only: URL where users can opt out of this customer-file audience (compliance field for CUSTOM audiences). Source: user-provided opt-out/privacy URL.

input_params.name: allowed_domains
input_params.description: Meta only: list of domain strings allowed to use/scope this audience. Source: user-provided list of domains the audience is scoped to.

input_params.name: event_sources
input_params.description: Meta only: list of {id,type} event-source objects (e.g. pixels) used to build a multi-source rule-based audience. type is one of 'pixel','app','offline_event'. Use instead of a single pixel_id when more than one source is needed. Source: ids from ads.list_pixels (type='pixel') or app/offline-event-set ids; agent assembles the list.

input_params.name: event_source_group
input_params.description: Meta only: id of an existing event source group (ESG) backing a rule-based audience, as an alternative to listing event_sources individually. Source: id of an existing event source group from Events Manager.

input_params.name: inclusions
input_params.description: Meta only: simplified inclusion rule object (operator + filters describing who to add) for rule-based audiences, used together with exclusions instead of a single hand-rolled rule. Source: agent-constructed inclusion spec per Meta flexible-rule schema.

input_params.name: exclusions
input_params.description: Meta only: simplified exclusion rule object (who to remove) for rule-based audiences, paired with inclusions. Source: agent-constructed exclusion spec per Meta flexible-rule schema.

input_params.name: origin_audience_id
input_params.description: Meta only: id of an existing custom audience to derive this new audience from (seed). For lookalikes use create_lookalike_audience instead; here it is for deriving a custom audience from another. Source: output id of a prior create_custom_audience or ads.list_custom_audiences rows[].id.

input_params.name: dataset_id
input_params.description: Meta only: id of an offline/CAPI dataset to bind a rule-based audience to. Source: id of an offline event set / CAPI dataset from Events Manager.

actions.description: Create a Google Ads label for organization tagging; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a reusable label that can be attached to Google campaigns or ad groups for organization and filtering.

Use when:
- The user wants a new tag like 'Q2 Promo' or 'Brand Awareness' to organize entities.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/create_ad_label.
- The user wants to apply an existing label. Use action:ads/apply_campaign_label or action:ads/apply_ad_group_label.

Workflow:
1. Choose a unique label name and optional background color (hex).
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Apply via apply_campaign_label / apply_ad_group_label.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_id is the new label's numeric id.

Side effects:
- Creates a Label row on the account.
- Reversible via Google Ads UI; no L1.5 remove handler exposed.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Label display name; must be unique within the account.

input_params.name: background_color
input_params.description: Google only: optional HEX background color for the label, applied to the nested TextLabel. Must match '^#([a-fA-F0-9]{6}|[a-fA-F0-9]{3})$' (the leading '#' is required). May not be visible on manager accounts. Source: user-provided HEX color constant chosen by the agent/user.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Google only: optional short descriptive text for the label (nested TextLabel.description, max 200 chars). This is the colored-label text that pairs with background_color; the top-level 'name' is the label's unique identifier name. Source: user-provided text chosen by the agent/user.

actions.description: Create a Meta lead form attached to a Page; review-gated, reversible via UI.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a Lead Ads form on a Facebook Page with questions, privacy policy URL, optional context card and thank-you page.

Use when:
- The user wants to launch a Lead Ads campaign and needs a leadgen_form_id.

Do not use when:
- The form already exists. Use action:ads/list_lead_forms first.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Choose page_id via action:ads/get_account_pages.
2. Define questions, privacy_policy {url, link_text} (url must be public https), follow_up_action_url, and optional thank_you_page.website_url.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Reference the new form_id in action:ads/create_ad with creative wired to leadgen_form_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new lead_form_id.

Side effects:
- POSTs {page_id}/leadgen_forms. The form is attached to the Page, not the ad account.
- privacy_policy.url, follow_up_action_url, and (if provided) thank_you_page.website_url are validated as public https URLs.
- Meta has no lead-form delete; retire an unwanted form via action:ads/archive_lead_form.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Lead form display name.

input_params.name: page_id
input_params.description: Facebook Page id from action:ads/get_account_pages.

input_params.name: questions
input_params.description: List of question objects. Each entry typically includes {type: EMAIL|FULL_NAME|PHONE|...|CUSTOM, key?, label?}.

input_params.name: privacy_policy
input_params.description: Privacy policy object {url, link_text}. url must be a public https URL; link_text defaults to 'Privacy Policy'. This is the create-time field — the flat privacy_policy_url is a read-only field and is silently ignored by Meta on create (a bare url string is still accepted for back-compat).

input_params.name: context_card
input_params.description: Optional context card object shown before the form.

input_params.name: thank_you_page
input_params.description: Optional thank-you page object. website_url (if set) must be public https. Legacy button_url is accepted by Soku for compatibility but is converted before calling Meta.

input_params.name: locale
input_params.description: Form locale, e.g. en_US.

input_params.name: is_optimized_for_quality
input_params.description: Toggle Meta's higher-intent optimization.

input_params.name: follow_up_action_url
input_params.description: Required public https follow-up URL embedded into the form completion flow. Meta currently rejects create calls without this field.

actions.description: Create a Meta lookalike audience from a source audience; review-gated, populates asynchronously.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Build a similarity-based Lookalike audience from an existing source audience in a chosen country and ratio (e.g. 0.01 = top 1%).

Use when:
- The user wants to scale acquisition by targeting users similar to an existing high-value audience.

Do not use when:
- The source audience does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_custom_audience first.
- The user wants a non-lookalike custom audience. Use action:ads/create_custom_audience.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Identify source_audience_id via action:ads/list_custom_audiences.
2. Pick country (ISO-2) and ratio (float).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Allow Meta time to populate; check via list_custom_audiences.delivery_status.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new audience id.

Side effects:
- POSTs to {account_id}/customaudiences (same endpoint as create_custom_audience).
- Forces subtype=LOOKALIKE and builds lookalike_spec={type:similarity, origin, country, ratio} if not supplied.
- Audience populates asynchronously after creation; no immediate spend.
- Removable via action:ads/remove_custom_audience (irreversible hard delete).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Lookalike audience display name.

input_params.name: source_audience_id
input_params.description: Source custom audience id from action:ads/list_custom_audiences.

input_params.name: country
input_params.description: ISO-2 country code.

input_params.name: ratio
input_params.description: Lookalike ratio (e.g. 0.01 = top 1%, 0.05 = top 5%).

input_params.name: subtype
input_params.description: Forced to LOOKALIKE by the handler.

input_params.name: lookalike_spec
input_params.description: Optional explicit lookalike_spec; otherwise built from country + ratio + origin.

actions.description: Create a Meta Pixel (ads pixel) on an ad account; review-gated, reversible via UI.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Register a new Meta Pixel for browser event tracking on an ad account. Meta restricts each account to a small number of pixels.

Use when:
- The user wants to add a new pixel for conversion tracking on a new website.

Do not use when:
- A pixel already exists. Call action:ads/list_pixels first and reuse the existing pixel_id.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/create_conversion_action.

Workflow:
1. Verify no existing pixel via action:ads/list_pixels.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Install the returned pixel base code on the website.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new pixel_id.

Side effects:
- POSTs to {account_id}/adspixels; Meta enforces a small per-account pixel cap.
- Renameable via action:ads/update_pixel; the Graph API has no pixel delete, so creation cannot be undone via this surface.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: account_id requiredness is enforced only implicitly. Capability docs mark account_id required, but registry.required_common omits it; the executor's auto-injection from resolved credentials silently picks an account when account_id is absent. If the agent omits account_id expecting an error, it will instead create the pixel under whatever account credential resolution selects, with no warning. Low risk because docs say required, but the validation/registry/doc layers disagree on enforcement. Also note: Docs do not state name length/uniqueness rules or that name is the ONLY settable field at creation. An agent might attempt to pass code, domain, or matching settings; these are silently dropped (no error), which could mislead the agent into believing configuration was applied. Capability gap: No first-class way to set pixel configuration at/after creation. Official AdsPixel POST (on an existing {pixel_id}) supports data_use_setting (enum), enable_automatic_matching (bool), automatic_matching_fields (list<enum>), first_party_cookie_status (enum), and server_events_business_ids[/_add/_remove] (list<string>). None of these are exposed by any layer (update_pixel forwards only name, and create_pixel's _EXTRAS_ALLOWLIST is empty). This is expected for a pure creation action but means pixel data-sharing/automatic-matching/CAPI business-linking settings are unreachable through ads_ops. Severity-wise this is a capability gap, not a break of create_pixel.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Pixel display name.

actions.description: Create a complete Google Performance Max campaign (budget + campaign + asset group + assets + optional audience signals) in one atomic mutate, PAUSED; review-gated, reversible via remove_campaign. Google only.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a ready-to-review Performance Max campaign in a single atomic mutate. Unlike create_campaign (which only builds a campaign shell), this wires budget -> campaign -> asset group -> asset group assets -> optional audience signals together so the campaign is structurally complete and force-paused.

Use when:
- The user asks to launch a Google Performance Max campaign and already has uploaded assets (text + image asset ids).

Do not use when:
- Platform is Meta. PMax is Google only.
- The campaign is a standard Search/Display/Shopping/Video campaign. Use action:ads/create_campaign.
- This is a retail PMax tied to a Merchant Center feed (listing groups / shopping_settings). Not supported in phase 1.
- The user wants to modify or copy an existing PMax campaign. Not supported in phase 1.

Phase-1 constraints:
- Assets must already be uploaded; pass them by asset_id. The field_type MUST match the asset's real type (text asset for HEADLINE/DESCRIPTION, image asset for MARKETING_IMAGE/SQUARE_MARKETING_IMAGE/LOGO), otherwise the whole atomic mutate fails and nothing is created.
- Brand assets (BUSINESS_NAME, LOGO) are attached at asset-group level (brand_guidelines_enabled=false path).
- Exactly one asset group per call.
- The account must have conversion tracking configured; PMax optimizes toward conversion goals.

Minimum assets (by field_type): HEADLINE>=3, LONG_HEADLINE>=1, DESCRIPTION>=2, MARKETING_IMAGE>=1, SQUARE_MARKETING_IMAGE>=1, BUSINESS_NAME>=1, LOGO>=1. The handler validates these locally before submitting and raises if unmet.

Bidding:
- bidding_strategy MUST be MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS or MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE. Optionally pass target_cpa_micros (with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS) or target_roas (with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE).

Workflow:
1. Ensure the text and image assets already exist in the Google Ads account and collect their numeric asset ids. (action:ads/upload_image is Meta-only and cannot create Google assets; phase 1 does not upload Google assets — they must be created out-of-band.)
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. After approval, confirm with action:ads/list_campaigns.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_id is the new campaign's numeric id; raw.asset_group_id is the new asset group's numeric id. raw.results holds every mutate operation result (campaignBudgetResult, campaignResult, assetGroupResult, assetGroupAssetResult, assetGroupSignalResult) from the single atomic mutate.

Side effects:
- Creates a CampaignBudget, a PERFORMANCE_MAX Campaign, one AssetGroup, its AssetGroupAssets, and any AssetGroupSignals, all PAUSED, in one atomic mutate (all-or-nothing).
- No immediate spend; both the campaign AND the asset group are created PAUSED. Phase 1 exposes no asset-group enable action, so activating spend currently requires enabling the campaign (update_campaign -> ENABLED) AND enabling the asset group in the Google Ads UI. Do not promise spend starts from update_campaign alone.
- Reversible via action:ads/remove_campaign.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google (PMax is Google only).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Campaign display name; must be unique within the account.

input_params.name: budget_amount_micros
input_params.description: Daily budget in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000).

input_params.name: bidding_strategy
input_params.description: MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS or MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE only. Other strategies are rejected for PMax.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Optional target CPA in micros; only with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Optional target ROAS (e.g. 3.5 = 350%); only with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE.

input_params.name: asset_group
input_params.description: The single asset group: {name, final_urls (>=1), final_mobile_urls?, path1?, path2?, assets: [{asset_id, field_type}] meeting minimum counts, audience_signals?: [{audience_id} | {search_theme}]}. field_type is one of HEADLINE, LONG_HEADLINE, DESCRIPTION, MARKETING_IMAGE, SQUARE_MARKETING_IMAGE, BUSINESS_NAME, LOGO, YOUTUBE_VIDEO, etc.

input_params.name: final_url_suffix
input_params.description: Google only: campaign-level final URL suffix (query params appended to the landing page URL, e.g. for analytics). Set on the PERFORMANCE_MAX campaign. Optional. Source: user-provided tracking string (ValueTrack params allowed, e.g. {lpurl}).

input_params.name: tracking_url_template
input_params.description: Google only: campaign-level tracking URL template (third-party click measurement). Set on the PERFORMANCE_MAX campaign. Optional. Source: user-provided / tracking-vendor-supplied template containing {lpurl}.

input_params.name: url_expansion_opt_out
input_params.description: Google only: when true, opts the PMax campaign OUT of final URL expansion. Final URL expansion is ON by default for PMax; this maps to the v24 asset_automation_settings FINAL_URL_EXPANSION_TEXT_ASSET_AUTOMATION = OPTED_OUT (the removed Campaign.url_expansion_opt_out field). Optional. Source: user/agent decision boolean.

input_params.name: contains_eu_political_advertising
input_params.description: Google only: EU political advertising declaration for the campaign. Defaults to DOES_NOT_CONTAIN_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING. Set to CONTAINS_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING (or 2 / true) only when the campaign actually contains EU political ads. Optional; required to be accurate by Google policy. Source: constant enum (DOES_NOT_CONTAIN_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING | CONTAINS_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING) chosen by user/advertiser truthfulness.

actions.description: Create a Meta product catalog under the account business; review-gated platform write.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a new Meta product catalog for commerce/product ads workflows.

Use when:
- The user needs a new catalog container before creating feeds or product sets.
- The catalog will belong to the business that owns the resolved Meta ad account.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Provide a catalog name and optional vertical/catalog settings.
3. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
4. After approval, call action:ads/list_product_catalogs to confirm the new catalog.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- Creates a product catalog in Meta under the account business.
- Requires catalog_management permission on the Meta credential.
- Does not upload products or create feeds by itself.


Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: vertical enum values are not listed in the capability doc (only 'e.g. commerce'); the agent must guess the legal set {commerce,hotels,flights,destinations,home_listings,vehicles} and the commerce default. Also note: destination_catalog_settings is described only as 'Optional Meta destination catalog settings payload' with no inner-field schema or example, and no mention that it is only valid when vertical=destinations — the agent cannot build a correct object without external knowledge. Capability gap: flight_catalog_settings — needed to create a flights-vertical catalog; absent from capability docs, whitelist, and handler, so the flights vertical cannot be properly configured at creation.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Catalog display name.

input_params.name: vertical
input_params.description: Optional Meta catalog vertical, e.g. commerce.

input_params.name: destination_catalog_settings
input_params.description: Optional Meta destination catalog settings payload.

actions.description: Create a Meta product feed in an owned catalog; review-gated platform write.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Attach a product feed source to an existing Meta product catalog.

Use when:
- The user has an owned catalog_id and wants Meta to ingest products from a feed.
- A catalog exists but needs a scheduled or uploaded product feed.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_product_catalogs or get_product_catalog_details to confirm catalog_id.
2. Provide name plus feed_url or Meta feed scheduling fields.
3. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
4. After approval, call action:ads/get_product_feed_details or get_catalog_diagnostics.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- Creates a feed under the owned product catalog.
- May cause Meta to ingest product data from the provided feed source.
- Requires catalog_management permission on the Meta credential.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Feed display name.

input_params.name: feed_url
input_params.description: Meta only: product feed file URL (http/https/ftp/sftp). The handler nests this into schedule.url and defaults schedule.interval to DAILY, so passing feed_url alone now schedules a real ingestion. To control the cadence, pass schedule instead. Source: user-provided public URL of the feed file (TSV/CSV/XML), not a website/Page URL.

input_params.name: schedule
input_params.description: Meta only: scheduled-fetch config. Subfields: url (string, the feed file URL), interval (enum HOURLY|DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY), hour (0-23 PT), minute (0-59), day_of_week (SUNDAY..SATURDAY for WEEKLY). If both feed_url and schedule are given, schedule.url wins. Source: user-provided URL + chosen cadence; interval from the official enum constant.

input_params.name: update_schedule
input_params.description: Meta only: separate schedule for incremental/update fetches; same shape as schedule (url, interval enum HOURLY|DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY, hour, minute, day_of_week). Source: user-provided URL + cadence.

input_params.name: default_currency
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO-4217 catalog-wide fallback currency applied to feed items that omit a currency. Source: user-provided currency code constant.

input_params.name: override_type
input_params.description: Meta only: marks this feed as a localized supplementary override feed. Enum: COUNTRY, LANGUAGE, LANGUAGE_AND_COUNTRY, LOCAL, VERSION (and others). Requires override_value. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent for localized/supplementary feeds.

input_params.name: override_value
input_params.description: Meta only: the value for override_type (e.g. a country code when override_type=COUNTRY, a locale when override_type=LANGUAGE). Source: user-provided value paired with override_type.

input_params.name: country
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO country code scoping the feed for localized feeds. Source: user-provided country code constant.

input_params.name: file_name
input_params.description: Meta only: file name label for the feed (already accepted by the backend; documenting it). Source: user-provided file name.

input_params.name: delimiter
input_params.description: Meta only: column delimiter for CSV/TSV feeds. Enum: AUTODETECT, BAR, COMMA, SEMICOLON, TAB, TILDE. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: encoding
input_params.description: Meta only: file character encoding. Enum: AUTODETECT, LATIN1, UTF8, UTF16BE, UTF16LE, UTF32BE, UTF32LE. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: quoted_fields_mode
input_params.description: Meta only: how quoted CSV fields are handled. Enum: AUTODETECT, OFF, ON. Source: constant enum.

actions.description: Create a Google Ads text asset (headline / description / long headline); returns the asset_id.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create an inline Google TextAsset via AssetService. Returns asset_id to reference in create_pmax_campaign's asset_group.

Use when:
- A Google PMax / asset-based campaign needs a text asset by id.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta (Meta copy is set inline on the creative).

Workflow:
1. Provide the text string.
2. Call directly (no HITL review).
3. Feed asset_id into the asset_group.

Output semantics:
- resource_id is the numeric asset id; raw.resource_name is the full path.

Side effects:
- Creates an account-level TextAsset; no ad spend.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id (digits only, no dashes).

input_params.name: text
input_params.description: Asset text (e.g. a headline or description).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional asset name.

actions.description: Create a Google CRM-based Customer Match user list destination; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a Customer Match user list with crm_based_user_list.upload_key_type = CONTACT_INFO. Use this as the destination for hashed customer-info uploads.

Use when:
- The user wants a new Customer Match audience for hashed email / phone uploads.
- A workflow needs a user_list_id to target via action:ads/add_audience_targeting.

Do not use when:
- The user wants a Meta custom audience. Use action:ads/create_custom_audience.
- The user wants to delete a user list. User lists cannot be deleted via API.
- The list already exists and only its name / description / membership window changes. Use action:ads/update_user_list.

Workflow:
1. Choose name, optional description, optional membership_lifespan_days.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Upload contacts via Google's Customer Match upload (outside this L1.5 surface).
4. Reference the list via action:ads/add_audience_targeting.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_id is the new user_list's numeric id.

Side effects:
- Creates a CRM-based UserList. No deletion via API.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: User list display name; must be unique within the account.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Google only: optional description. Default "Created by Soku ads-ops". Source: user-provided or default constant.

input_params.name: membership_lifespan_days
input_params.description: Days a contact stays in the list. Valid range: 0-540 inclusive, OR exactly 10000 for no expiration (CRM-based lists). Values 541-9999 are invalid. Default 540. Source: agent/user choice within the allowed range; constant integer.

input_params.name: upload_key_type
input_params.description: Google only: Customer Match key type for the list. One of CONTACT_INFO (default; hashed email/phone/address), CRM_ID (your own user ids), MOBILE_ADVERTISING_ID (device ids IDFA/AAID). Each list holds exactly one type. MOBILE_ADVERTISING_ID additionally requires app_id. Source: agent decision based on what identifiers will be uploaded; constant enum.

input_params.name: app_id
input_params.description: Google only: REQUIRED when upload_key_type=MOBILE_ADVERTISING_ID. The mobile app id that uniquely identifies the app the device ids were collected from. Ignored for other key types. Source: user-provided app store / package id of the advertiser's mobile app.

actions.description: Create a Google Ads YouTube video asset from an existing YouTube video id; returns the asset_id.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Create a Google YouTubeVideoAsset referencing a public YouTube video. Google does NOT host video bytes — the video must already be on YouTube; pass its 11-char youtube_video_id (not a URL). Returns asset_id.

Use when:
- A Google PMax / Video campaign needs a video asset already on YouTube.

Do not use when:
- You have raw video bytes / a non-YouTube URL — upload to YouTube first.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/upload_video (returns video_id).

Workflow:
1. Get the 11-char youtube_video_id of a public YouTube video.
2. Call directly (no HITL review).
3. Feed asset_id into the asset_group / creative.

Output semantics:
- resource_id is the numeric asset id; raw.resource_name is the full path.

Side effects:
- Creates an account-level YouTubeVideoAsset; no ad spend.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id (digits only, no dashes).

input_params.name: youtube_video_id
input_params.description: 11-char id of a public YouTube video (not a URL).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional asset name.

actions.description: Review-gated delete of an Admin View conversion_group.
actions.long_description: Soft-deletes a brand-scoped Admin View conversion_group after human approval. Requires expected_version from list_conversion_groups; stale versions are rejected.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

actions.description: Create many Meta image creatives and paused ads from existing image_hash values; review-gated bulk write.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Deploy a batch of Meta image ads after images have already been uploaded. The handler creates AdCreatives in Graph Batch, then creates PAUSED Ads in Graph Batch using the returned creative ids.

Use when:
- The user has many uploaded Meta image_hash values and wants to create matching creatives + ads in one approved operation.
- The adset/page/link and optional lead_gen_form_id are shared across the batch, with item-level copy/image variations.

Do not use when:
- Images are not uploaded yet. Use upload_images first.
- The user needs videos, carousel creatives, or Google ads. Use the platform-specific primitive actions instead.
- The request previously returned META_BATCH_AMBIGUOUS. Query Meta by client_ref/name before retrying; do not automatically resubmit.

Workflow:
1. Use upload_images to get image_hash values, or reuse known hashes for the same Meta ad account.
2. Build items[] with stable client_ref, image_hash, and name. client_ref is appended to creative/ad names so ambiguous recovery can query by name.
3. For lead-gen, provide lead_gen_form_id and page_id. The handler preflights destination_type=ON_AD, optimization_goal=LEAD_GENERATION, campaign objective OUTCOME_LEADS/LEAD_GENERATION, and ACTIVE form ownership.
4. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
5. Verify via list_ads filtered by adset_id or by the deterministic names.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.items[] preserves input order and includes client_ref, creative_id, ad_id, stage, success, ambiguous, error, and error_code.
- raw.success=false with META_BATCH_AMBIGUOUS means a non-idempotent Graph Batch write may have reached Meta but no reliable response was received. Do not retry without manual/query confirmation.
- Partial item failures leave successful creative/ad ids in raw.items[].

Side effects:
- Creates Meta AdCreatives under the supplied account.
- Creates Meta Ads under the supplied adset with status=PAUSED.
- No immediate spend; activation requires update_ad with status=ACTIVE.
- Non-idempotent creative/ad create batches are not automatically whole-batch retried on request-level unknown outcome.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Meta ad set id that will receive the new paused ads.

input_params.name: page_id
input_params.description: Facebook Page id for object_story_spec.page_id and lead-form ownership preflight.

input_params.name: link
input_params.description: Shared landing URL for image link creatives.

input_params.name: items
input_params.description: Batch items. Each item requires stable client_ref and image_hash, plus name unless top-level name is provided. Items may override message/headline/description/link/caption/call_to_action_type/url_tags.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional shared base name used when an item omits name. The handler appends [client_ref] to creative and ad names.

input_params.name: message
input_params.description: Optional shared primary text; item.message overrides it.

input_params.name: headline
input_params.description: Optional shared headline; item.headline overrides it.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Optional shared link description; item.description overrides it.

input_params.name: caption
input_params.description: Optional shared display caption; item.caption overrides it.

input_params.name: call_to_action_type
input_params.description: Optional shared Meta CTA type. Defaults to SIGN_UP for lead-gen when lead_gen_form_id is supplied.

input_params.name: url_tags
input_params.description: Optional shared Meta url_tags appended to creative links; item.url_tags overrides it.

input_params.name: lead_gen_form_id
input_params.description: Optional Meta Lead Form id. When present the handler validates the adset/campaign/page/form semantics before creating anything.

input_params.name: instagram_user_id
input_params.description: Optional Instagram user id for the creative object_story_spec. Must be the ad-account-connected IG identity from action:ads/get_connected_instagram_accounts — a public IG @handle or the Facebook Page id is rejected by the ads API.

input_params.name: instagram_actor_id
input_params.description: Optional legacy alias for instagram_user_id. Use only when the account setup exposes instagram_actor_id.

actions.description: Read Meta delivery estimate for a targeting spec; returns reach bounds and DAU/MAU.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Estimate reach and delivery for a Meta targeting spec. Use this before action:ads/create_adset to validate the audience size.

Use when:
- The user wants to know the reachable audience for a targeting spec.
- A workflow needs DAU/MAU to compare two targeting variants.

Do not use when:
- The user wants delivered impressions or clicks. Use action:ads/get_insights.
- The platform is Google. Google does not expose an equivalent endpoint here.

Workflow:
1. Build a targeting spec (use action:ads/search_interests and action:ads/search_geo_locations for canonical keys).
2. Call this action with optional optimization_goal (default REACH).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[0].estimate_ready indicates whether Meta produced the estimate.
- users_lower_bound / users_upper_bound is the reach range.
- daily_outcomes_curve plots spend vs reach trade-off points.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: targeting
input_params.description: Meta only: targeting spec object. MUST contain geo_locations (the handler rejects specs without it); also accepts age_min/age_max, genders, interests, behaviors, custom_audiences, etc. Estimate is account-level (no ad set context). Source: build from search_geo_locations (geo_locations keys) and search_interests (interests[].id). geo_locations is effectively mandatory at the account delivery_estimate edge.

input_params.name: optimization_goal
input_params.description: Meta only: delivery optimization goal; default REACH. Non-REACH conversion/app/lead/DPA goals (OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS, VALUE, APP_INSTALLS, LEAD_GENERATION, PAGE_LIKES, CONVERSATIONS, PRODUCT_CATALOG_SALES) also require promoted_object. estimate_ready=false in the response is a valid low-confidence result, not an error. Source: constant enum from Meta OptimizationGoal (see create_adset for the full legal set).

input_params.name: promoted_object
input_params.description: Meta only: object identifying the promoted entity for conversion/app/lead/DPA optimization goals. Required when optimization_goal needs a dependent object (OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS/VALUE -> {pixel_id, custom_event_type}; APP_INSTALLS -> {application_id, object_store_url}; LEAD_GENERATION/PAGE_LIKES/CONVERSATIONS -> {page_id}; Dynamic Product Ads -> {product_catalog_id, optional product_set_id}). Omit for REACH/IMPRESSIONS/LINK_CLICKS etc. Source: pixel_id from the account's tracking pixel; application_id/object_store_url from the promoted app; page_id from the connected Facebook Page; product_catalog_id from the product catalog. Same values used in create_adset promoted_object.

actions.description: Realtime Google Ads GAQL exploration for native fields, segments, resource attributes, or combinations outside cached query coverage.
actions.long_description:
FALLBACK PATH for Google-native ads exploration.

Primary use:
Realtime GAQL search for fields, segments, or dimension combinations that are not exposed by cached query_single_dimension or query_multi_dimension.

Use when:
- list_dimensions does not expose the field or segment the user needs.
- query_multi_dimension returns unsupported_dimension_combination and a GAQL fallback is acceptable.
- You need Google-native resource fields, raw.segments.*, raw.metrics.*, or deep attribute paths such as raw.ad_group_ad.ad.responsive_search_ad.headlines.

First-class Google Ads readback dimensions:
- Use dimensions=['campaign','bidding_strategy'] for campaign bidding strategy reports.
- Use dimensions=['keyword_status'] with a raw.ad_group_criterion.criterion_id filter to read current keyword status after pause/enable actions; this pure entity read does not require date_range.

Do not use when:
- Cached business-layer analytics can answer the question. Use query_single_dimension or query_multi_dimension.
- The user needs CPA, CPP, ROAS, Reg Rate, Pur Rate, or cg.{slug}.* fan-out. Use query_single_dimension.
- The user needs platform-side change history. Use get_change_history.
- The platform is Meta. This action is Google-only.

Workflow:
1. Call list_dimensions first to confirm cached analytics cannot satisfy the query.
2. Call get_resource_metadata if you do not know selectable/filterable/sortable Google fields for the target resource.
3. Call this action with account_id, dimensions[], metrics[], optional resource_name, filters, date_range, order_by, limit, and mode. Set resource_name when the select list mixes the root resource with selectable-with related resources, such as FROM asset_group_asset selecting asset.* fields.

Output semantics:
- Returns translated Google Ads query text in query.
- columns contains normalized field arrays; rows may also be present for convenience.
- Native, Pipedream, and Composio declare the Google Ads v24 RPC before I/O. Managed REST responses are parsed as complete official protobuf responses, so lowerCamel fieldMask paths and row fields become canonical snake_case together.
- Every returned field-mask path is validated against the official GoogleAdsRow descriptor. A response-shape violation returns gaql_response_contract_error (HTTP 424) with provider, api_version, rpc, field_path, and request_id; it is not an OAuth error.
- account.data_freshness is realtime call time, not Parquet sync time.
- No mapping-aware derived metrics or conversion-group fan-out are computed.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform. Omit or use google; Meta is not supported.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google account id from list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Use digits only with no dashes.

input_params.name: dimensions
input_params.description: Dimension fields. Use L1.5 slugs such as date, campaign, bidding_strategy, or keyword_status, or raw.<resource>.<field>[.<subfield>...] fields discovered via get_resource_metadata. keyword_status is a current entity-status readback dimension and does not require date_range when used without metrics or segments.

input_params.name: metrics
input_params.description: Metric fields. Use L1.5 slugs such as clicks or cost, or raw.metrics.X fields discovered via get_resource_metadata.

input_params.name: resource_name
input_params.description: Optional explicit GAQL FROM resource, e.g. ad_group_ad or asset_group_asset. Use when dimensions include selectable-with fields from related resources.

input_params.name: date_range
input_params.description: Two-item [date_start, date_end] array in YYYY-MM-DD format. Required for metric or segment queries; optional for pure metadata-style entity reads. Top-level date_start/date_end are also accepted for compatibility.

input_params.name: filters
input_params.description: Filter expressions over fields marked filterable by get_resource_metadata.

input_params.name: order_by
input_params.description: [{field, direction}] where direction is ASC or DESC; field must be sortable.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Omit to use the service default; hard budget still applies.

input_params.name: mode
input_params.description: rows or summary.

actions.description: Read a Meta ad preview HTML iframe for a creative + ad_format; read-only.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Render a preview iframe for a Meta creative in a chosen placement format. Read-only — does not create or modify any creative.

Use when:
- The user wants to see what an ad will look like on a placement before publishing.
- QA flow needs to confirm creative renders correctly across placements.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to actually create a creative. Use action:ads/create_ad_creative.
- The platform is Google. Google's preview is not exposed here.

Workflow:
1. Have a creative_id ready (from create_ad_creative or list_ads.creative.id).
2. Pick ad_format (see enum below).
3. Call this action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[0].body is the iframe HTML markup; render it as-is for the preview.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: ad_format enum in capability docs is a curated 12-value subset of Meta's much larger AdPreview.AdFormat enum; an agent asked to preview a Reels or Audience Network placement will either guess an unlisted value (and the normalize whitelist will pass it through to Meta, which may accept it) or wrongly conclude it is unsupported. The docs do not state the list is non-exhaustive. Also note: Docs say creative_id comes 'from action:ads/create_ad_creative' but the long_description also points to list_ads.creative.id; the input_params description omits the list_ads/get_ad_creatives sources, narrowing the agent's discovery path. Capability gap: Inline/unsaved creative preview (creative={'object_story_spec': {}}) — cannot preview a creative before persisting it; agent is forced to call create_ad_creative first, defeating the documented 'see before publishing' use case.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: creative_id
input_params.description: Creative id from action:ads/create_ad_creative.

input_params.name: ad_format
input_params.description: One of DESKTOP_FEED_STANDARD, MOBILE_FEED_STANDARD, MOBILE_FEED_BASIC, RIGHT_COLUMN_STANDARD, MOBILE_BANNER, MOBILE_FULLWIDTH, MOBILE_INTERSTITIAL, INSTAGRAM_STANDARD, INSTAGRAM_STORY, FACEBOOK_STORY_MOBILE, MESSENGER_MOBILE_INBOX_MEDIA, MARKETPLACE_MOBILE.

actions.description: Generate ChatGPT Ads draft ad units from a landing page URL.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Ask Gravity to generate 1-5 draft ad units from a site URL and attach them to an existing ChatGPT Ads campaign.

Use when:
- A campaign already exists and the user wants platform-generated draft variants.
- The landing page is public and appropriate for creative generation.

Do not use when:
- The campaign does not exist yet. Create ad units and a campaign first.
- The user needs deterministic approved copy. Use create_ad_unit instead.

Workflow:
1. Resolve campaign_id via create_campaign, get_campaign, or list_campaigns.
2. Submit site_url and count for HITL review. count must be between 1 and 5.
3. Read the campaign back and review generated drafts before activation.

Side effects:
- Creates draft ad units on Gravity and attaches them to the campaign.
- Does not activate the campaign.

Output semantics:
- raw is the Gravity response and may include generated ad-unit records or ids.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: chatgpt_ads only

input_params.name: count
input_params.description: Number of ad units to generate, 1-5. Default 2.

actions.description: Read Meta ad account info; returns account status, currency, timezone, amount_spent, balance.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Snapshot of a Meta ad account's lifetime spend, currency, timezone, and status. Use this to validate account health before mutating.

Use when:
- The user asks for the account's currency, total spend, or balance.
- A workflow needs to confirm account_status before issuing any write.

Do not use when:
- The user wants per-campaign or per-ad metrics. Use action:ads/get_insights.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/list_customers or get_tree_structure.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. If account_status != 1 (ACTIVE), surface an error before any mutation.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: id, name, account_status (1=ACTIVE, 2=DISABLED, ...), currency, timezone_name, amount_spent (cents string, total lifetime), balance (cents string).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Capability docs mark account_id required=True, but the executor auto-fills account_id from the bound/resolved account when it is omitted (executor.py:51-52). The agent is told it must supply account_id even though a single-account context works without it. Minor over-statement of requiredness. Also note: long_description maps account_status integers partially (1=ACTIVE, 2=DISABLED, ) and tells the agent to 'surface an error if account_status != 1' but does not enumerate the other meaningful states (3=UNSETTLED, 7=PENDING_RISK_REVIEW, 8=PENDING_SETTLEMENT, 9=IN_GRACE_PERIOD, 100=PENDING_CLOSURE, 101=CLOSED, 201=ANY_ACTIVE, 202=ANY_CLOSED). An UNSETTLED or IN_GRACE_PERIOD account may still accept reads/writes, so a blanket 'block all writes unless status==1' rule the doc implies is overly strict and could cause the agent to wrongly refuse valid operations. Capability gap: No agent-facing way to request additional AdAccount fields beyond the 7 hard-coded ones (e.g. disable_reason, funding_source_details, business, spend_cap, min_daily_budget, capabilities, is_prepay_account, tos_accepted, age, account_id). For an account-health/validation use case, disable_reason (why a DISABLED account was disabled) and spend_cap are commonly needed but unreachable. This is a coverage limitation, not a break.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

actions.description: Read Meta Pages promotable from this ad account; returns data[] with page id, name, category, tasks.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
List Facebook Pages the current ad account can promote. Use the returned page_id when building creatives or lead forms.

Use when:
- You need a page_id before action:ads/create_ad_creative or create_lead_form.
- The user asks which pages are connected to the ad account.

Do not use when:
- The user wants all pages the user manages. This endpoint returns only promotable pages (subset).
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. Pick a page_id for creative or lead form workflows.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[].tasks lists capabilities (ADVERTISE, ANALYZE, MANAGE, ...).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: The documented output_shape includes "paging": {"cursors": {"after": ""}} but no input param accepts a cursor, so an agent that sees paging.cursors.after has no documented way to request the next page — misleading guidance about pagination availability. Also note: The capability doc does not enumerate the full tasks enum (it lists "ADVERTISE, ANALYZE, MANAGE, " with an ellipsis). Agents deciding whether a page is usable for ads should be told the relevant gate is the presence of "ADVERTISE" in tasks; the complete enum is ANALYZE, ADVERTISE, MODERATE, CREATE_CONTENT, MANAGE, MESSAGING. Capability gap: Pagination (limit/after/before) is not wired: _EXTRAS_ALLOWLIST[("meta","get_account_pages")] is an empty frozenset (normalize.py:157), and the handler hard-codes no limit. For ad accounts with many promotable pages the agent only ever sees Meta's default first page and cannot iterate, even though the documented output_shape promises paging.cursors.after. Compare list_custom_audiences / list_saved_audiences / list_adsets which DO allowlist {limit, after}.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

actions.description: Read one Meta ad by id; returns realtime platform status, effective status, creative, tracking specs, and timestamps.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime single-ad verification after create_ad or bulk_create_ads. Use this when the workflow already has an ad_id and needs current delivery state or creative linkage without paging through list_ads.

Use when:
- A write just returned a Meta ad id and the agent needs to verify status.
- The user asks for one ad's current creative, adset, campaign, or tracking specs.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/gaql_search or list_ads.
- The user needs a page of ads. Use action:ads/list_ads.

Workflow:
1. Obtain account_id from list_ad_accounts and ad_id from create_ad, bulk_create_ads, or list_ads.
2. Call this action directly; no HITL review is required.
3. Use raw.status / raw.effective_status to confirm PAUSED / ACTIVE / IN_PROCESS / DISAPPROVED state.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body with id, name, adset_id, campaign_id, status, effective_status, creative{id,...}, tracking_specs, conversion_specs, created_time, and updated_time.

Side effects:
- None. Read-only Graph API call.

input_params.description: Meta ad id from create_ad, bulk_create_ads, or list_ads.

actions.description: Read one Meta ad creative by id; returns creative content and object_story_spec.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime single-creative inspection by creative_id, including image / video / title / body / object_story_spec. Reads ANY creative in the account by id — it is not scoped to one ad, so it also works for creatives not yet attached to any ad (e.g. fresh output of action:ads/create_ad_creative).

Use when:
- A write just returned a creative_id (create_ad_creative, bulk_create_ad_creatives) and the agent needs to verify the persisted content.
- The user asks about one specific creative and the creative_id is already known from list_ad_creatives or get_ad_creatives.

Do not use when:
- The creative_id is unknown. Use action:ads/list_ad_creatives (account-wide) or action:ads/get_ad_creatives (per ad) first.
- You want the creatives wired to a specific ad. Use action:ads/get_ad_creatives.
- The user wants creative-level metrics. Use action:ads/get_insights with level=ad.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/list_ads (headlines/descriptions on RSAs).

Workflow:
1. Obtain creative_id from create_ad_creative, list_ad_creatives, or get_ad_creatives.
2. Call this action directly; no HITL review is required.
3. Read raw.object_story_spec / raw.asset_feed_spec to confirm the creative content before wiring it into action:ads/create_ad or update_ad.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is a single Graph AdCreative object (not a paged data[] list); object_story_spec and asset_feed_spec are nested creative-content objects.
- The AdCreative node exposes status (ACTIVE|IN_PROCESS|WITH_ISSUES|DELETED), not effective_status.

Side effects:
- None. Read-only Graph API call.

input_params.description: Meta ad creative id from action:ads/create_ad_creative (raw.id), action:ads/list_ad_creatives, or action:ads/get_ad_creatives.

actions.description: Read Meta ad creatives for a given ad; returns data[] with creative content and object_story_spec.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect the creatives wired to a Meta ad, including image / video / title / body / object_story_spec.

Use when:
- The user asks what creative is running on a Meta ad.
- A workflow needs to debug missing fields before action:ads/update_ad.

Do not use when:

- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/list_ads (headlines/descriptions on RSAs).

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ads to get ad_id.
2. Call this action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response; object_story_spec and asset_feed_spec are nested creative-content objects.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_id
input_params.description: Meta ad id from action:ads/list_ads.

actions.description: Read one Meta ad set by id; returns realtime platform status, targeting, budget, bidding, and delivery-detail fields.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime single-ad-set verification after create_adset, bulk_create_adsets, or update_adset. Use this when the workflow already has an adset_id and needs the current platform state or the extended detail fields (attribution_spec, promoted_object, pacing_type, budget_remaining) that list_adsets does not return.

Use when:
- A write just returned a Meta adset_id and the agent needs to verify status.
- The user asks for one ad set's current targeting, budget, bid strategy, promoted object, or learning stage without paging through list_adsets.

Do not use when:
- The adset_id is unknown. Use action:ads/list_adsets first.
- The platform is Google. Google uses ad groups; use action:ads/gaql_search.
- The user needs cached analytics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension with dimension=adgroup.

Workflow:
1. Obtain account_id from list_ad_accounts and adset_id from create_adset, bulk_create_adsets, or list_adsets.
2. Call this action directly; no HITL review is required.
3. Read raw.status, raw.budget_remaining, and raw.learning_stage_info to confirm delivery state before the next write.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API ad set object with id, name, campaign_id, status, daily_budget, lifetime_budget, targeting, bid_amount, bid_strategy, optimization_goal, billing_event, start_time, end_time, created_time, updated_time, attribution_spec, destination_type, promoted_object, pacing_type, budget_remaining, and learning_stage_info.
- daily_budget, lifetime_budget, and budget_remaining are strings in cents; targeting is a nested object.
- Spend caps: an ad set has NO spend_cap field — spend_cap is a campaign/account concept. Its absence here does NOT mean 'no spend cap'; it was never a field on this resource. To confirm a spend cap, read action:ads/get_campaign (returns spend_cap) or action:ads/get_account_info. More generally, a field missing from raw means 'not present on this resource', never a confirmed value; and this action ignores any custom fields param (you cannot widen the returned field set here).

Side effects:
- None. Read-only Graph API call.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Meta ad set id from create_adset, bulk_create_adsets, or list_adsets.

actions.description: Read one reusable Google portfolio bidding strategy.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Read one reusable Google portfolio bidding strategy.

Use when:
- You need a reusable Google Target CPA or Target ROAS portfolio strategy.

Do not use when:
- You need a campaign-specific standard bidding strategy; use action:ads/update_campaign.

Workflow:
1. Read the current strategy or campaign state when changing an existing resource.
2. For write operations, submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify the resulting state with action:ads/get_bidding_strategy.

Output semantics:
- Returns the created, read, updated, listed, or removed Google portfolio strategy result.
