actions.description: Read one Google, Meta, or ChatGPT Ads campaign by id; returns realtime platform state.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime read-back for a known Google, Meta, or ChatGPT Ads campaign id, especially after a create/update approval returns a campaign_id.

Use when:
- You already know a campaign_id and need the current platform state.
- You need to confirm status after a write.
- You need one campaign's budget, bidding targets, targeting, landing page, objective, or timestamps without paging through list_campaigns.

Do not use when:
- The campaign id is unknown. Use action:ads/list_campaigns first.
- You need cached analytics or KPI data. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension.
Workflow:
1. Obtain account_id from list_ad_accounts and campaign_id from a create result, review result, or list_campaigns.
2. Call this action with platform, account_id, and campaign_id.
3. Read raw.campaign for Google, or the platform-native delivery fields for Meta and ChatGPT Ads.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.campaign includes status, budget, flight dates, bidding strategy and targets, network settings, language ids, and geo target ids.
- Meta and ChatGPT Ads raw values are their platform-native campaign objects.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google, meta, or chatgpt_ads

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from create_campaign, bulk_create_campaigns, or list_campaigns.

actions.description: Read Meta product catalog diagnostics for feed and item quality issues.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect live Meta catalog diagnostics without using synced ads metrics.

Use when:
- The user asks why catalog products or feeds are rejected, missing, or unhealthy.
- A product ads workflow needs item/feed issue evidence before making changes.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached business-layer analytics or KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs to mutate catalog resources. Use a create_* catalog action through review-gated batch submission.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Call this action with catalog_id.
3. Use diagnostics to decide whether to fix feeds, products, or account permissions.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph fields without L1.5 normalization.
- For catalog object ids, the backend verifies ownership against the resolved Meta ad account business before returning data.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id.

input_params.name: affected_entity
input_params.description: Meta only: scope diagnostics to one entity type. Common enum values: PRODUCT_ITEM, CHECKOUT, HOME_LISTINGS, VEHICLES. Omit to return all diagnostics. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor from a previous response's paging.cursors.after to fetch the next page of diagnostics. Source: output of a prior get_catalog_diagnostics call (paging.cursors.after).

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Meta only: page size; default 100. Combine with after to page. Source: agent-chosen integer.

actions.description: Live Google/Meta platform-side change history: Google change_event or Meta ad account activities; distinct from Nex audit.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime platform-side change history for Google Ads or Meta Ads. Google returns change_event rows with userEmail and changedFields; Meta returns ad account activities with actor_name, translated_event_type, object_type, and extra_data. Distinct from Nex-recorded ads history.

Use when:
- The user asks who changed what in Google Ads UI/API or Meta Ads Manager/API.
- You need Google clientType (e.g. GOOGLE_ADS_AUTOMATED_RULE vs GOOGLE_ADS_API) or Meta actor_name / translated_event_type to attribute changes.

Do not use when:
- The user wants Nex-side agent action records. Use action:ads/list_operation_records.
- The user wants KPI metrics or reporting breakdowns. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension.

Workflow:
1. Pick platform, account_id, start_date, and end_date.
2. For Google, optionally narrow by resource_type (CAMPAIGN, AD_GROUP, ...).
3. For Meta, optionally narrow returned rows by event_type or object_type.
4. Call this action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.changes[] contains change_event fields; changeDateTime is ISO-8601, resourceChangeOperation is CREATE / UPDATE / REMOVE, and limit is 1000.
- Meta raw.changes[] contains ad account activity rows; event_time, event_type, translated_event_type, object_type, actor_name, event_message, and extra_data come directly from Graph API activities.
- This action returns platform-side history, not Nex-side HITL/audit records.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: event_type post-filter: docs give a single example (ad_account_update) and never state that the value must exactly equal one of the 79 official lowercase AdActivity.EventType values (e.g. update_campaign_name, update_ad_run_status). An agent guessing 'STATUS_CHANGE' or 'budget_update' gets zero rows with no error. Also note: object_type post-filter: docs example 'campaign' but there is NO official enum for object_type (it is a read-only returned field). The exact casing/vocabulary the Graph API returns is undocumented, so the lowercase-exact-match filter may silently drop every row. Capability gap: category (official server-side filter: account|ad|ad_keywords|ad_set|audience|bid|budget|campaign|date|status|targeting) — absent from capability docs, normalize allowlist, and handler. This is the canonical efficient way to narrow activities; its absence forces over-fetch + brittle client-side event_type/object_type filtering.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google or meta.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes. For Meta, act_ prefix is optional.

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Google only: inclusive start date YYYY-MM-DD. Google change_event only retains the past 30 days; start_date older than today-30d is rejected with a clear error. Meta has no such hard window. Source: user-provided / computed date within the last 30 days for Google.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive end date YYYY-MM-DD.

input_params.name: resource_type
input_params.description: Google only: optional change_resource_type filter. Full supported set: AD, AD_GROUP, AD_GROUP_AD, AD_GROUP_ASSET, AD_GROUP_BID_MODIFIER, AD_GROUP_CRITERION, AD_GROUP_FEED, ASSET, ASSET_SET, ASSET_SET_ASSET, CAMPAIGN, CAMPAIGN_ASSET, CAMPAIGN_ASSET_SET, CAMPAIGN_BUDGET, CAMPAIGN_CRITERION, CAMPAIGN_FEED, CUSTOMER_ASSET, FEED, FEED_ITEM (asset/feed values matter for Performance Max accounts). Source: constant enum (ChangeEventResourceType).

input_params.name: event_type
input_params.description: Meta-only optional post-filter on activity event_type.

input_params.name: object_type
input_params.description: Meta-only optional post-filter on activity object_type.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Meta: page size, default 100, max 1000. Google only: max change_event rows to return, 1..10000 (default 1000) -- caps the result set at the official 10,000 ceiling instead of a silent 1000-row cap. Source: user-provided integer.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor from paging.cursors.after of the previous page; pass it back to fetch the next page. Google returns all rows in one call and has no cursor.

actions.description: Read Instagram accounts connected to this Meta ad account; returns data[] with the IG id and username to use as instagram_user_id in creatives.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Resolve the ad-account-connected Instagram identity BEFORE building any creative that runs on Instagram placements. The ads API rejects a public IG handle or Page id as instagram_user_id — it requires the IG identity connected to the ad account, which this action returns.

Use when:
- You are about to call action:ads/create_ad_creative or deploy_image_ads_batch with an Instagram placement and need a valid instagram_user_id.
- A creative or ad create failed because the supplied Instagram id was rejected (e.g. a public @handle or the Page id).

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google.
- You only need Facebook Pages. Use action:ads/get_account_pages.

Workflow:
1. Call this action with platform=meta, account_id.
2. Pick the returned data[].id and pass it as instagram_user_id to create_ad_creative / deploy_image_ads_batch.
3. If data is empty, no IG account is connected to this ad account — connect it in Business Manager first (a public handle will not work).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[].id is the ad-account-connected IG identity; use it directly as instagram_user_id (creatives currently map it to that field). If a creative still rejects it, fall back to the Page's page_backed_instagram_accounts.

Side effects:
- None. Read-only Graph API call.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Meta only: page size for the connected IG accounts list.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor from a prior response's paging.cursors.after; pass it back to fetch the next page.

actions.description: Read installable Google website or call-conversion tag snippets.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Retrieve the installable snippets for an existing Google website or click-to-call conversion.

Use when:
- A developer needs the global site tag and event snippet after conversion creation.

Do not use when:
- The conversion action has not been created; use action:ads/create_conversion_action first.
- The conversion type does not provide website/call tag snippets.

Workflow:
1. Pass the numeric conversion_action_id.
2. Install the returned snippets according to the type and page format.

Output semantics:
- Returns structured global_site_tag and event_snippet values; it does not modify the conversion action.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id without dashes.

input_params.name: conversion_action_id
input_params.description: Google conversion action id.

actions.description: Read per-account window×scope data-readiness so you can plan a segmented audit: audit ready windows now, flag not-ready windows as still syncing (never as zero).
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Before a deep / multi-window audit of a freshly-onboarded account, check which time windows are already synced. Onboarding metrics land in tiers, so recent windows are ready while older ones are still backfilling — an empty result for a not-ready window means 'still syncing', NOT zero spend.

Windows: last_30d, 31_90d, ytd, history. Scopes per window: core (spend/impr/clicks/conv), dimensions (breakdowns), search_terms. Status per window×scope: ready | syncing | failed | stalled | pending. bootstrap_state is the account-level lifecycle (not_started | in_progress | completed | stalled); eta_hint is a coarse 'minutes' / 'hours' / null. live_fallback names a live query path (google -> gaql_search) available while syncing.

Segmented-audit protocol:
1. Call get_data_readiness first for the account(s) under audit.
2. Audit ready window×scope with query_single_dimension / query_multi_dimension as normal.
3. For a not-ready window: on google use gaql_search (live) if live_fallback=google; on meta/tiktok/chatgpt_ads note it is still syncing and revisit after eta_hint. NEVER report a not-ready window as zero spend.
4. State the data boundary honestly in your answer, e.g. 'based on the last 30 days (ready); 31–90 days and full history are still syncing, ~{eta_hint}'.
5. A 409 data_syncing error or a 200 response carrying a top-level 'readiness' object with partial=true means the same thing — apply this protocol.

Notes:
- readiness reflects the LAST PERSISTED bootstrap progress; it is not a live re-read of the ad platform.
- A granted account on a platform with no ads metrics manifest (e.g. GA4) returns readiness=null with reason=no_metrics_manifest. That is NOT a missing or ungranted account — it simply has no window matrix.

Do not use when:
- bootstrap_state is already completed for the account (all windows are ready; just query normally).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Optional. Narrow to one granted account_id (from list_ad_accounts). Omit to return readiness for every brand-granted ads account.

actions.description: Read Meta Pixel/Dataset signal quality fields from Graph API.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect live Pixel/Dataset quality signals such as event stats and match rate.

Use when:
- The user asks whether Meta Pixel/CAPI signal quality looks healthy.
- A conversion tracking workflow needs dataset diagnostics before changing ads.

Do not use when:
- The user needs stable cross-platform KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs Google recommendations or Google change history. Use action:ads/get_recommendations or action:ads/get_change_history.
- The diagnostic candidate has no public Graph API endpoint; such actions are not registered in this PR.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with optional pixel_id or dataset_id for one dataset; omit it to list account pixels.
3. Use raw.event_stats and raw.match_rate_approx to diagnose signal quality.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph diagnostic fields.
- These diagnostics are live platform reads and are not written to synced metrics storage.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: pixel_id
input_params.description: Optional Meta Pixel id to inspect.

input_params.name: dataset_id
input_params.description: Optional Meta Dataset id; treated as the Pixel/Dataset Graph object id.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Account pixel page size; default 100.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor (paging.cursors.after from a prior response) for the account dataset list path. Only used when pixel_id/dataset_id are omitted. Lets the agent page large accounts beyond the first 'limit' datasets. Source: output of a prior get_dataset_quality list call (paging.cursors.after).

actions.output_shape: {"data": [{"id": "...", "name": "...", "event_stats": {...}, "match_rate_approx": "..."}], "paging": {"cursors": {"after": "..."}}}  // Omitting both pixel_id and dataset_id triggers the list-all path (pass 'after' back to page). Supplying an unowned/nonexistent pixel_id returns {success:false, error:"Dataset not found", error_code:"not_found"}; match_rate_approx is approximate and may be null for low-volume or recently-created datasets.

actions.description: Run a predefined, fixed-column Google Ads performance report.
actions.long_description: Use this action for account, campaign, ad, keyword, search-term, or bidding-strategy reports with stable default columns. It is read-only and uses the live Google Ads API. Omit dates for the latest continuous 30-day range.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id without dashes.

input_params.name: report_type
input_params.description: Predefined Google Ads report type.

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Inclusive YYYY-MM-DD; supply with end_date.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive YYYY-MM-DD; supply with start_date.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row limit; defaults to 100.

actions.description: Live Meta Insights for an account/campaign/adset/ad; returns data[] with actions and metrics by breakdown.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Realtime Meta Marketing Insights API. Returns metrics and action breakdowns by level (account, campaign, adset, ad) and optional breakdown dimensions. Distinct from cached business-layer ads analytics.

Use when:
- The user wants up-to-the-minute Meta performance numbers.
- A workflow needs Meta-native breakdowns (age, gender, country, placement, ...).

Do not use when:
- The user is fine with cached, cross-platform business metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension.
- The user needs CPA / ROAS via Nex conversion-mapping groups. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension with cg.* columns.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/gaql_search or action:ads/gaql_search.

Workflow:
1. Identify object_id (ad account, campaign, ad set, or ad id).
2. Pick date_preset or time_range, optional breakdown, optional fields.
3. Call this action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response.
- raw.data[].actions is a list of {action_type, value}, e.g. {"action_type": "link_click", "value": "123"} or {"action_type": "offsite_conversion.fb_pixel_purchase", "value": "45"}.
- raw.data[].action_values mirrors actions with monetary value.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: object_id
input_params.description: Insights target: ad account id (act_...), campaign_id, adset_id, or ad_id.

input_params.name: date_preset
input_params.description: Meta only: relative window. One of today, yesterday, this_week_mon_today, this_week_sun_today, last_week_mon_sun, last_week_sun_sat, last_3d, this_month, last_month, this_quarter, last_quarter, this_year, last_year, last_7d, last_14d, last_28d, last_30d, last_90d, maximum. Mutually exclusive with time_range/time_ranges (if an explicit range is supplied, date_preset is ignored). Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: time_range
input_params.description: Explicit {"since": "YYYY-MM-DD", "until": "YYYY-MM-DD"} object.

input_params.name: level
input_params.description: One of account, campaign, adset, ad.

input_params.name: breakdown
input_params.description: Meta only: DEPRECATED legacy singular form -- Graph never accepted a singular `breakdown` param. Prefer `breakdowns` (list). If supplied alone it is now auto-promoted into `breakdowns` so it still takes effect. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent (prefer breakdowns instead).

input_params.name: breakdowns
input_params.description: List of breakdown dimensions. Values include age, gender, country, region, dma, impression_device, publisher_platform, platform_position, placement, device_platform.

input_params.name: fields
input_params.description: Comma-separated field list. Default 'campaign_id,campaign_name,adset_id,adset_name,ad_id,ad_name,impressions,clicks,spend,actions,action_values'.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 100.

input_params.name: time_increment
input_params.description: Meta only: time-series bucketing. 'all_days' (single total, default), 'monthly', or an integer number of days as a string ('1' daily, '7' weekly). Without it you only get one aggregated total for the window. Hourly is not supported. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent based on the user's requested granularity (daily/weekly/monthly).

input_params.name: action_attribution_windows
input_params.description: Meta only: list of attribution windows controlling how conversions in actions[]/action_values are counted. Valid values (post Jan 2026): '1d_click', '7d_click', '28d_click', '1d_view'. The view windows '7d_view' and '28d_view' were removed and now return empty data. If omitted, the account's default (Ads Manager) attribution is used. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent; defaults to account setting when omitted.

input_params.name: action_breakdowns
input_params.description: Meta only: split the actions[] array by an action dimension. Values include 'action_type', 'action_destination', 'action_carousel_card_id', 'action_carousel_card_name', 'action_video_sound', 'action_video_type'. Default is 'action_type'. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: time_ranges
input_params.description: Meta only: list of {since,until} (YYYY-MM-DD) objects to compare multiple periods in one request. Mutually exclusive with date_preset (date_preset is dropped when this is set). Source: user-provided date ranges or agent-computed comparison windows.

input_params.name: filtering
input_params.description: Meta only: server-side row filter. List of {field, operator, value} objects; operator is one of EQUAL, NOT_EQUAL, GREATER_THAN, GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL, LESS_THAN, LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL, IN_RANGE, NOT_IN_RANGE, CONTAIN, NOT_CONTAIN, IN, NOT_IN. Use to drop rows like spend=0. Source: agent-constructed from the user's threshold criteria.

input_params.name: sort
input_params.description: Meta only: server-side ordering for top-N queries. List of strings of the form '<field>_descending' or '<field>_ascending', e.g. 'spend_descending', 'impressions_descending'. Source: constant chosen by the agent from the requested ranking metric/direction.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor to fetch the next page beyond `limit`. Pass the value from the previous response's raw.paging.cursors.after to continue a multi-page pull. Source: output of a prior get_insights call: raw.paging.cursors.after.

actions.description: Read Meta leads collected by a lead form or ad; returns data[] with field_data. Smart ad-id fallback.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Fetch the actual lead submissions for a lead form or an ad. Handler tries the object_id directly and, if object_id is an ad_id whose direct call fails, falls back to resolving leadgen_form_id from the ad's creative and retrying.

Use when:
- The user wants the leads collected by a lead form or by a specific ad.
- A workflow ingests lead field_data into a CRM.

Do not use when:
- The user wants lead form metadata only. Use action:ads/list_lead_forms.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Pick object_id (either a lead_form_id from list_lead_forms or an ad_id from list_ads).
2. Call this action.
3. Page via limit / after if needed.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response; field_data is a list of {name, values} per question.
- If the original object_id call fails, the handler may retry against the resolved leadgen_form_id; if no form is found, the original error is re-raised.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Capability docs never state the permission prerequisite (leads_retrieval) or that a Page-level token is typically required to read field_data PII; an agent hitting a permission error has no documented remedy. Also note: No mention of the 90-day lead retention window — agents may expect to retrieve historical leads older than 90 days and silently get nothing. Capability gap: filtering parameter (time_created GREATER_THAN <epoch>) for incremental / since-watermark lead retrieval — absent from handler, allowlist, and capability docs; the single biggest functional gap for CRM-ingest workflows that must avoid re-pulling the whole 90-day window each run.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: object_id
input_params.description: Either a lead_form_id (from action:ads/list_lead_forms) or an ad_id (from action:ads/list_ads); handler falls back to resolving the form_id from the ad's creative on direct-call failure.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 100.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Pagination cursor.

actions.description: Read Meta account opportunity_score and recommendations from Graph API.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect Meta-native account opportunity score and recommendation payloads.

Use when:
- The user asks for Meta optimization opportunities similar to Google recommendations.
- A live account audit needs Meta opportunity_score without querying synced metrics.

Do not use when:
- The user needs stable cross-platform KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs Google recommendations or Google change history. Use action:ads/get_recommendations or action:ads/get_change_history.
- The diagnostic candidate has no public Graph API endpoint; such actions are not registered in this PR.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with platform=meta and account_id.
3. Use raw.opportunity_score and raw.recommendations to plan next diagnostic or optimization steps.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph diagnostic fields.
- These diagnostics are live platform reads and are not written to synced metrics storage.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: include_recommendations
input_params.description: Whether to also call the Graph recommendations edge; default true.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Recommendation page size; default 25.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Meta only: optional campaign id to read campaign-level opportunity_score instead of account-level. opportunity_score is officially available at account/campaign/adset level (0-100). Source: output of action:ads/list_campaigns (campaign id).

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Meta only: optional ad set id to read ad-set-level opportunity_score instead of account-level. Takes effect only if campaign_id is not also supplied. Source: output of action:ads/list_ad_sets (ad set id).

input_params.name: object_id
input_params.description: Meta only: generic Graph object id to read opportunity_score from (alternative to campaign_id/adset_id). Lowest precedence among entity selectors. Source: any Meta Graph entity id (account/campaign/adset).

input_params.name: recommendation_fields
input_params.description: Meta only: comma-separated field list for the recommendations edge. Default 'title,message,importance,blame_field'. Previously read by the handler but undocumented. Source: constant enum of AdRecommendation fields.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor for the recommendations edge (paging.cursors.after from a prior response). Source: output of a prior get_opportunity_score call (recommendations.paging.cursors.after).

actions.output_shape: {"id": "act_...", "opportunity_score": 82, "recommendations": {"data": [...]}, "recommendations_error": "...optional, present only when the recommendations edge failed..."}  // opportunity_score is an integer 0-100 (not a 0-1 fraction); may be null/absent when not yet rolled out for the account or when the token lacks ads performance read scope.

actions.description: Read a Google Performance Max campaign, asset-group state, attachments, and signal status.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Read a Performance Max campaign and its persisted asset-group structure without changing delivery.

Use when:
- A create_pmax_campaign result must be verified through a live readback.
- You need the asset attachments or signal approval state for an existing PMax campaign.

Do not use when:
- The campaign is a Search, Display, Shopping, or Video campaign; use action:ads/get_campaign.
- You need to change assets or bidding; submit the relevant write action through HITL.

Workflow:
1. Pass the numeric campaign_id returned by create_pmax_campaign.
2. Inspect campaign status, asset-group final URLs, attachments, and signal records.

Output semantics:
- Returns realtime campaign and asset-group state. Signal output contains resource identity and approval status because Google GAQL does not allow selecting audience or search-theme content.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Performance Max campaign id returned by create_pmax_campaign.

actions.description: Get live details for one owned Meta product catalog.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect catalog metadata and ownership before feed, product, or diagnostic reads.

Use when:
- The user has a catalog_id and needs current catalog metadata.
- A workflow must verify catalog ownership before using catalog object ids.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached business-layer analytics or KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs to mutate catalog resources. Use a create_* catalog action through review-gated batch submission.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Call this action with catalog_id.
3. Use the raw details to decide the next catalog operation.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph fields without L1.5 normalization.
- For catalog object ids, the backend verifies ownership against the resolved Meta ad account business before returning data.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: account_id is declared required in the capability docs but is actually auto-resolved from the credential context when omitted (executor.py:51-52). The docs do not state this, so the agent may over-collect account_id when it is unnecessary. Also note: The "Catalog not found" / not_found envelope is overloaded: it is returned for (a) account has no business, (b) catalog id unreadable/forbidden, AND (c) catalog owned by a different business than account_id. The capability docs ('the backend verifies ownership against the resolved Meta ad account business') hint at this but the agent gets no distinguishing error, so on failure it cannot tell whether to fix catalog_id, fix account_id, or escalate a permissions issue. Capability gap: No agent-facing way to request additional ProductCatalog fields beyond the hardcoded _CATALOG_FIELDS set (e.g. da_display_settings, default_image_url, fallback_image_url, flight_catalog_settings, store_catalog_settings, catalog_store, commerce_merchant_settings). For a read action this is a deliberate simplification, not a break.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id.

actions.description: Get live details for a Meta product feed in an owned catalog.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect product feed metadata, upload state, schedule, and latest ingestion details.

Use when:
- The user has product_feed_id and needs current feed status.
- Catalog diagnostics indicate feed upload or schedule problems.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached business-layer analytics or KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs to mutate catalog resources. Use a create_* catalog action through review-gated batch submission.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Call this action with catalog_id and product_feed_id.
3. Use raw latest_upload/status fields to diagnose feed health.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph fields without L1.5 normalization.
- For catalog object ids, the backend verifies ownership against the resolved Meta ad account business before returning data.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id.

input_params.name: product_feed_id
input_params.description: Meta product feed id.

actions.description: Read rules configured on a Meta product feed.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inspect live product feed transformation rules before changing feed setup.

Use when:
- The user needs to understand how Meta transforms incoming feed fields.
- Catalog diagnostics point to feed rule or mapping issues.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached business-layer analytics or KPI reporting. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The user needs to mutate catalog resources. Use a create_* catalog action through review-gated batch submission.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Get product_feed_id from action:ads/get_product_feed_details or Meta UI.
3. Call this action with catalog_id and product_feed_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope includes success, platform, action, raw, and freshness=realtime.
- raw is the native Meta Graph API response and preserves Graph fields without L1.5 normalization.
- For catalog object ids, the backend verifies ownership against the resolved Meta ad account business before returning data.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Doc workflow (_capability_docs.py:6234) says get product_feed_id from get_product_feed_details or Meta UI, but get_product_feed_details itself requires product_feed_id — circular; only real source is Meta UI. Also note: Doc does not say catalog_id is only an ownership guard (not sent to Meta) nor that a mismatched/cross-account catalog_id yields generic Catalog not found, masking a valid-but-unowned feed. Capability gap: No list_product_feeds action: agent cannot discover a product_feed_id. get_product_feed_details also requires the id as input, so both feed actions dead-end; the feed-rules dimension is effectively unreachable autonomously without Meta-UI input.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id.

input_params.name: product_feed_id
input_params.description: Meta product feed id.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 100.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor from the previous response's paging.cursors.after; pass it back to fetch the next page.

actions.description: Live Google Ads recommendations with impact estimates; returns recommendations[].
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Surface Google Ads automated recommendations (e.g. add-keyword, raise-budget, switch-bidding-strategy) with baseline and potential impact metrics.

Use when:
- The user asks for optimization suggestions.
- A workflow needs prioritized improvement ideas with quantified impact.

Do not use when:
- The user wants account-level optimization score. Use action:ads/gaql_search.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/list_ad_rules and Meta-side suggestions.

Workflow:
1. Optionally filter by recommendation type (validated against Google's enum).
2. Call this action.
3. Apply chosen recommendations via the matching ads mutate (e.g. add_keywords).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.recommendations[].impact.baseMetrics vs potentialMetrics shows the estimated delta in impressions / clicks / costMicros.
- raw.recommendations[].dismissed flags previously-dismissed items.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: include_dismissed boolean coercion: docs say type=boolean default false, but the handler does not parse stringified booleans. An agent emitting include_dismissed="false" (a very common LLM serialization) will INVERT the intent and receive dismissed recommendations. Docs give no warning to pass a JSON-native boolean. Also note: type enum is not enumerated in the docs (only 3 examples given). With 50+ valid RecommendationType values and a hard ValueError on mismatch, the agent must guess valid spellings; docs should point to the enum or list the full set. Capability gap: No way to filter by attached resource (e.g. recommendation.campaign) — agent cannot scope recommendations to a single campaign; Google supports `WHERE recommendation.campaign = 'customers/X/campaigns/Y'` but it is not exposed.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: type
input_params.description: Optional recommendation type filter; validated against the Google Ads RecommendationType enum (e.g. KEYWORD, CAMPAIGN_BUDGET, TARGET_CPA_OPT_IN).

input_params.name: include_dismissed
input_params.description: Include previously dismissed recommendations; default false.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap; default 50, max 1000.

actions.description: Discover selectable, filterable, and sortable Google Ads fields for a GAQL resource before building gaql_search raw.* inputs.
actions.long_description:
FALLBACK PATH companion to gaql_search.

Primary use:
Google Ads field discovery for a single GAQL resource such as campaign, ad_group, ad_group_criterion, or customer.

Use when:
- You plan to call gaql_search and need to know which native Google Ads fields exist.
- You need to validate whether a field is selectable, filterable, or sortable.
- A previous gaql_search failed because a raw field was unknown or not allowed.

Do not use when:
- You need cached business-layer analytics dimensions. Use list_dimensions.
- You need conversion group business semantics. Use list_conversion_groups.
- The platform is Meta. This action is Google-only.

Availability:
- Native, Pipedream, and Composio Google Ads connections declare and validate the v24 GoogleAdsFieldService.search_google_ads_fields RPC before I/O. Managed providers parse the complete response with the official v24 protobuf type and preserve native pagination semantics.

Workflow:
1. Choose the Google Ads GAQL resource name.
2. Call this action to inspect fields.
3. Use returned field names in gaql_search via raw.<resource>.<field>[.<subfield>...], raw.segments.X, or raw.metrics.X; pass resource_name to gaql_search when selecting related resources returned through selectable_with.

Output semantics:
- selectable fields can be requested in gaql_search dimensions or metrics.
- filterable fields can be used in gaql_search filters.
- sortable fields can be used in gaql_search order_by.
- data_type is the Google Ads API field data type.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform. Omit or use google; Meta is not supported.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google account id from list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Use digits only with no dashes.

input_params.name: resource_name
input_params.description: Google Ads resource lowercase name, such as campaign or ad_group. Must match ^[a-z_]+$.

actions.description: Read the Google MCC customer tree under a manager account; returns hierarchical customers[].
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Tree view of customers below a Google Ads MCC manager account. Use this to navigate sub-accounts and their currencies / timezones.

Use when:
- The customer_id supplied is an MCC manager account.
- You need currency, timezone, or manager flag per sub-account.

Do not use when:
- The customer_id is a leaf (non-manager) account. The query returns just that account.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not expose an analogous tree.

Workflow:
1. Call this action with the MCC customer_id.
2. Use the sub-accounts as the account_id for later actions.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.root_customer_id matches the supplied MCC.
- raw.customers[].level indicates depth in the manager hierarchy.
- Filters out status='CANCELED'.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: The capability docs advertise account_ids as 'Optional list of customer IDs to narrow the tree response', but the get_tree_structure handler does not implement any narrowing — the agent will believe it can filter and silently receive the full tree. This is a guidance/contract mismatch that will cause the agent to mis-plan. Also note: Docs do not state that there is no depth limit or pagination, so the agent cannot anticipate large responses on big MCCs. Capability gap: No level/depth filter: the handler always returns the entire descendant hierarchy (no WHERE customer_client.level <= N). The official hierarchy example uses customer_client.level <= 1 for direct children; large MCCs return very large unbounded result sets with no pagination or limit param.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: MCC manager account id from ads.list_ad_accounts. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: account_ids
input_params.description: Optional list of customer IDs to narrow the tree response.

actions.description: List account-level negative keywords from Google shared sets; read-only.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Query account-level negative keywords via GAQL over shared_criterion joined with shared_set (type NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS). Returns keyword text, match type, the shared_criterion resource_name, and the owning shared set id/name.

Use when:
- Auditing account-level negative keyword shared sets, or deduplicating candidates before action:ads/add_account_negative_keywords.
- Collecting shared_criterion resource_name values to feed action:ads/remove_account_negative_keywords.

Do not use when:
- You need campaign-level negatives. Use action:ads/list_campaign_negative_keywords.
- You need ad-group-level negatives. Use action:ads/list_ad_group_negative_keywords.

Workflow:
1. Call with account_id; optionally pass shared_set_id to scope to one shared set.
2. Returns all negative keywords across non-removed NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS shared sets.

Output semantics:
- negative_keywords: list of {criterion_id, text, match_type, resource_name, shared_set_id, shared_set_name}.
- resource_name identifies the shared_criterion and is the exact value action:ads/remove_account_negative_keywords expects.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: shared_set_id
input_params.description: Optional shared set id to scope results to a single negative keyword list. Omit to list all NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS shared sets.

actions.description: Discover brand-granted Google, Meta, TikTok, and ChatGPT Ads accounts; returns account_id values required by later ads queries and ads actions.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Account discovery for the current brand. Use the returned account_id values as the only valid account_id inputs for later ads analytics or ads calls.

Use when:
- The user did not provide an account_id.
- The user asks which ad accounts are available.
- You need to choose a Google, Meta, TikTok, or ChatGPT Ads account before querying synced metrics or running supported ads actions.

Do not use when:
- You already have an account_id from this response in the same workflow.
- You need campaign/ad/keyword metrics. Use list_dimensions plus query_* instead.

Workflow:
1. Call this action, optionally filtering by platform.
2. Pick one row's account_id and platform.
3. Reuse that account_id in query_single_dimension, list_operation_records, or supported platform actions. Use query_multi_dimension, gaql_search, and get_resource_metadata only when the selected platform supports them.

Output semantics:
- rows[].account_id is the external platform account id expected by later calls.
- rows[].data_freshness is the latest completed ads sync run finish time for that account.

Constraints:
- Rows are filtered by ResourceBrandGrant for the sandbox brand.
- An account absent from this response cannot be used in ads or ads calls; the dispatcher returns 403.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform filter. Use google, meta, tiktok, or chatgpt_ads; omit to list all granted accounts.

actions.description: List Meta ad creatives under an account; returns data[] with creative content and object_story_spec.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Account-wide creative inventory. Use this to discover creative_id values for reuse in action:ads/create_ad or update_ad, including creatives not attached to any ad yet.

Use when:
- You need a creative_id but do not know which ad (if any) uses the creative.
- The user asks what creatives exist in a Meta account.
- A workflow wants to reuse an existing creative instead of creating a duplicate via create_ad_creative.

Do not use when:
- You want the creatives wired to one specific ad. Use action:ads/get_ad_creatives with ad_id.
- The user wants creative-level metrics. Use action:ads/get_insights with level=ad.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/list_ads (headlines/descriptions on RSAs).

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with platform=meta and account_id.
3. Page with 'after' from raw.paging.cursors.after until raw.paging.next is absent.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response; object_story_spec and asset_feed_spec are nested creative-content objects.
- Default limit=25; pagination cursor is raw.paging.cursors.after — pass it back as 'after' to fetch the next page.

Side effects:
- None. Read-only Graph API call.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 25.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta pagination cursor; pass it back to fetch the next page. Source: output of a prior list_ad_creatives call: raw.paging.cursors.after.

actions.description: List negative keywords for a Google ad group; read-only.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Query ad-group-level negative keywords via GAQL. Returns keyword text, match type, status, and parent ad group/campaign info.

Use when:
- Deduplicating negative keyword candidates before adding new ones.
- Auditing existing ad-group negatives for coverage gaps.
- Collecting ad_group_criterion resource_name values to feed action:ads/remove_ad_group_negative_keywords.

Do not use when:
- You need campaign-level negatives. Use GAQL query on campaign_criterion.
- You need account-level shared sets. Use GAQL query on shared_criterion.

Workflow:
1. Call with account_id and ad_group_id.
2. Returns all negative keywords for the specified ad group.

Output semantics:
- negative_keywords: list of {criterion_id, text, match_type, status, resource_name, ad_group_id, ad_group_name, campaign_id, campaign_name}.
- resource_name identifies the ad_group_criterion and is the exact value action:ads/remove_ad_group_negative_keywords expects.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Capability-docs output_shape example omits resource_name, but the handler does return resource_name in every negative_keywords item (negatives.py:232) — the example undersells the response. Also note: ad_group_id input_params description ('Ad group id to list negatives for.') does not state the digits-only/no-dashes expectation; account_id description states it but ad_group_id does not. Dashed input is tolerated (validate_numeric_id strips dashes) but agents are not told the format. Capability gap: No pagination/limit/page_token parameter exposed. GoogleAdsService.search auto-paginates internally so all rows are still returned, but a very large ad group could be slow/heavy with no caller-side cap; sibling list_ads accepts a 'limit' extra and this action does not.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id to list negatives for.

actions.description: Read Meta ad labels on an account; returns data[] with id and name.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inventory of Meta ad labels on the account. Use the name (NOT id) when applying labels via action:ads/apply_label.

Use when:
- The user asks which ad labels exist.
- Before action:ads/apply_label to confirm the label name is correct.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/gaql_search on label resources.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. Capture the exact label name (case-sensitive) for apply_label.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Capability docs say limit 'default 100' which matches the handler but diverges from Meta's native edge default of 25; harmless but could confuse anyone reasoning from raw Graph API behavior. Also note: output_shape advertises a paging object, implying the agent can paginate, but there is no after/cursor input wired — the agent could mistakenly attempt to page and have no parameter to do so. Capability gap: fields selector: the handler hardcodes id,name so AdLabel fields created_time, updated_time, and account are permanently unreachable. Auditing 'newest label' or 'who created' use cases cannot be served.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 100.

actions.description: Read Meta automated ad rules; returns data[] with id, name, evaluation_spec, execution_spec, status.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inventory of automated rules on a Meta ad account, including their evaluation filters and execution actions.

Use when:
- The user asks which automated rules are active.
- An audit needs to confirm no rule will fight a planned mutation.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. Inspect rules with status=ENABLED before mutating related objects.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response; status values include ENABLED, PAUSED, DELETED.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Output-contract status enum is wrong: capability doc says status values are 'ENABLED, PAUSED, DELETED' but the official Meta AdRule enum is ENABLED, DISABLED, DELETED, HAS_ISSUES. An agent that filters/branches on status==PAUSED or expects DISABLED/HAS_ISSUES will misjudge rule state. Also note: No mention that results are capped at one page (default 100, no cursor support), so an agent auditing 'all active rules' on a large account may silently believe it has the complete inventory. Capability gap: after / pagination cursor: the handler never reads or forwards a cursor, so results beyond a single page (limit, default 100) are unreachable. For accounts with >100 ad rules the agent cannot enumerate the rest. This is a genuine capability gap (the official edge supports paging.cursors.after).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 100.

actions.description: Read ads from Google/Meta Ads; returns ads[] or data[] with id, name, status, creative fields.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Live ad inventory under an account, campaign, ad group, or ad set. Use this to discover ad_id values for updates, removes, creative inspection, or lead retrieval.

Use when:
- You need an ad_id for action:ads/update_ad, remove_ad, get_ad_creatives, or get_leads.
- The user asks what ads are running.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached analytics by ad. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension with dimension=ad.
- The user needs ad strength or policy approval. Use action:ads/gaql_search.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Optionally narrow by campaign_id, ad_group_id (Google), or adset_id (Meta).
3. Call this action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.ads[] uses snake_case; RSAs include headlines[] and descriptions[]; ad_group_ad_resource_name is required for remove_ad.
- Meta raw.data[] is the Graph response; creative is a nested object with id, name, title, body, image_url, thumbnail_url.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: limit semantics: capability doc says 'Page size; Google default 100' but on Google there is no paging cursor and limit is a one-shot GAQL LIMIT (truncation), not a page size. Calling it 'page size' may mislead the agent into expecting an 'after'-style continuation on Google, which does not exist. Also note: No guidance that list_ads only returns non-REMOVED ads and only fully decodes RSA creatives; an agent auditing 'all ad copy' on a mixed-ad-type ad group could wrongly conclude ads have empty headlines/descriptions. Capability gap: No status_filter parameter: the handler hard-codes status != 'REMOVED' and exposes no way to filter by ENABLED/PAUSED only, or to include REMOVED. Google list_campaigns DOES expose status_filter; list_ads does not. The agent cannot list only paused/enabled ads or audit removed ads without falling back to gaql_search.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google or meta

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Optional campaign filter.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Google only: optional ad group filter.

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Meta only: optional ad set filter; the endpoint is scoped to the most specific filter.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; Google default 100, Meta default 25.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor for the next page. Pass the value of paging.cursors.after returned by the previous list_ads call to fetch the next page. Without it you only ever see the first page. Source: output of a prior list_ads response (paging.cursors.after).

input_params.name: effective_status
input_params.description: Meta only: array of effective statuses to include, e.g. ["ACTIVE","PAUSED","ARCHIVED","DISAPPROVED"]. Needed to surface ARCHIVED/PAUSED ads that are hidden from the default listing. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: filtering
input_params.description: Meta only: general-purpose edge filter, an array of {field, operator, value} objects matching Graph API filtering syntax. Source: constructed by the agent from field/operator/value.

input_params.name: fields
input_params.description: Meta only: comma-separated Graph field selector to override the default ad fields (e.g. to also request creative{object_story_spec,asset_feed_spec} or tracking_specs). Source: constant field list chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: date_preset
input_params.description: Meta only: relative time window for the listing (e.g. last_7d, last_30d). Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: time_range
input_params.description: Meta only: explicit {since, until} (YYYY-MM-DD) window; mutually exclusive with date_preset. Source: user-provided date range.

input_params.name: updated_since
input_params.description: Meta only: Unix timestamp; only return ads updated after this time. Source: user-provided timestamp.

actions.description: Read Meta ad sets; returns data[] with id, name, campaign_id, status, daily_budget, targeting.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Live ad set inventory under a Meta account or campaign. Use this to discover adset_id values before ad, targeting, or budget writes.

Use when:
- You need an adset_id for action:ads/create_ad, update_adset, or remove_adset.
- The user asks what ad sets exist under a Meta campaign.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Google uses ad groups; use action:ads/gaql_search.
- The user needs cached analytics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension with dimension=adgroup.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Optionally call action:ads/list_campaigns to pick a campaign_id filter.
3. Call this action with platform=meta, account_id, optional campaign_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API paged response; daily_budget and lifetime_budget are strings in cents; targeting is a nested object.
- Ad sets have no spend_cap field (spend_cap is campaign/account-level); a missing field is never a confirmed value — read get_campaign or get_account_info to confirm a spend cap.
- Default limit=25; use 'after' for pagination.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action; Google uses GAQL ad_group queries).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Optional Meta campaign filter; if set, the endpoint is scoped to the campaign.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Page size; default 25.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor from a prior response's paging.cursors.after; pass it back to fetch the next page (now actually forwarded by the handler). Source: output of a prior list_adsets call: raw.paging.cursors.after.

input_params.name: effective_status
input_params.description: Meta only: filter ad sets by effective status. Values: ACTIVE, PAUSED, DELETED, CAMPAIGN_PAUSED, ARCHIVED, IN_PROCESS, WITH_ISSUES (per AdSet.EffectiveStatus). Use ["ARCHIVED"] to retrieve archived ad sets hidden from the default listing. Source: constant enum list chosen by the agent based on what the user wants to see.

actions.description: List reusable Google portfolio Target CPA and Target ROAS strategies.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
List reusable Google portfolio Target CPA and Target ROAS strategies.

Use when:
- You need a reusable Google Target CPA or Target ROAS portfolio strategy.

Do not use when:
- You need a campaign-specific standard bidding strategy; use action:ads/update_campaign.

Workflow:
1. Read the current strategy or campaign state when changing an existing resource.
2. For write operations, submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify the resulting state with action:ads/get_bidding_strategy.

Output semantics:
- Returns the created, read, updated, listed, or removed Google portfolio strategy result.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id without dashes.

actions.description: List brand-visible campaigns for manually linking a campaign to a conversion group via set_conversion_group_manual_campaign.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Find candidate campaigns and their internal campaign_id when you need to manually include or exclude a campaign on a conversion group.

Output semantics:
- campaigns[].campaign_id is the INTERNAL id required by set/clear_conversion_group_manual_campaign (not the platform id).
- campaigns[].platform_campaign_id is the platform's campaign id.
- source/included are always manual/true here; auto and excluded states only appear inside a group's resolved campaigns from list_conversion_groups.
- Set include_metadata=true to include campaign-level UTM params when the synced campaign metadata has them.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform filter (google/meta/chatgpt_ads).

input_params.name: search
input_params.description: Optional name/id substring filter.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Max rows (default 100, max 500).

input_params.name: include_metadata
input_params.description: When true, include campaign metadata such as utm_params when available.

actions.description: List negative keywords for a Google campaign; read-only.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Query campaign-level negative keywords via GAQL. Returns keyword text, match type, status, and parent campaign info.

Use when:
- Deduplicating campaign-level negative keyword candidates before adding new ones.
- Auditing existing campaign negatives for search-term waste workflows.
- Collecting campaign_criterion resource_name values to feed action:ads/remove_campaign_negative_keywords.

Do not use when:
- You need ad-group-level negatives. Use action:ads/list_ad_group_negative_keywords.
- You need account-level shared sets. Use GAQL query on shared_criterion.

Workflow:
1. Call with account_id and campaign_id.
2. Returns all negative keywords for the specified campaign.

Output semantics:
- negative_keywords: list of {criterion_id, text, match_type, status, resource_name, campaign_id, campaign_name}.
- resource_name identifies the campaign_criterion and is the exact value action:ads/remove_campaign_negative_keywords expects.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: account_id description says 'digits only without dashes' but the handler/sanitizer actually accepts the dashed form too (it strips dashes). Harmless over-restriction in the doc; the agent will not pass dashed values and that is fine. Also note: Output_shape and long_description omit that 'status' values come from CampaignCriterionStatus (ENABLED/PAUSED) and that REMOVED is filtered out server-side; an agent auditing 'all' negatives might assume removed ones are included. Minor doc polish. Capability gap: No pagination/limit parameter. The handler returns all matching rows from client.search without a LIMIT or page-size control. Sibling read actions (e.g. list_ads) expose a 'limit' extra; this action does not. For campaigns with very large negative-keyword sets the agent cannot page or cap results. Low impact (negative-keyword sets are usually small) but it is a genuine capability gap vs the GAQL LIMIT clause Google supports.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id to list negatives for.

actions.description: Read campaigns from Google, Meta, or ChatGPT Ads; returns platform-native campaign rows.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Live campaign inventory directly from Google Ads or Meta Marketing API. Use this to confirm what exists on the platform before mutating, or to pick a campaign_id for a downstream write.

Use when:
- You need the current status, budget, or platform-side metadata of campaigns.
- You are about to call update_campaign, remove_campaign, list_adsets, or set_geo_targeting and need a campaign_id.
- The user asks what campaigns exist on the platform right now.

Do not use when:
- The user needs cached business-layer analytics or KPIs. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension instead.
- The user needs Nex-side audit of agent actions. Use action:ads/list_operation_records instead.
- The user needs platform-side change history. Use action:ads/get_change_history instead.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ad_accounts if account_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with platform and account_id.
3. Use status_filter (Google or Meta) or objective_filter (Meta) to narrow.
4. Pick a campaign_id for the next mutation.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.campaigns[] uses snake_case with budget_amount_micros (micros).
- Meta raw.data[] is the Graph API body with daily_budget in cents and string IDs; effective_status reflects platform-side delivery state (WITH_ISSUES, IN_REVIEW, etc.) and may differ from status.
- Google excludes REMOVED by default; pass status_filter to include it.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Docs do not state status_filter REMOVED returns only removed campaigns; no single call for all-including-removed Also note: Docs do not state ENABLED PAUSED REMOVED are the only accepted values; validate_status hard-errors otherwise Capability gap: No agent-facing limit or pagination for Google: returns all non-REMOVED campaigns, no SELECT LIMIT, no page token; relies on SDK implicit 10k-row paging with no cursor surfaced
- meta: Guidance: Capability doc for status_filter says only 'Meta: effective_status filter' without enumerating allowed values; official effective_status enum is ACTIVE, PAUSED, ARCHIVED, DELETED, IN_PROCESS, WITH_ISSUES. Agent must guess valid strings. Also note: Doc does not state status_filter accepts only ONE value (single-element array), so an agent may try a comma list or array and get unexpected results. Capability gap: before cursor (reverse pagination) is not supported (handler only reads after); agents cannot page backward.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google, meta, or chatgpt_ads

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: status_filter
input_params.description: Google: campaign.status filter (ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED). Meta: effective_status filter.

input_params.name: objective_filter
input_params.description: Meta only: filter by objective such as OUTCOME_TRAFFIC or OUTCOME_SALES.

input_params.name: filtering
input_params.description: Meta only: raw Graph filtering list, e.g. [{field, operator, value}].

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Meta only: page size; default 25.

input_params.name: after
input_params.description: Meta only: pagination cursor returned in paging.cursors.after.

actions.description: List synced platform conversion actions that can be mapped into conversion groups for the caller's brand-granted ad accounts.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Conversion action candidate discovery for maintaining conversion_group rules. Use this action when you need the platform conversion_action_id values that can be bound to a conversion group.

Use when:
- You are preparing to create or update conversion_group rules.
- You need to know whether a platform conversion action is already mapped.
- You need account-scoped conversion action metadata such as platform, customer_id, category, type, or status.

Do not use when:
- You need business conversion group slugs or derived metric labels. Use list_conversion_groups.
- You need live platform inventory from ads. This action returns synced Nex data limited to the current brand's granted ad accounts.

Output semantics:
- conversion_actions[].account_id is the internal ad account id used by conversion mapping rules.
- conversion_actions[].conversion_action_id is the platform action id used by rules.
- conversion_actions[].data_freshness is the latest completed ads sync run finish time for the action's account.
- mapped_to lists conversion_group slugs that already include the action.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform filter. Use google, meta, or chatgpt_ads; omit to list all synced actions.

actions.description: List platform conversion goals visible to the brand. A goal is the bridge that links a conversion group to the campaigns optimizing for it.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Discover platform conversion goals (Google/Meta) that can be linked to a conversion group via bind_conversion_group_goal. ChatGPT Ads has conversion actions but no conversion-goal catalog, so it returns an empty list here. A goal carries the campaign_ids that optimize for it, so linking a goal is how a group inherits those campaigns.

Output semantics:
- conversion_goals[].id is the internal goal id used by bind/unbind_conversion_group_goal.
- conversion_goals[].conversion_action_ids are platform action ids the goal covers.
- conversion_goals[].campaign_ids are platform campaign ids optimizing for the goal. Known limitation: Google does not expose a campaign->custom goal edge so this may be empty for Google goals; Meta only resolves campaigns bound via custom_conversion_id.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform filter. Use google, meta, or chatgpt_ads. ChatGPT Ads currently has no conversion-goal catalog and returns an empty list.

actions.description: Discover conversion group slugs and derived CPA/ROAS labels needed for cg.{slug}.* columns in query_single_dimension. Scoped to the caller's brand.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Conversion Mapping Group discovery for business metrics such as CPA, CPP, ROAS, Reg Rate, and Pur Rate. Use the returned group slugs to build legal cg.{slug}.* column references for query_single_dimension.

Use when:
- The user asks for CPA, CPP, ROAS, registration rate, purchase rate, or other mapped conversion metrics.
- You plan to request or interpret cg.{slug}.* fan-out columns.
- You need to know the default attribution_window, time_field, count_field, value_field, or display labels for conversion-derived metrics.

Do not use when:
- You only need platform raw metrics such as clicks, impressions, spend_micros, ctr, or conversions.
- You are using query_multi_dimension. Multi-dimensional queries do not support conversion-group fan-out or mapping-aware derived metrics.

Workflow:
1. Call list_dimensions to confirm the dimension supports cg fan-out.
2. Call this action to get legal group slugs, labels, metadata, and version tokens.
3. Call query_single_dimension with cg.{slug}.* columns implied by the selected metrics.

Output semantics:
- groups[].slug is the only legal slug in cg.{slug}.* column references.
- groups[].brand_id echoes the caller's brand; same slug can mean a different configuration in another brand of the same org.
- groups[].version is an opaque precondition token for conversion_group write actions.
- derived_metrics explains the display label and denominator/value semantics.
- metadata is a customer-defined list of key/value/description fields for agent interpretation.
- attribution_window is the group default unless query_single_dimension overrides it.
- Admin-Web fields such as color, sort_order, owner_admin_user_id, and level are intentionally not exposed.
actions.output_shape: {"groups": [{"id": 1, "slug": "qualified-new-user", "name": "Qualified New User", "brand_id": "...", "version": "opaque", "time_field": "click_time", "count_field": "all_conversions", "value_field": "conversions_value", "attribution_window": "7d_click", "derived_metrics": {"cost_per_label": "CPA", "rate_label": "Reg Rate", "rate_denominator": "clicks", "value_label": null, "roas_label": null}, "metadata": [{"key": "business_stage", "value": "activation", "description": "How the agent should interpret this field."}]}]}

actions.description: Deprecated alias for list_conversion_groups. Returns conversion group business fields under rows for short-term compatibility.
actions.long_description:
Deprecated compatibility alias.

Use list_conversion_groups for new workflows. This action exists only so older agents that learned the legacy list_conversions name can still discover conversion group slugs and derived metric labels.

Output semantics:
- rows[] contains conversion_group specs with the same field set as list_conversion_groups.groups[].
- This action does not list platform conversion actions. Use list_conversion_actions for that.

actions.description: Read Google geo target constants for countries; returns countries[] with location_id, name, country_code.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Lookup table for Google geo target constants of type 'Country'. Use this to get location_id values for action:ads/set_geo_targeting.

Use when:
- You need a country location_id before set_geo_targeting.
- The user asks which countries Google supports as targeting.

Do not use when:
- The user needs sub-country regions or cities. Use action:ads/gaql_search on geo_target_constant with target_type='Region' / 'City'.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/search_geo_locations.

Workflow:
1. Call this action to discover location_id and country_code.
2. Pass the chosen IDs to action:ads/set_geo_targeting.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.countries[].status may be ENABLED or REMOVAL_PLANNED.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Capability docs mark account_id required=True, which matches runtime, but the docs do not explain WHY a customer_id is needed for a global constant lookup (any owned Google account works; the choice of account_id does not change the country list). An agent may wrongly assume it must pick a 'relevant' account. Also note: output_shape example shows status:'ENABLED' but long_description says status may be ENABLED or REMOVAL_PLANNED — yet the handler filters out REMOVAL_PLANNED in the WHERE clause, so REMOVAL_PLANNED countries are NEVER returned. The doc 'raw.countries[].status may be ENABLED or REMOVAL_PLANNED' is misleading; in practice only ENABLED rows appear. Capability gap: No way to query sub-country geo levels (Region/City/Postal Code/Metro) through this action — target_type is hardcoded to 'Country'. The agent must fall back to action:ads/gaql_search for other target_type values (the long_description already directs this), so this is a deliberate scope limit, not a defect.

input_params.description: Row cap; default 250, max 1000.

actions.description: Read Meta custom audiences; returns data[] with id, name, subtype, delivery_status.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Inventory of custom audiences on a Meta account. Use this to discover audience_id values for ad set targeting or for lookalike source selection.

Use when:
- The user asks which custom audiences exist.
- A workflow needs source_audience_id for action:ads/create_lookalike_audience.

Do not use when:
- The user wants saved (targeting-spec) audiences. Use action:ads/list_saved_audiences.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Call this action with optional limit / after.
2. Pick an audience_id for downstream targeting or lookalike use.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph paged response; delivery_status indicates populating / ready status.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: delivery_status semantics: long_description says it 'indicates populating / ready status' but does not enumerate the structure ({code, description}) or the common codes (e.g. 200 ready, 300/414 populating/too-small). The output_shape shows code 200 but the agent has no code-to-meaning map, so it must guess when judging readiness. Also note: Pagination loop guidance is implicit: docs say 'optional limit / after' but do not tell the agent how to continue paging (read raw.paging.cursors.after and re-call), so multi-page inventories may be silently truncated at the first 100. Capability gap: business_id read filter (official optional) — not exposed; cannot scope listing to a specific Business Manager. Low impact.
