actions.description: Hard-delete a Meta custom audience; review-gated, IRREVERSIBLE (true Graph DELETE, not an archive).
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Permanently delete a Meta custom (or lookalike) audience that is no longer needed, e.g. stale seed lists or abandoned test audiences.

Use when:
- The user explicitly wants an audience gone for good and it is not targeted by active ad sets.

Do not use when:
- The audience is still used in ad set targeting. Meta rejects the delete with a platform error; update the ad sets' targeting first (action:ads/update_adset).
- The user only wants to rename or re-describe the audience. Use action:ads/update_custom_audience.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Confirm audience_id via action:ads/list_custom_audiences.
2. Check the audience is not referenced by ad set targeting (action:ads/get_adset targeting).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_custom_audiences (audience gone).

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.
- Returns success=false with error_code=not_found when the audience does not exist OR belongs to a different ad account than the resolved one (ownership pre-check).

Side effects:
- Sends DELETE {audience_id} — a TRUE hard delete. Unlike action:ads/remove_campaign / remove_adset / remove_ad, which archive and are reversible, this cannot be undone; uploaded/collected membership data is discarded and the id becomes unusable (including as a lookalike source).
- Fails with a platform error (passed through verbatim) if the audience is still in use by ad sets.
- Ownership is verified against the active account before deletion: the handler reads the audience's account_id and rejects mismatches as not_found, so audiences of other workspaces cannot be deleted.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Used for the ownership pre-check: the audience must belong to this account or the action fails with not_found.

input_params.name: audience_id
input_params.description: Custom audience id to delete, from action:ads/list_custom_audiences or action:ads/create_custom_audience (raw.id).

actions.description: Remove a Google keyword with terminal REMOVED status; review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Terminate a single Google ad group keyword. Terminal mutate; not reversible.

Use when:
- The user wants to permanently drop a keyword.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to temporarily pause. Use action:ads/update_keyword with status=PAUSED.
- The user wants to remove a negative keyword. The negative remove handler is not exposed; use the Google Ads UI.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/gaql_search to confirm criterion_id.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.resource_name is the removed criterion resource path.

Side effects:
- Terminal mutate(remove); sets status=REMOVED.
- Not reversible.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Capability docs do not state the action is non-idempotent: removing an already-REMOVED keyword returns a server error (RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). The agent could retry-loop or mis-handle. Workflow step 1 ('confirm criterion_id via gaql_search') implicitly mitigates only if the agent also filters status!=REMOVED. Also note: Docs say criterion_id comes 'from action:ads/gaql_search' but do not warn that ad_group_id and criterion_id must come from the SAME row (same ad group) or the composite resource_name is invalid. An agent that takes criterion_id from one query and ad_group_id from a different context can build a bad path. Capability gap: validate_response_content_setting / partial_failure: not exposed. mutate is all-or-nothing; no partial_failure flag is surfaced, so a single bad criterion errors the whole call. Acceptable for a single-op remove but worth noting vs. add_keywords batch.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Parent ad group id.

input_params.name: criterion_id
input_params.description: Keyword criterion id from action:ads/gaql_search.

actions.description: Remove a Meta ad label (by NAME) from a campaign/adset/ad/creative; review-gated, reversible (re-apply).
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Detach a Meta ad label from an object (campaign, ad set, ad, or creative). Reverse of action:ads/apply_label. Like apply, Meta resolves the label by NAME (case-sensitive), not by id.

Use when:
- The user wants to untag an object previously labeled via action:ads/apply_label.

Do not use when:
- The label is not attached to the object. The handler verifies attachment first and returns error_code=not_found without writing.
- The platform is Google. No Google unlabel action is exposed here (removal only via the Google Ads UI).

Workflow:
1. Look up the exact label name via action:ads/list_ad_labels.
2. Identify object_id (campaign / adset / ad / creative id).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body of the field write: {success: true}.
- If the label is not attached: {success: false, error_code: 'not_found'}.

Side effects:
- The DELETE {object_id}/adlabels edge is deprecated (Meta 2020-02-03 changelog), so the handler does a read-modify-write: reads the object's adlabels field, drops the named label, and POSTs the remaining set back (a field write replaces the full list).
- Case-sensitive name match against the currently attached labels.
- Reversible via action:ads/apply_label (re-apply the same label).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: object_id
input_params.description: Target object id (campaign / ad set / ad / creative id) to unlabel.

input_params.name: label_name
input_params.description: Label name (case-sensitive) from action:ads/list_ad_labels. Meta resolves by name, not id.

actions.description: Hard-delete an owned Meta product catalog; review-gated, IRREVERSIBLE (true Graph DELETE, not an archive).
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Permanently delete a Meta product catalog that is no longer needed, e.g. an abandoned test catalog.

Use when:
- The user explicitly wants the catalog gone for good and none of its products or product sets are used by live ads.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Confirm catalog_id via action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Check for live usage first (action:ads/list_products, action:ads/list_product_sets, and product ads referencing its product sets); Meta rejects the delete while they are in active use.
3. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
4. After approval, call action:ads/list_product_catalogs to confirm the catalog is gone.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- Sends DELETE {catalog_id} — a TRUE hard delete. Unlike action:ads/remove_campaign / remove_adset / remove_ad, which archive and are reversible, this cannot be undone; the catalog's feeds, products, and product sets are deleted with it.
- Fails with a platform error (passed through verbatim) when products or product sets in the catalog are in active use (e.g. referenced by live product ads). Meta's allow_delete_catalog_with_live_product_set override is intentionally NOT exposed on this surface — stop the referencing ads first instead of force-deleting.
- Catalog ownership is verified against the resolved account business before deletion; mismatches return error_code=not_found, so catalogs of other workspaces cannot be deleted.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id to delete.

actions.description: Hard-delete a Meta product feed from an owned catalog; review-gated, IRREVERSIBLE (true Graph DELETE, not an archive).
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Permanently delete a product feed whose ingestion source is no longer wanted, e.g. a superseded or misconfigured feed.

Use when:
- The user explicitly wants the feed gone for good and understands its ingested products leave the catalog with it.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Confirm product_feed_id via action:ads/get_product_feed_details.
3. Check which products came from this feed (action:ads/list_products) — they are removed from the catalog with it, which can empty product sets used by live product ads.
4. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
5. After approval, call action:ads/get_catalog_diagnostics or action:ads/list_products to confirm.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- Sends DELETE {product_feed_id} — a TRUE hard delete. Unlike action:ads/remove_campaign / remove_adset / remove_ad, which archive and are reversible, this cannot be undone.
- Products ingested by this feed are removed from the catalog, which can empty product sets and affect product ads that depend on them; Meta rejects the delete with a platform error (passed through verbatim) when the feed's products are in active use.
- Catalog ownership AND the feed's membership in that catalog are verified before deletion; mismatches return error_code=not_found, so feeds of other workspaces cannot be deleted.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id that contains the feed.

input_params.name: product_feed_id
input_params.description: Meta product feed id to delete.

actions.description: Replace the full ChatGPT Ads ad-unit set on a campaign.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Replace the complete ChatGPT Ads ad-unit set attached to a campaign.

Use when:
- The user approved an exact final list of campaign ad units.
- Old campaign ad units should be detached from the campaign.

Do not use when:
- The user only wants to add variants. Use add_campaign_ad_units.
- The provided ad_unit_ids list is empty.

Workflow:
1. Resolve campaign_id and the exact non-empty ad_unit_ids list.
2. Submit the replace operation for HITL review.
3. Read the campaign back to confirm the final binding.

Side effects:
- Replaces the campaign's full ad-unit binding set.
- Does not archive detached ad units and does not activate the campaign.

Output semantics:
- raw is the Gravity response for the replacement mutation.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: chatgpt_ads only

actions.description: Resolve ads words like ROAS, CPA, conversion group, search term, asset group, or change history to the right ads action and payload hints.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Semantic routing for ambiguous ads terms before choosing a data action. This is a lightweight resolver: it maps user-facing KPI, dimension, conversion group, change-history, and Google-native vocabulary to action ids and payload hints. It does not query metrics or platform APIs.

Use when:
- The user names a metric or dimension and you are unsure whether it is cached, conversion-group derived, or Google-native.
- The user asks for ROAS, CPA, CPP, registration rate, purchase rate, or cg.* columns and you need the correct conversion group workflow.
- The user asks for change history and you need to choose platform-side get_change_history vs Nex-side list_operation_records.
- The user mentions asset group, PMax asset, ad strength, geo target constants, or another Google-native field that may require get_resource_metadata + gaql_search.

Do not use when:
- You already know the exact action and parameters. Call that action directly.
- You need the full available schema. Use list_dimensions or list_conversion_groups.
- You need live Google Ads field metadata. Use get_resource_metadata.

Workflow:
1. Pass a short list of user terms in terms[].
2. Read rows[].matches[0].recommended_action as the first route.
3. Follow followup_actions and parameter_hints to fill the next action payload.

Output semantics:
- rows[].recommended_action is the best first action for that term.
- matches[].kind classifies dimension, metric, conversion_group_metric, platform_change_history, nex_audit, gaql_native_hint, or unknown.
- parameter_hints are routing hints, not an executable schema.

input_params.name: terms
input_params.description: One term or a list of terms to resolve. Examples: ROAS, CPA, search term, asset group, change history.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform context: google, meta, or tiktok. Defaults to google.

actions.description: Search Meta ad geo location keys; returns data[] with location key, name, type.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Look up Meta geo location keys for targeting.geo_locations. Use the returned 'key' values inside ad set targeting.

Use when:
- The user provides a country / region / city name and needs Meta's canonical key.
- A workflow builds targeting from human-readable place names.

Do not use when:
- The user wants Google geo target constants. Use action:ads/list_countries or action:ads/gaql_search on geo_target_constant.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Call this action with the user-supplied query and optional location_types.
2. Embed the chosen keys into targeting.geo_locations for action:ads/create_adset.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[].key is the targeting key (e.g. 'US', '2421422'); raw.data[].type is the location type (country, region, city, ...).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: The official q caveat — q must contain ONLY the locality name (no state/region/country text) and disambiguation must come from country_code — is NOT stated in the capability long_description. Example in docs is 'San Francisco' (fine) but nothing warns against 'San Francisco, CA' or 'Paris, Texas', so the agent may craft poor queries. Also note: No way to disambiguate same-named places: docs say 'multiple ad geo locations with same name/type can be returned' but provide no field/param (region_id) for the agent to narrow down — the agent must guess which returned key is correct. Capability gap: region_id: official optional param to disambiguate same-named cities (e.g. multiple 'Springfield'); absent from handler, whitelist, and capability docs — the agent cannot resolve ambiguous localities precisely.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: query
input_params.description: Free-text place name.

input_params.name: location_types
input_params.description: Optional list of location types to include. One or more of country, region, city, zip, geo_market, electoral_district, neighborhood, country_group, subneighborhood.

input_params.name: country_code
input_params.description: ISO-2 country code to constrain the search.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap; default 25.

actions.description: Search Meta ad interest taxonomy; returns data[] with interest_id, name, audience size bounds.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Look up Meta ad interest IDs and audience-size bounds by free-text query. Use the returned interest_id values inside targeting.interests on ad sets.

Use when:
- The user provides interest keywords and needs Meta's canonical interest IDs.
- A workflow builds a targeting spec from human-readable interest names.

Do not use when:
- The user provides locations. Use action:ads/search_geo_locations.
- The platform is Google. Google audience interests are not exposed here.

Workflow:
1. Call this action with the user-supplied query.
2. Pick interests with sufficient audience_size_lower_bound.
3. Embed the interest IDs into targeting.interests for action:ads/create_adset.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.data[] contains id, name, audience_size_lower_bound, audience_size_upper_bound, topic, path (category breadcrumbs).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta only: ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Used ONLY to resolve the access token; the interest search itself is not scoped to the account. Source: output of ads/list_ad_accounts (rows[].account_id).

input_params.name: query
input_params.description: Free-text interest keyword.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap; default 25.

input_params.name: locale
input_params.description: Meta only: locale code (e.g. en_US, ja_JP) to localize the returned interest taxonomy names/IDs. Omit for default locale. Source: constant enum of Meta locale codes (retrievable via Targeting Search type=adlocale); typically derived from the advertiser's market/language.

actions.description: Send a Meta Conversions API server-side event; review-gated and requires SHA-256 hashed PII fields.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
POST a server-to-server conversion event to Meta's Conversions API. Event is attributed immediately by Meta's attribution windows and cannot be retracted.

Use when:
- The user wants to send a server-side event (purchase, lead, signup) for attribution.
- A workflow needs to backfill or dedupe events with browser-side Pixel via event_id.

Do not use when:
- The user has not hashed PII fields. Email, phone, name, gender, DOB, city, state, ZIP, country, external_id MUST be SHA-256 lowercase hex (64 chars). The handler rejects unhashed values BEFORE calling Meta.
- The platform is Google. Use the Google Ads conversion upload outside this surface.

Workflow:
1. Pick pixel_id via action:ads/list_pixels.
2. Hash all PII fields in user_data per Meta's spec.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify delivery via raw.events_received and fbtrace_id.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.events_received is the count Meta accepted.
- raw.messages may carry warnings or validation hints.
- raw.fbtrace_id is the trace id for debugging.
- Returns success=false with 'user_data.<field> must be SHA-256 hashed...' before calling Meta if any PII field is unhashed.

Side effects:
- POSTs {pixel_id}/events with data=[<event_obj>].
- Event is attributed immediately by Meta's attribution windows.
- NOT REVERSIBLE — there is no API to retract an event once sent.
- No ad spend triggered, but reported metrics will reflect the event.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: pixel_id
input_params.description: Meta pixel id from action:ads/list_pixels.

input_params.name: event_name
input_params.description: Meta standard event name (Purchase, AddToCart, ViewContent, Lead, CompleteRegistration) or a custom string.

input_params.name: event_time
input_params.description: Unix seconds; defaults to current time if omitted by handler.

input_params.name: action_source
input_params.description: Meta CAPI event source. One of website, email, app, phone_call, chat, physical_store, system_generated, business_messaging, other.

input_params.name: user_data
input_params.description: Meta only: user identity payload. MUST contain at least one identifier so Meta can match the event (em, ph, external_id, or client_ip_address + client_user_agent). PII fields (em, ph, fn, ln, ge, db, ct, st, zp, country, external_id) MUST be SHA-256 lowercase hex (64 chars); the handler rejects unhashed values. Non-PII fields (client_ip_address, client_user_agent, fbc, fbp, subscription_id) pass through unchanged. For action_source='website', client_user_agent is REQUIRED here. Source: user-provided / collected at the conversion point; PII pre-hashed by the caller per Meta's spec; client_user_agent and client_ip_address captured from the originating browser request.

input_params.name: custom_data
input_params.description: Meta only: custom event payload (value, currency, content_ids, content_type, order_id, etc.). For Purchase events, value AND currency are required by Meta. Source: user-provided / derived from the order or conversion record.

input_params.name: event_source_url
input_params.description: Meta only: URL of the page where the event occurred. REQUIRED when action_source='website'. Validated as a public https URL. Source: user-provided page URL where the conversion happened.

input_params.name: event_id
input_params.description: Deduplication key paired with Pixel browser-side events.

input_params.name: app_data
input_params.description: Meta only: app-event context. REQUIRED when action_source='app'. Contains advertiser_tracking_enabled (0/1), application_tracking_enabled (0/1) and extinfo (ordered array of device/app fields per Meta's extinfo spec). Source: captured from the mobile SDK / app at event time.

input_params.name: opt_out
input_params.description: Meta only: per-event flag; when true the event is used for attribution but excluded from ads optimization. Source: user-provided privacy preference.

input_params.name: data_processing_options
input_params.description: Meta only: Limited Data Use / privacy controls. Pass ["LDU"] to enable Limited Data Use, or [] to disable. Source: constant enum chosen for US-privacy compliance.

input_params.name: data_processing_options_country
input_params.description: Meta only: country for Limited Data Use (0 = let Meta geolocate, 1 = United States). Used with data_processing_options. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: data_processing_options_state
input_params.description: Meta only: state for Limited Data Use (0 = let Meta geolocate, 1000 = California). Used with data_processing_options. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: attribution_data
input_params.description: Meta only: optional CAPI attribution optimization payload (e.g. attribution_share) forwarded verbatim. Source: user-provided / advanced attribution config per Meta docs.

input_params.name: original_event_data
input_params.description: Meta only: original event reference (event_name, event_time) used to link a derived event back to its source event for optimization. Source: derived from the originating event record.

actions.description: Replace the Google campaign ad schedule criteria (remove-all then create-new); review-gated, non-atomic at criterion level.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Replace all AD_SCHEDULE criteria on a Google campaign with a new list of day-of-week + hour windows.

Use when:
- The user wants to restrict ad serving to specific hours or days.
- The user wants to remove all existing schedules and start over.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to add a single schedule without removing the others. There is no append handler; replace is the only path.
- The platform is Meta. Use the Meta ad set start_time / end_time fields.

Workflow:
1. Capture the current schedule via action:ads/gaql_search if rollback may be needed.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_criterion where type='AD_SCHEDULE'.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count of schedules applied.

Side effects:
- Removes ALL existing AD_SCHEDULE criteria on the campaign, then creates new ones, all in one mutate.
- Non-atomic at the criterion level: if a create fails after removes succeed, the campaign may have zero schedules. Caller must re-run after fixing the input.
- Reversible: re-call with the previously captured schedule list.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Capability docs say start_minute/end_minute default to ZERO and hours have ranges, but do NOT warn that the official API treats all five sub-fields as required-at-creation; an agent may assume omitting start_hour leaves the window 'open' rather than defaulting to 00:00-24:00. The silent defaulting (start_hour=0, end_hour=24) can produce an unintended all-day schedule. Also note: No guidance that end must be after start and that end is exclusive of end_minute. The agent could build a zero-length or inverted window (e.g. start_hour=17,end_hour=9) and only discover the failure at the API, with no local hint. Capability gap: bid_modifier per AdSchedule criterion: AdSchedule CampaignCriterion supports an optional bid_modifier (dayparting bid adjustment) to raise/lower bids during a window. This is a core dayparting use case and is absent from all three layers (handler hard-creates criteria with no bid_modifier, no whitelist key, no capability-docs param). The agent cannot do bid dayparting via this action.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: schedules
input_params.description: List (or JSON string) of {day_of_week, start_hour, start_minute, end_hour, end_minute}. day_of_week: MONDAY..SUNDAY. start_minute / end_minute: ZERO, FIFTEEN, THIRTY, FORTY_FIVE (default ZERO). start_hour: 0-23. end_hour: 0-24.

actions.description: Set a Google campaign device bid modifier (replaces existing DEVICE criteria); review-gated.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Replace all DEVICE-type bid modifiers on a Google campaign with a single new modifier for one device.

Use when:
- The user wants to raise or lower bids for mobile / desktop / tablet / connected TV.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to add modifiers for multiple devices independently. There is no append handler; calling this action again replaces the prior modifier.
- The platform is Meta. Meta does not expose this concept; use ad set targeting.

Workflow:
1. Choose the device and modifier_value (1.0 = no change).
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_criterion where type='DEVICE'.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message echoes the device and modifier value.

Side effects:
- Removes ALL existing DEVICE criteria on the campaign, then creates one new bid modifier, all in one mutate.
- Non-atomic at criterion level: same partial-failure risk as set_ad_schedule.
- Reversible: re-call with the prior device + modifier_value.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: device
input_params.description: Device to adjust: one of MOBILE, DESKTOP, TABLET, CONNECTED_TV, OTHER. CONNECTED_TV is only valid on Video/Demand-Gen campaigns and will be rejected by Google on Search/Display campaigns. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: modifier_value
input_params.description: Bid multiplier. 1.0 = no change; valid range 0.1-10.0 (handler now rejects out-of-range clean). 0 is NOT accepted (use a separate opt-out path); e.g. 1.2 = +20%, 0.8 = -20%. Source: user/agent-chosen multiplier.

actions.description: Review-gated manual include/exclude of a campaign on a conversion group.
actions.long_description: Manually includes or excludes a campaign on an Admin View conversion group after human approval. include adds a campaign goals did not auto-resolve; exclude removes an auto campaign. Requires expected_version and the INTERNAL campaign_id from list_brand_campaigns.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Internal campaign id from list_brand_campaigns.

input_params.name: mode
input_params.description: include or exclude.

actions.description: Set the Google campaign final URL suffix (UTM tracking template); review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Set or replace the final_url_suffix on a Google campaign for tracking-template parameter injection.

Use when:
- The user wants to install UTM tracking parameters on every ad URL.
- A workflow needs to standardize tracking across all ads in the campaign.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Meta uses ad-level URL tags via tracking_specs.
- The user wants per-ad URL overrides. Use action:ads/update_ad with final_urls.

Workflow:
1. Build the suffix string without a leading '?' or '&'.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_campaigns or the Google Ads UI.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message echoes the applied suffix string.

Side effects:
- Campaign update with field-mask ['final_url_suffix'].
- Reversible: re-call with the prior value (or empty string to clear).

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: Empty-string-to-clear contradiction: long_description says 'Reversible: re-call with the prior value (or empty string to clear)', but suffix is declared required=True in both capability docs and registry. The agent may believe it cannot clear. In practice passing suffix="" works (key present -> required-check passes; .strip() -> "" -> Google clears the field), but this is implicit and the agent must guess that '' is acceptable. Also note: No campaign-type guardrail or warning: docs do not tell the agent that Video campaigns reject this mutation, so the agent may attempt it and surface a raw Google error. Add a 'do not use on Video campaigns' note. Capability gap: No missing required Google Ads API parameters for THIS specific field update - final_url_suffix is a single optional string and the action correctly sets only it. (Related but out-of-scope: tracking_url_template and custom_parameters / url_custom_parameters are sibling URL-options fields not covered by this action; an agent wanting full tracking-template control would need a separate capability, but that is a scope decision, not a defect of set_final_url_suffix.)

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: suffix
input_params.description: Query-string fragment without a leading '?' or '&'. Supports Google value-track parameters like {_campaign}.

actions.description: Replace Google campaign location criteria (remove-all + create-new); review-gated, non-atomic at criterion level.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Replace all LOCATION criteria on a Google campaign with a new list of geo target constants. Use the IDs from action:ads/list_countries or Google's geo target constants table.

Use when:
- The user wants to limit the campaign to specific countries, regions, or cities.
- The user wants to clear and reset all geographic targeting.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to add one location without removing the others. There is no append handler; replace is the only path.
- The platform is Meta. Use targeting.geo_locations in action:ads/create_adset or action:ads/update_adset.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_countries to look up country location_id values.
2. Capture current criteria via gaql_search if rollback may be needed.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_criterion where type='LOCATION'.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count of locations applied.

Side effects:
- Removes ALL existing LOCATION criteria, then creates new criteria referencing geo_target_constant_path(location_id), all in one mutate.
- Non-atomic at criterion level: partial-failure risk.
- Reversible: re-call with the prior location_id list.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: location_ids
input_params.description: Google only: list of geo target constant IDs to REPLACE the campaign's location targeting (removes all existing LOCATION criteria first). Each item is either a bare ID (positive include) or an object {"id": <geo_target_constant_id>, "negative": true|false, "bid_modifier": <0.1-10.0>}. negative=true EXCLUDES the location; bid_modifier raises/lowers the bid for that location (1.5 = +50%). A negative location cannot have a bid_modifier (API constraint, returns an error). Source: geo target constant IDs from action:ads/list_countries (countries) or Google's geo target constants table for regions/cities/postal codes.

input_params.name: location_type
input_params.description: Google only: DEPRECATED alias of geo_target_type. Sets the campaign-level positive geo target type. Valid: PRESENCE (people physically in the locations) or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST (also people interested in them). AREA_OF_INTEREST/INTEREST map to PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST; SEARCH_INTEREST is deprecated and rejected. Prefer geo_target_type. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent based on user intent.

input_params.name: geo_target_type
input_params.description: Google only: campaign-level positive geo target type applied via Campaign.geo_target_type_setting in the same mutate. Valid: PRESENCE (people physically in the targeted locations) or PRESENCE_OR_INTEREST (also people who searched for / showed interest in them). SEARCH_INTEREST is deprecated and not settable. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent based on user intent (physical presence vs presence-or-interest).

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Google only: maximum number of locations allowed in location_ids. If the list exceeds this, the action returns an error (it does NOT silently truncate). Source: user-provided cap.

actions.description: Replace Google campaign language criteria (remove-all + create-new); review-gated, non-atomic at criterion level.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Replace all LANGUAGE criteria on a Google campaign with a new list of language constants. Use the IDs from action:ads/list_languages.

Use when:
- The user wants to restrict the campaign to specific Google Ads languages.
- The user wants to clear and reset language targeting.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to append one language. There is no append handler; replace is the only path.
- The platform is Meta. Use targeting locales inside the Meta ad set targeting spec.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_languages to look up language_id values.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via gaql_search on campaign_criterion where type='LANGUAGE'.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message reports the count of languages applied.

Side effects:
- Removes ALL existing LANGUAGE criteria, then creates new criteria referencing language_constant_path(language_id), all in one mutate.
- Non-atomic at criterion level.
- Reversible: re-call with the prior language_id list.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: The 'limit' param is documented as 'Optional cap on the number of languages applied' but the handler ignores it entirely. An agent that passes limit=1 expecting only the first language to be applied will instead get ALL languages applied — silent contract violation. Either implement the cap (language_ids[:limit]) or remove limit from docs+allowlist. Also note: Docs example uses integer language IDs (example=[1000,1003]) while description says 'List of Google language constant IDs (or a JSON string)'. _parse_json_array + validate_numeric_id coerce both ints and strings, so this works, but the mixed int/string guidance is mildly ambiguous. Capability gap: No way to set CampaignCriterion.negative — language criteria are positive-only here, which is the normal case, but the API does support negative; not exposed (acceptable, low value).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns.

input_params.name: language_ids
input_params.description: List of Google language constant IDs (or a JSON string). Use action:ads/list_languages to look them up.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Optional cap on the number of languages applied.

actions.description: Toggle Google campaign search-partners or display-network distribution; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Enable or disable search partners and display network distribution on a Google Search campaign.

Use when:
- The user wants to enable / disable Google Search Partners.
- The user wants to enable / disable Display Network for a Search campaign.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Meta uses publisher_platforms inside the ad set targeting spec.
- The user wants to toggle YouTube or other channels. This action handles only the four Google campaign network_settings booleans.

Workflow:
1. Decide which network booleans to flip.
   Prefer the native target_* parameters. search_partners maps to target_search_network and display_network maps to target_content_network.
   Enabling target_search_network may require target_google_search=true in the same update.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_campaigns and/or gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message lists the field-mask paths that were updated.

Side effects:
- Campaign update with field-mask for the supplied native network_settings fields.
- Raises ValueError if neither boolean is provided.
- Reversible: re-call with the prior boolean values.

input_params.name: target_content_network

input_params.name: target_partner_search_network

actions.description: Suggest onboarding templates for creating conversion groups from synced platform conversion actions.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Onboarding assistant for brands with no or incomplete conversion_group setup. Call this after list_conversion_groups returns empty, or when the user asks for CPA/ROAS/Reg Rate/Pur Rate but no business conversion groups are configured.

The action inspects brand-visible synced conversion actions and returns recommended templates such as qualified-new-user and payment-success, including suggested rules with confidence and a human-readable reason.

This action is read-only. Applying the recommended setup requires the review-gated apply_conversion_group_template action.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Optional platform filter. Use google, meta, or chatgpt_ads; omit to inspect all.

input_params.name: template_slugs
input_params.description: Optional subset of template slugs. Supported values: qualified-new-user, payment-success.

actions.output_shape: {"configured": false, "existing_group_slugs": [], "templates": [{"slug": "qualified-new-user", "name": "Qualified New User", "already_configured": false, "derived_metrics": {"cost_per_label": "CPA", "rate_label": "Reg Rate", "rate_denominator": "clicks", "value_label": null, "roas_label": null}, "suggested_rules": [{"account_id": 5, "conversion_action_id": "sign_up", "conversion_action_name": "Sign Up", "confidence": 0.82, "reason": "..."}], "missing_required_mapping": false}]}

actions.description: Review-gated unlink of a conversion goal from a conversion group.
actions.long_description: Removes a goal link from an Admin View conversion group after human approval. Idempotent: unlinking a goal that is not linked still succeeds. Requires expected_version.

input_params.description: Internal goal id to unlink.

actions.description: Update ad fields on Google/Meta; review-gated. Google RSA headlines/descriptions are immutable.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Mutate an existing ad's status, URLs (Google), or creative wiring (Meta).

Use when:
- The user wants to activate, pause, or remove an ad.
- The user wants to update final_urls on a Google RSA.
- The user wants to swap a Meta ad's creative_id or tracking_specs.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to update Google RSA headlines or descriptions. These are NOT mutable; use action:ads/remove_ad then action:ads/create_ad.
- The user wants to update the ad group or ad set. Use action:ads/update_ad_group or action:ads/update_adset.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_ads to capture prior state.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_ads.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.message lists the fields updated; warnings[] may explain that RSA creative fields were skipped.
- Google returns success=false with the warning message if only RSA creative fields are supplied (nothing actually mutable).
- Meta raw is {success: true}.

Side effects:
- Google: status update targets AdGroupAd; final_urls update targets the Ad. Headlines/descriptions are silently skipped with a warning.
- Meta: POST {ad_id} with supplied fields. If creative_id is provided it is wrapped as creative={creative_id: ...}.
- Setting status to ENABLED/ACTIVE resumes spend immediately.
- Reversible if prior values captured.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google or meta

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_id
input_params.description: Ad id from action:ads/list_ads.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Google only: ENABLED | PAUSED | REMOVED (REMOVED is terminal). Invalid values now return a clear error instead of silently becoming PAUSED. Meta only: common status ENABLED->ACTIVE, PAUSED->PAUSED, REMOVED->ARCHIVED (REMOVED maps to ARCHIVED, not DELETED). Setting ACTIVE resumes spend immediately. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

input_params.name: final_urls
input_params.description: Google only: list of final landing URLs (or a JSON string).

input_params.name: headlines
input_params.description: Google only: RSA headlines ARE mutable in place (correcting prior docs). Provide the FULL replacement set of 3-15 headlines, each <=30 chars; replaces (not merges) existing headlines. Items may be strings or {text, pinned_field=HEADLINE_1|2|3}. Do NOT remove+recreate the ad just to change copy. Source: agent-generated replacement ad copy.

input_params.name: descriptions
input_params.description: Google only: RSA descriptions ARE mutable in place. Provide the FULL replacement set of 2-4 descriptions, each <=90 chars; replaces existing. Items may be strings or {text, pinned_field=DESCRIPTION_1|2}. Source: agent-generated replacement ad copy.

input_params.name: final_mobile_urls
input_params.description: Google only: replacement set of mobile-specific landing URLs on the Ad resource. Replace-not-merge. Source: user-provided mobile landing URL(s).

input_params.name: tracking_url_template
input_params.description: Google only: set/replace the ad-level click-tracking URL template. Pass an empty string to clear it. Source: user-provided tracking template.

input_params.name: creative_id
input_params.description: Meta only: swap to a different creative id.

input_params.name: creative
input_params.description: Meta only: inline AdCreative spec OR {"creative_id": "..."} to swap the ad's creative. If creative_id is also supplied, creative_id wins and the inline object is ignored. Swapping a creative re-enters ad review. Source: constructed by the agent or output of create_ad_creative.

input_params.name: tracking_specs
input_params.description: Meta only: list of conversion-tracking spec objects, e.g. [{"action.type": ["offsite_conversion"], "fb_pixel": ["<PIXEL_ID>"]}]. Source: pixel id from the account's tracking setup.

input_params.name: conversion_domain
input_params.description: Meta only: the attribution domain for a conversion/sales/leads ad (off-site optimization). Conditionally REQUIRED by Meta for those objectives; setting/repairing it is otherwise impossible. Must be a verified domain associated with the pixel event. Source: user-provided verified domain (must match Business Manager domain verification).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Meta only: new name for the ad (renames the Ad node). Google only: not applicable. Source: user-provided ad name.

actions.output_shape: {"success": true, "message": "Ad updated: status, final_urls", "results": [...], "warnings": ["Headlines and descriptions cannot be updated on an existing RSA..."]}  // Meta: {"success": true}

actions.description: Update Google Ads ad group name/status/cpc_bid_micros; review-gated, setting status=ENABLED resumes spend.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Mutate an existing Google ad group's name, status, or default CPC bid.

Use when:
- The user wants to activate or pause an ad group.
- The user wants to rename an ad group or change its default CPC bid.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/update_adset.
- The user wants to change keywords or ads. Use action:ads/update_keyword or action:ads/update_ad.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/gaql_search to capture the prior state.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message lists the fields actually updated.
- Returns success=false with 'No fields to update were provided.' if no mutable field is supplied.

Side effects:
- Field-mask update of (name, status, cpc_bid_micros) on the AdGroup.
- Setting status=ENABLED resumes spend immediately if the parent campaign is also enabled.
- Reversible if the prior value was captured.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: cpc_bid_micros: docs do not warn that it is a no-op (or error) unless the ad group is on a manual-CPC-compatible bidding strategy. An agent could set it under an automated strategy and report success while the bid has no effect. Also note: status: capability docs list 'ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED' but do not warn that an out-of-enum value is silently coerced to PAUSED by the handler. An agent that sends 'ACTIVE' or 'ENABLE' will unexpectedly PAUSE the ad group instead of getting an error — a potentially harmful silent behavior. Capability gap: No automated-bid override fields are exposed: target_cpa_micros, target_roas, cpm_bid_micros, percent_cpc_bid_micros, and the newer target_cpc_micros (added v22) are all mutable AdGroup fields per the official reference but absent from handler, capability docs, and normalize allowlist. Agents cannot set an ad-group-level Target CPA/ROAS or CPM bid override through this action — only manual cpc_bid_micros. This is a capability gap (P2; cpc covers the common manual-bidding case).

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Ad group id from action:ads/gaql_search.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New ad group display name.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: One of ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED.

input_params.name: cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: Default CPC bid in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000).

actions.output_shape: {"success": true, "message": "Ad group updated: name, status, cpc_bid_micros", "results": [...]}

actions.description: Update a Meta automated ad rule (name / specs / status); review-gated, reversible by writing back the previous values.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Modify an existing automated rule in place: rename it, replace its evaluation/execution/schedule specs, or turn it on/off via status.

Use when:
- The user wants to pause (disable) or re-enable an automated rule. Pausing a rule = status=DISABLED.
- Thresholds, filters, or the executed action of a rule need changing.
- A rule's schedule cadence needs adjusting.

Do not use when:
- The user wants the rule permanently gone. Use action:ads/remove_ad_rule.
- The user wants a new rule alongside the old one. Use action:ads/create_ad_rule.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Confirm rule_id via action:ads/list_ad_rules.
2. Provide only the fields to change among name / evaluation_spec / execution_spec / schedule_spec / status (at least one). Spec objects REPLACE the stored spec wholesale — send the complete new spec, not a partial patch.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_ad_rules.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.
- Returns success=false with error_code=not_found when the rule does not exist OR belongs to a different ad account than the resolved one (ownership pre-check).
- Returns success=false with error_code=invalid_param when no updatable field is provided.

Side effects:
- POSTs only the provided fields among {name, evaluation_spec, execution_spec, schedule_spec, status} to {rule_id}.
- STATUS ENUM WARNING: the status enum here is the AdRule enum ENABLED/DISABLED (plus HAS_ISSUES), NOT the generic ACTIVE/PAUSED lifecycle enum — the platform maps generic values (ENABLED/ACTIVE->ENABLED, PAUSED->DISABLED) before the write. status=DELETED is rejected with error_code=invalid_param: deleting a rule is irreversible and goes through action:ads/remove_ad_rule.
- Ownership is verified against the active account before the write.
- Reversible: write the previous name/specs/status back.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Used for the ownership pre-check: the rule must belong to this account or the action fails with not_found.

input_params.name: rule_id
input_params.description: Ad rule id from action:ads/list_ad_rules or action:ads/create_ad_rule (raw.id).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New rule display name. At least one updatable field must be provided.

input_params.name: evaluation_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: replacement evaluation spec (same shape as action:ads/create_ad_rule). REPLACES the stored spec wholesale — send the full spec including the entity_type scoping filter, not just the changed filters.

input_params.name: execution_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: replacement execution spec (same shape as action:ads/create_ad_rule; execution_type enum uses NOTIFICATION, not NOTIFY). REPLACES the stored spec wholesale.

input_params.name: schedule_spec
input_params.description: Meta only: replacement schedule spec (same shape as action:ads/create_ad_rule). REPLACES the stored spec wholesale.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Meta only: new rule status. WARNING — the enum here is the AdRule enum ENABLED / DISABLED (plus HAS_ISSUES), NOT the generic ACTIVE/PAUSED lifecycle enum; the platform maps generic values (PAUSED->DISABLED, ACTIVE->ENABLED). Pausing a rule = status=DISABLED. DELETED is rejected (invalid_param) — deletion is irreversible and goes through action:ads/remove_ad_rule. Source: constant enum chosen by the agent.

actions.description: Update Meta ad set fields; review-gated, setting status=ACTIVE resumes spend immediately.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Mutate an existing Meta ad set's status, targeting, budget, billing, or optimization goal.

Use when:
- The user wants to activate (status=ACTIVE) or pause an ad set.
- The user wants to change targeting, budget, bid_amount, or optimization_goal.

Do not use when:
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/update_ad_group.
- The ad set is archived and the user wants to re-activate. Meta requires un-archive to PAUSED first via update_adset status=PAUSED, then a second update_adset with status=ACTIVE.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_adsets to capture the prior state.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_adsets.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.

Side effects:
- POSTs the supplied fields to {adset_id}; Meta merges into the existing ad set.
- Setting status=ACTIVE resumes spend immediately if budget is non-zero.
- Reversible if prior values captured.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: adset_id
input_params.description: Ad set id from action:ads/list_adsets.

input_params.name: name

input_params.name: billing_event

input_params.name: frequency_control_specs
input_params.description: Meta only: frequency capping rules (REACH/awareness), list of {event, interval_days, max_frequency} objects. Source: constant config from the user's frequency cap request.

input_params.name: is_dynamic_creative
input_params.description: Meta only: toggle Dynamic Creative on the ad set. Source: user intent.

input_params.name: attribution_spec

input_params.name: destination_type
input_params.description: Meta only: conversion destination, e.g. WEBSITE, MESSENGER, WHATSAPP, INSTAGRAM_DIRECT. Source: constant enum chosen from objective/user intent.

actions.description: Update a reusable Google portfolio Target CPA or Target ROAS strategy.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Update a reusable Google portfolio Target CPA or Target ROAS strategy.

Use when:
- You need a reusable Google Target CPA or Target ROAS portfolio strategy.

Do not use when:
- You need a campaign-specific standard bidding strategy; use action:ads/update_campaign.

Workflow:
1. Read the current strategy or campaign state when changing an existing resource.
2. For write operations, submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify the resulting state with action:ads/get_bidding_strategy.

Output semantics:
- Returns the created, read, updated, listed, or removed Google portfolio strategy result.

Side effects:
- Creates, updates, or removes a Google portfolio strategy. Removing a strategy is irreversible and requires it to be unused.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: Must be google.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id without dashes.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Portfolio bidding strategy name; optional when updating.

input_params.name: bidding_strategy_id
input_params.description: Google portfolio bidding strategy id.

input_params.name: bidding_strategy_type
input_params.description: Portfolio strategy type; optional for a rename-only update.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Required when bidding_strategy_type is TARGET_CPA.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Required when bidding_strategy_type is TARGET_ROAS.

actions.description: Update campaign name/status/budget on Google/Meta Ads; review-gated, setting status to ENABLED/ACTIVE resumes spend immediately.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Mutate an existing campaign's name, status, budget, bidding strategy, or platform-specific settings. This is the canonical way to activate, pause, rename, rebudget, or change campaign-level bidding.

Use when:
- The user wants to activate a paused campaign (status -> ENABLED on Google or ACTIVE on Meta).
- The user wants to pause an active campaign or change its daily budget.
- The user wants to change a Google campaign bidding strategy (MAXIMIZE_CLICKS, TARGET_CPA, TARGET_ROAS, etc.).
- The user wants to retarget the Meta objective, bid_strategy, or spend_cap.

Do not use when:
- The campaign does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_campaign.
- The change touches ad groups, ad sets, ads, or keywords. Use the matching action:ads/update_ad_group / update_adset / update_ad / update_keyword.
- The user wants targeting changes. Use action:ads/set_geo_targeting, set_language_targeting, set_network_settings, or set_bid_modifier.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/list_campaigns to fetch the current state and capture old values for rollback.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/list_campaigns or action:ads/gaql_search with dimensions=['campaign','bidding_strategy'].

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- Google raw.message lists the fields actually updated (name, status, start_date, end_date, budget_amount_micros, bidding_strategy, target_cpa_micros, target_roas, max_cpc_bid_micros). Returns success=false with 'No fields to update were provided.' if no mutable field is supplied.
- Meta raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.

Side effects:
- Modifies platform-side campaign settings; reversible only if the caller captured the prior value before the update.
- Setting status to ENABLED (Google) or ACTIVE (Meta) resumes spend immediately if the campaign has non-zero budget.
- Google performs at most two MutateOperations in one mutate: a Campaign update plus an optional CampaignBudget update for amount_micros. Google bidding changes update the Campaign bidding oneof; TARGET_CPA requires target_cpa_micros and TARGET_ROAS requires target_roas. MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS accepts an optional target_cpa_micros and MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE an optional target_roas (Google's standard tCPA/tROAS forms); a target value that does not apply to the chosen strategy is rejected instead of silently dropped. Switching to MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS/MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE without a target clears any existing target on the campaign (warning emitted).
- Reversibility: re-call with prior values to roll back.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google, meta, or chatgpt_ads

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: campaign_id
input_params.description: Campaign id from action:ads/list_campaigns. Numeric on both platforms.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New campaign name.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Google/Meta use their native status contracts; for ChatGPT Ads, pass draft, active, or paused. active may fail with 402 if the advertiser has no payment method; deployment skills should keep new campaigns paused unless the user explicitly approves activation.

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Google only: new campaign start date in YYYY-MM-DD. Cannot be edited once the campaign has started. Source: user-provided date.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Google only: new campaign end date in YYYY-MM-DD. Source: user-provided date.

input_params.name: budget_daily_micros
input_params.description: Daily budget in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Google updates the linked CampaignBudget; Meta converts to daily_budget in cents.

input_params.name: bidding_strategy
input_params.description: Google only: campaign bidding strategy. One of MANUAL_CPC, MAXIMIZE_CLICKS, MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS, MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE, TARGET_CPA, TARGET_ROAS, TARGET_SPEND. Required when updating target_cpa_micros, target_roas, or max_cpc_bid_micros. Source: constant enum chosen from the user's bidding intent.

input_params.name: portfolio_bidding_strategy_id
input_params.description: Google only: reusable portfolio Target CPA/ROAS strategy id. Mutually exclusive with bidding_strategy and its target fields.

input_params.name: target_cpa_micros
input_params.description: Google only: target cost-per-action in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000). Required companion when bidding_strategy=TARGET_CPA; optional target when bidding_strategy=MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS (Google's standard tCPA form). Rejected for other strategies.

input_params.name: target_roas
input_params.description: Google only: return on ad spend as a ratio, e.g. 4.0 for 400%. Required companion when bidding_strategy=TARGET_ROAS; optional target when bidding_strategy=MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE (Google's standard tROAS form). Rejected for other strategies.

input_params.name: max_cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: Google only: optional CPC bid ceiling for MAXIMIZE_CLICKS / TARGET_SPEND. Ignored with a warning for other bidding strategies.

input_params.name: objective
input_params.description: Meta only: campaign objective (see create_campaign for legal values).

input_params.name: special_ad_categories
input_params.description: Meta only: special category list.

input_params.name: buying_type
input_params.description: Meta only: AUCTION or RESERVED.

input_params.name: bid_strategy
input_params.description: Meta only: bid strategy enum.

input_params.name: spend_cap
input_params.description: Meta only: campaign lifetime spend cap in account-currency cents (NOT micros). budget_daily_micros is in micros and auto-converted; spend_cap is raw cents -- do not confuse the two units. Source: user-provided cap converted to cents.

input_params.name: special_ad_category_country
input_params.description: Meta only: list of ISO country codes; required when special_ad_categories is updated to include a regulated category. Note: objective/buying_type/special_ad_categories are creation-time fields and Meta may reject changing them on an existing campaign. Source: user-provided target country/countries.

input_params.name: lifetime_budget
input_params.description: Meta only: campaign-level lifetime budget in account-currency cents (CBO). Mutually exclusive with the daily budget from budget_daily_micros. Only valid on Advantage Campaign Budget (CBO) campaigns; setting budget on an ad-set-budget campaign returns a Meta error. Source: user-provided total spend cap converted to cents.

input_params.name: start_time
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO-8601 start time.

input_params.name: stop_time
input_params.description: Meta only: ISO-8601 stop time.

actions.output_shape: {"success": true, "message": "Campaign updated: name, status, budget_amount_micros", "results": [...]}  // Meta: {"success": true}

actions.description: Update a Google conversion action (name, status ENABLED|HIDDEN, category, value settings) via field mask; risk-gated — value/category steer Smart Bidding.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Update an existing Google Ads conversion action by conversion_action_id. Field-mask update: only the fields provided are written; everything else is left untouched.

RISK — Smart Bidding input:
category and value settings (default_value / always_use_default_value) are direct Smart Bidding inputs. Changing them on a conversion action that is in use immediately changes bidding across every campaign optimizing toward it — same blast radius as a bid change, hence the risk gating.

Use when:
- The user wants to rename a conversion action.
- The user wants to pause reporting visibility (status=HIDDEN) or restore it (status=ENABLED).
- The user wants to re-categorize the action or change its default conversion value settings.

Do not use when:
- The action does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_conversion_action.
- The user wants to remove/delete the action. status accepts ENABLED or HIDDEN only — REMOVED is rejected here because removal is permanent; use action:ads/remove_conversion_action.
- The user wants to change type. It is immutable after create; create a new action instead.
- The platform is Meta. Meta conversion config lives on pixels/events.

Workflow:
1. Get conversion_action_id from action:ads/create_conversion_action raw.resource_id, from action:ads/list_conversion_actions (synced multi-platform list), or via action:ads/gaql_search on conversion_action.
2. Provide at least one of name / status / category / default_value / always_use_default_value.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search on conversion_action.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- The message lists exactly which fields were updated.
- Providing none of the updatable fields fails fast with a validation error before any mutate.

Side effects:
- category / value settings changes take effect immediately for Smart Bidding on all campaigns using this conversion action.
- Reversible by updating the fields back to their previous values (record them before the change).
- status=HIDDEN keeps recording conversions; it only hides them from default report views.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: conversion_action_id
input_params.description: Numeric conversion action id from action:ads/create_conversion_action raw.resource_id, action:ads/list_conversion_actions, or action:ads/gaql_search on conversion_action.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New display name; must stay unique within the account. At least one of name / status / category / default_value / always_use_default_value is required.

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: New status: ENABLED or HIDDEN only. REMOVED (or any other value) is rejected — removal is permanent and must go through action:ads/remove_conversion_action. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: category
input_params.description: New conversion category; same enum as action:ads/create_conversion_action (DEFAULT, PAGE_VIEW, PURCHASE, SIGNUP, ...). RISK: category feeds Smart Bidding goal grouping immediately. Source: constant enum.

input_params.name: default_value
input_params.description: New default monetary value per conversion (value_settings.default_value). RISK: on value-based bidding (tROAS / Maximize Conversion Value) this immediately changes what every conversion is worth to the bidder. Source: user-provided business value.

input_params.name: always_use_default_value
input_params.description: Whether every conversion is recorded with default_value instead of a transaction-specific value (value_settings.always_use_default_value). RISK: flipping this on an ecommerce action replaces real order values with the flat default for bidding. Source: user-provided. NOTE: to set this back to false, use `soku call ads update_conversion_action` with a JSON payload — the typed CLI renders booleans as valueless flags, so the typed command can only send true (omitting sends no field-mask update at all).

actions.description: Review-gated update of an Admin View conversion_group.
actions.long_description: Updates a brand-scoped Admin View conversion_group after human approval. Requires expected_version from list_conversion_groups; stale versions are rejected at approval execution time.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

input_params.name: group_id
input_params.description: Conversion group id.

input_params.name: expected_version
input_params.description: Opaque version from list_conversion_groups.groups[].version.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Updated display name.

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Updated stable slug.

input_params.name: metadata
input_params.description: Replacement [{key, value, description}] metadata fields.

actions.description: Update a Meta custom audience's name/description metadata; review-gated, reversible by writing back the previous values.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Rename a Meta custom audience or refresh its description without touching how the audience is populated.

Use when:
- The user wants to fix a typo in an audience name or align naming conventions.
- A workflow needs to annotate an audience with an updated description.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to change the audience rule, subtype, retention, or membership. Those are create-time properties; build a new audience via action:ads/create_custom_audience instead.
- The user wants to delete the audience. Use action:ads/remove_custom_audience.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Confirm audience_id via action:ads/list_custom_audiences.
2. Provide the new name and/or description (at least one).
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_custom_audiences.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.
- Returns success=false with error_code=not_found when the audience does not exist OR belongs to a different ad account than the resolved one (ownership pre-check).
- Returns success=false with error_code=invalid_param when neither name nor description is provided.

Side effects:
- POSTs only the provided fields among {name, description} to {audience_id}; targeting/population behavior is unchanged.
- Ownership is verified against the active account before the write.
- Reversible: write the previous name/description back.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Used for the ownership pre-check: the audience must belong to this account or the action fails with not_found.

input_params.name: audience_id
input_params.description: Custom audience id from action:ads/list_custom_audiences or action:ads/create_custom_audience (raw.id).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New audience display name. At least one of name / description must be provided.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: New audience description. At least one of name / description must be provided.

actions.description: Update a Google keyword's bid or status; review-gated, reversible if prior value known.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Mutate a single Google ad group keyword's cpc_bid_micros or status.

Use when:
- The user wants to raise/lower a keyword's CPC bid.
- The user wants to pause or re-enable a specific keyword.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to add new keywords. Use action:ads/add_keywords.
- The user wants to remove keywords permanently. Use action:ads/remove_keyword.
- The user wants to change match_type. match_type is not mutable; remove and re-add.

Workflow:
1. Call action:ads/gaql_search to capture criterion_id and prior values.
2. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
3. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.message lists the fields actually updated.
- Returns success=false if no mutable field is supplied or status is invalid.

Side effects:
- Field-mask update of (cpc_bid_micros, status) on the ad_group_criterion.
- Status validated against {ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED}.
- Reversible if prior value captured.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- google: Guidance: input_params marks both cpc_bid_micros and status as required=False with no note that AT LEAST ONE is mandatory; an agent reading only the param table could submit neither and get success=false. The rule lives only in long_description output semantics. Also note: No guidance that cpc_bid_micros only works under Manual CPC bidding. An agent could lower/raise a bid on an automated-bidding ad group and receive an opaque platform error with no recovery hint. Capability gap: final_url_overrides / keyword-level final URLs and tracking_url_template: AdGroupCriterion supports per-keyword final URLs and url tracking template; not exposable here (handler only edits cpc_bid_micros/status). Acceptable scope choice but worth noting as an unreachable mutable dimension.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: ad_group_id
input_params.description: Parent ad group id from action:ads/gaql_search.

input_params.name: criterion_id
input_params.description: Keyword criterion id from action:ads/gaql_search.criterion_id.

input_params.name: cpc_bid_micros
input_params.description: New CPC bid in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000).

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: One of ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED.

actions.description: Rename a Meta Pixel; review-gated, reversible by writing the previous name back.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Rename a Meta Pixel without touching its tracking configuration, base code, or collected event history.

Use when:
- The user wants to fix a typo in a pixel name or align naming conventions across accounts.

Do not use when:
- The user wants to delete a pixel. The Graph API has no pixel delete; renaming is the only lifecycle write on this surface.
- The user wants to change pixel configuration (data_use_setting, automatic matching, first-party cookie settings). Not exposed by this surface; use Meta Events Manager.
- The user wants to send events. Use action:ads/send_conversion_event.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Confirm pixel_id via action:ads/list_pixels.
2. Provide the new name.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/list_pixels.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Graph API body: {success: true}.
- Returns success=false with error_code=not_found when the pixel does not exist OR belongs to a different ad account than the resolved one (ownership pre-check).
- Returns success=false with error_code=invalid_param when name is not provided.

Side effects:
- POSTs only name to {pixel_id}; tracking, event history, and the installed base code are unchanged.
- Ownership is verified against the active account before the write: the handler reads the pixel's owner_ad_account and rejects mismatches as not_found, so pixels of other workspaces cannot be renamed.
- Reversible: write the previous name back.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. Used for the ownership pre-check: the pixel must belong to this account or the action fails with not_found.

input_params.name: pixel_id
input_params.description: Meta pixel id from action:ads/list_pixels or action:ads/create_pixel (raw.id).

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New pixel display name. The only updatable field on this surface; the handler rejects the call when it is missing.

actions.description: Rename an owned Meta product catalog; review-gated platform write.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Rename an existing Meta product catalog without touching its feeds, products, or product sets.

Use when:
- The user wants to fix or standardize a catalog display name.
- Catalog naming drifted from conventions across accounts.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Confirm catalog_id via action:ads/list_product_catalogs or action:ads/get_product_catalog_details.
2. Provide the new name; it is the only updatable field on this surface (vertical is create-only on the Graph API).
3. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
4. After approval, call action:ads/get_product_catalog_details to confirm the rename.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- POSTs only name to {catalog_id}; feeds, products, and product sets are unchanged.
- Catalog ownership is verified against the resolved account business before the write; a mismatched or unreadable catalog returns error_code=not_found.
- Reversible: write the previous name back.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.
