input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id to rename.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New catalog display name. The only updatable field on this surface — vertical is create-only on the Graph API — and the handler rejects the call with error_code=invalid_param when it is missing.

actions.description: Update a Meta product feed's name or fetch schedule; review-gated platform write.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Rename a product feed or replace its scheduled-fetch configuration in an owned catalog.

Use when:
- The user wants to change the feed's fetch cadence or source URL (schedule.url).
- A feed display name needs fixing or standardizing.

Do not use when:
- The user only needs to inspect catalog state. Use the matching list/get action first.
- The user needs cached ads performance metrics. Use action:ads/query_single_dimension or action:ads/query_multi_dimension instead.
- The platform is Google; this action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Get catalog_id from action:ads/list_product_catalogs.
2. Get product_feed_id from action:ads/create_product_feed (raw.id) or action:ads/get_product_feed_details.
3. Provide at least one of name / schedule. schedule REPLACES the stored scheduled-fetch config wholesale — send the complete object (url + interval), not a partial patch.
4. Submit through action:_batch/submit for HITL approval.
5. After approval, call action:ads/get_product_feed_details to confirm.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw is the Meta Graph API mutation response.
- The action is review-gated through the ads HITL path.

Side effects:
- POSTs only the provided fields among {name, schedule} to {product_feed_id}; already-ingested products are unchanged.
- A schedule change alters when and from where Meta fetches the feed file.
- Returns error_code=invalid_param when neither name nor schedule is provided, or when schedule.interval is outside the official enum.
- Catalog ownership AND the feed's membership in that catalog are verified before the write; mismatches return error_code=not_found.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: catalog_id
input_params.description: Owned Meta product catalog id that contains the feed.

input_params.name: product_feed_id
input_params.description: Meta product feed id to update.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New feed display name. At least one of name / schedule must be provided.

input_params.name: schedule
input_params.description: Meta only: replacement scheduled-fetch config; same shape as action:ads/create_product_feed schedule (url, interval enum HOURLY|DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY, hour, minute, day_of_week). REPLACES the stored schedule wholesale. At least one of name / schedule must be provided.

actions.description: Update a Google user list's metadata (name, description, membership_lifespan_days) via field mask; review-gated, reversible.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Update the metadata of an existing Google user list (Customer Match audience) by user_list_id. Field-mask update: only the fields provided are written; everything else is left untouched.

Use when:
- The user wants to rename a user list or fix its description.
- The user wants to change how long members stay in the list (membership_lifespan_days).

Do not use when:
- The list does not exist yet. Use action:ads/create_user_list.
- The user wants to delete a user list. User lists cannot be deleted via API.
- The user wants to change upload_key_type or app_id. Those are immutable after create; create a new list instead.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/update_custom_audience.

Workflow:
1. Get user_list_id from action:ads/create_user_list raw.resource_id, or discover it via action:ads/gaql_search (e.g. SELECT user_list.id, user_list.name, user_list.membership_life_span FROM user_list).
2. Provide at least one of name / description / membership_lifespan_days.
3. Submit via action:_batch/submit for HITL review.
4. Verify via action:ads/gaql_search on user_list.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- The message lists exactly which fields were updated.
- Providing none of the updatable fields fails fast with a validation error before any mutate.

Side effects:
- Metadata-only change; list membership and targeting links are untouched.
- Reversible by updating the fields back to their previous values.
- Shortening membership_lifespan_days can expire existing members earlier.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: user_list_id
input_params.description: Numeric user list id from action:ads/create_user_list raw.resource_id or action:ads/gaql_search on user_list.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: New display name; must stay unique within the account. At least one of name / description / membership_lifespan_days is required.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: New description. An explicit empty string clears the existing description. Source: user-provided text.

input_params.name: membership_lifespan_days
input_params.description: Days a contact stays in the list. Valid range: 0-540 inclusive, OR exactly 10000 for no expiration (CRM-based lists). Values 541-9999 are invalid. Same param name and validation as action:ads/create_user_list; maps to the UserList.membership_life_span proto field. Source: agent/user choice within the allowed range; constant integer.

actions.description: Upload an image to a Meta ad account from a public URL; executes immediately and returns image_hash for creative wiring.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Download a public https image, validate it, and POST the bytes to Meta's {account_id}/adimages. Returns image_hash for action:ads/create_ad_creative.

Use when:
- The user wants to ingest a new image asset for ad creatives.

Do not use when:
- The source URL is private, http (non-https), or an IP literal. The handler rejects such URLs before any upload.
- The image is larger than 25 MB. The handler returns an error.
- The platform is Google. Use action:ads/upload_image_asset (returns asset_id).

Workflow:
1. Get a public https image URL <= 25 MB without redirects.
2. Call this action directly (no HITL review; executes immediately).
3. Use the returned image_hash in action:ads/create_ad_creative.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.image_hash is the value to feed into create_ad_creative.image_hash.
- Errors: 'Image exceeds 25 MB byte limit.' or 'Image URL returned a redirect.'

Side effects:
- Downloads the URL (rejects redirects, enforces 25 MB cap, validates public https).
- Base64-encodes and POSTs to {account_id}/adimages; image is stored against the account.
- Consumes Meta storage quota but no ad spend.
- Reversible only via the Meta UI; no L1.5 remove handler.

Audit guidance (batch 4 P2):
- meta: Guidance: Capability docs describe image_url as the input but do not state the internal mechanism is base64 'bytes' upload (not a Meta-fetched URL), nor that the 25MB cap is stricter than Meta's ~30MB native limit — an agent could wrongly assume a <30MB image will succeed. Also note: Docs do not mention image format constraints (Meta accepts JPG/PNG; GIF/WEBP/other may be rejected by Meta with code 100), so the agent cannot pre-validate format. Capability gap: copy_from: cross-account image reuse (clone an existing hash into another account) is unreachable in all three layers; agent must re-upload bytes per account.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: image_url
input_params.description: Public https URL of the image; rejects http, redirects, IP literals, and files > 25 MB.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional file name. Default "upload.jpg".

actions.description: Upload an image to a Google Ads account asset library from a public https URL; executes immediately and returns the asset_id.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Download a public https image and create a Google ImageAsset via AssetService. Returns asset_id (+ resource_name) to reference in create_pmax_campaign's asset_group or Display/Video creatives.

Use when:
- A Google PMax / Display / Video campaign needs an image asset the account does not have yet.

Do not use when:
- The image URL is not public https, or is larger than 5 MB.
- The platform is Meta. Use action:ads/upload_image (returns image_hash).

Workflow:
1. Get a public https image URL <= 5 MB.
2. Call this action directly (no HITL review; executes immediately).
3. Feed the returned asset_id into the asset_group / creative.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- resource_id is the numeric asset id; raw.resource_name is the full path.
- Google content-deduplicates images: re-uploading returns the same asset.

Side effects:
- Downloads the URL (enforces public https + 5 MB cap).
- Creates an account-level ImageAsset; no ad spend.
- Reversible only via the Google Ads UI; no L1.5 remove handler.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: google (Google-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Google customer id (digits only, no dashes).

input_params.name: image_url
input_params.description: Public https URL of the image; rejects http and files > 5 MB.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Optional asset name (omit to let Google de-duplicate).

actions.description: Upload multiple images to a Meta ad account from direct base64 bytes or public URLs with bounded concurrency; executes immediately and returns per-item image_hash values.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Bulk-ingest local image bytes or public https image URLs into Meta's {account_id}/adimages edge. Use this instead of many serial upload_image calls when the user has multiple assets for one account.

Use when:
- The user wants to upload more than one image asset for Meta creatives.
- A batch campaign workflow has local files or public URLs and needs image_hash values before creative creation.

Do not use when:
- A URL source is private, http (non-https), an IP literal, redirects, or points to an image larger than 25 MB. Invalid items fail individually.
- The platform is Google. This action is Meta-only.

Workflow:
1. Build images[] with {client_ref, name, image_url} for public URLs or {client_ref, name, bytes_base64} for local files. client_ref should be stable so the returned item can be mapped back to the source asset.
2. Call this action directly (no HITL review; executes immediately). When image count is greater than 2, the handler uses bounded concurrency (default 6, max 10) plus transient Meta retry/backoff per image.
3. Feed each successful items[].image_hash into create_ad_creative or deploy_image_ads_batch.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.items[] preserves input order and includes index, client_ref, image_hash, retry_count, and per-item error/error_code.
- raw.success=false means at least one item failed; successful items remain usable.

Side effects:
- Downloads each URL (rejects redirects, enforces 25 MB cap, validates public https) or decodes bytes_base64 directly.
- POSTs successful items to {account_id}/adimages using Meta's bytes field.
- Consumes Meta storage quota but no ad spend.
- Reversible only via the Meta UI; no L1.5 remove handler.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Meta ad account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id.

input_params.name: images
input_params.description: List of image upload objects. Each item should include name, stable client_ref, and exactly one of image_url (public https URL) or bytes_base64 (local image bytes encoded by the CLI).

input_params.name: concurrency
input_params.description: Optional concurrency limit for >2 images. Default 6, max 10. Values outside the range are clamped by the handler.

actions.description: Upload a video to a Meta ad account from a public URL; executes immediately and returns video_id for async encoding.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Submit a public https video URL to Meta's {account_id}/advideos. Meta downloads + encodes asynchronously; the returned video_id may not be immediately usable in creatives.

Use when:
- The user wants to ingest a new video asset for ad creatives.

Do not use when:
- The source URL is private, http, or an IP literal. The handler rejects it.
- The platform is Google.

Workflow:
1. Get a public https video URL.
2. Call this action directly (no HITL review; executes immediately).
3. After Meta finishes encoding, use the video_id in action:ads/create_ad_creative.

Output semantics:
- Top-level envelope: see Cross-cutting envelope in capabilities-api.md.
- raw.id is the new video_id; raw.success indicates upload accepted, not encoding done.

Side effects:
- POSTs file_url to {account_id}/advideos; Meta pulls and encodes the file asynchronously.
- Encoding is async on Meta's side — the video may not be ready for creatives immediately.
- No immediate spend; consumes Meta storage quota.
- Reversible only via the Meta UI.

input_params.name: platform
input_params.description: meta (Meta-only action).

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Account id from ads.list_ad_accounts.rows[].account_id. For Google, digits only without dashes.

input_params.name: file_url
input_params.description: Meta only: public https URL of the video to ingest. Required -- this is the only supported upload path (Meta's resumable chunked/source upload for very large files is NOT supported by this action). Must be a direct https URL (no http, no IP literal, no redirect); the handler SSRF-validates it and rejects redirects. Prefer reasonably sized MP4/H.264 clips; multi-GB assets that require chunked upload will fail. Source: user-provided public https URL of the video file.

input_params.name: title
input_params.description: Optional video title.

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Optional video description.

input_params.name: name
input_params.description: Meta only: optional asset name for the uploaded video in the ad account media library. Distinct from title/description (which annotate the video). Free-form, agent- or user-provided. Source: agent-provided or user-provided label (e.g. a campaign/asset naming convention).

actions.description: List the AppsFlyer accounts this brand can query.
actions.long_description: Returns the AppsFlyer accounts granted to the current brand (account_id + name). START HERE for any AppsFlyer task: every other appsflyer action requires one of these account_ids. Do NOT ask the user for an account id — this action is the source of truth. A single AppsFlyer MCP token maps to one account; individual apps within it are chosen via tool arguments, not as separate resources.

actions.description: List the AppsFlyer attribution/analytics tools available for an account.
actions.long_description: Returns the live AppsFlyer MCP tool catalog for one granted account, each annotated with `requires_review`. The default response is a slim catalog without input schemas. Pass `tool` to fetch one exact tool's full input schema before calling `appsflyer/query`. Tools with `requires_review=true` (anything not on our read-only allowlist) must go through `appsflyer/request_change`, not `appsflyer/query`. Data is live from AppsFlyer (realtime, no cached pipeline); AppsFlyer MCP is Open Beta, so treat results as best-effort.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: AppsFlyer account id (from appsflyer/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact AppsFlyer tool name; when provided, returns that tool's full input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.project_id, data.tools[].{name, description, requires_review[, input_schema when tool is provided]}

actions.description: Run a read-only AppsFlyer tool (aggregated performance, SKAN, cost & ad-revenue integrations, OneLink, audiences, app settings).
actions.long_description: Forwards a read-only AppsFlyer tool call for one granted account and returns live data (realtime — there is no cached sync pipeline). The hosted MCP exposes aggregated attribution/performance (`fetch_aggregated_data`: installs, ROAS, LTV, retention by media-source/campaign/date), SKAN aggregates (`skan_get_app_data`), cost & ad-revenue integration health, OneLink templates/links, active audiences, and app settings. Choose the app via tool arguments. Get the account_id from `appsflyer/list_accounts` (never ask the user for it). Only allowlisted read tools run immediately; anything else returns `requires_review` — call `appsflyer/request_change`. The response echoes which account was queried.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: AppsFlyer account id (from appsflyer/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact AppsFlyer tool name from appsflyer/list_tools.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured, truncated}

actions.description: Request a non-allowlisted AppsFlyer tool (or a future write) via human approval.
actions.long_description: Submits an AppsFlyer tool call that is not on our read-only allowlist for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the call is forwarded to AppsFlyer only after a human approves. Use this for anything `appsflyer/query` rejected as requires_review. Note: account-wide user listing (`get_users`) and product feedback (`send_feedback`) are hard-hidden and never available, even here.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: AppsFlyer account id (from appsflyer/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact AppsFlyer tool name.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured}

actions.description: Write to a GraphQL toolkit's official API by POSTing a mutation GraphQL document via human approval.
actions.long_description: The write action for GraphQL toolkits (e.g. Monday). GraphQL uses one POST endpoint for reads and writes, so writes cannot use request_change; this action POSTs a mutation document for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the request is forwarded to the vendor only after a human approves. The document must contain a top-level mutation (a pure query is steered to composio/graphql_query; subscriptions are rejected). Get the (account_id, toolkit) from composio/list_accounts; the mutation schema comes from the provider's official API reference (docs_url, or web_search it) — never guess a mutation shape.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Connected account id from composio/list_accounts.

input_params.name: toolkit
input_params.description: GraphQL toolkit of the account (from composio/list_accounts).

input_params.name: endpoint
input_params.description: The toolkit's GraphQL endpoint — a relative path (recommended) or an absolute https URL on the toolkit's official host.

input_params.name: body
input_params.description: GraphQL request body: a 'query' string (must contain a mutation operation) and optional 'variables' object.

example.query: mutation ($b: ID!, $g: String!, $n: String!) { create_item(board_id: $b, group_id: $g, item_name: $n) { id } }

input_params.name: parameters
input_params.description: Optional Composio proxy parameter objects.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{toolkit, endpoint, method, status, data}

actions.description: Read from a GraphQL toolkit's official API by POSTing a query-only GraphQL document (relayed through Composio; runs immediately).
actions.long_description: The read action for GraphQL toolkits (e.g. Fireflies). GraphQL uses one POST endpoint for reads and writes, so it cannot use proxy_get; this action POSTs a query-only document to the toolkit's GraphQL endpoint and runs immediately (no HITL). Mutations and subscriptions are rejected here — writes go through composio/graphql_mutate where the toolkit has them enabled. Get the (account_id, toolkit) from composio/list_accounts; the schema (types, queries, variables) comes from the provider's official API reference (docs_url, or web_search it). Data is realtime (no cached pipeline). Bulk-read rule: GraphQL is natively batch-friendly — fetch many items with only the fields you need in ONE query (list field + selection set + limit/pagination arguments from the official schema); never loop item-by-item over a list you could select in a single document. Size pages so one response fits in one tool result.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Connected account id from composio/list_accounts.

input_params.name: toolkit
input_params.description: GraphQL toolkit of the account (from composio/list_accounts).

input_params.name: endpoint
input_params.description: The toolkit's GraphQL endpoint — a relative path (recommended, e.g. /graphql) or an absolute https URL on the toolkit's official host.

input_params.name: body
input_params.description: GraphQL request body: a 'query' string (query operation only) and optional 'variables' object.

input_params.name: parameters
input_params.description: Optional Composio proxy parameter objects.

actions.description: List the connected third-party accounts (Gmail, Notion, Google Sheets, ...) this brand can use.
actions.long_description: Returns the Composio connected accounts granted to the current brand: each row is (account_id, toolkit, name, status, execution_available, api_shape, docs_url, api_hosts, recommended). START HERE for any third-party task — every proxy action needs an (account_id, toolkit) pair from this list; neither is guessable. Do NOT ask the user for an account id; this action is the source of truth. Every connected toolkit is executable (execution_available is true). api_shape is 'rest' (use proxy_get / request_change) or 'graphql' (use graphql_query / graphql_mutate). docs_url is the provider's OFFICIAL API reference when vetted; when null, web_search the provider's official API reference before building requests. Unless you are certain of the exact endpoint, consult the reference first — never guess endpoints from memory or use Composio tool names. api_hosts lists the toolkit's official hosts: a relative endpoint resolves against the toolkit's PRIMARY host only, so an endpoint served from any other host in api_hosts must be an absolute https URL.
actions.output_shape: data.rows[].{account_id, toolkit, name, status, execution_available, api_shape, docs_url, api_hosts, recommended}

actions.description: Read from a connected account's official API (HTTP GET, relayed through Composio with the account credential injected).
actions.long_description: Forwards a GET request to the toolkit's official API; Composio injects the connected account's OAuth credential server-side (you never see a token). Get the (account_id, toolkit) from composio/list_accounts FIRST, and consult the official API reference (docs_url, or web_search it) when unsure of an endpoint. Put query parameters directly in the endpoint URL. Only the toolkit's official API hosts are allowed. Anything that mutates state (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) must go through composio/request_change. Data is realtime (no cached pipeline). Bulk-read rule: before fetching more than a few similar items, check the official API reference for a cheaper request shape, in priority order: (1) server-side filtering/search parameters, (2) a list/bulk endpoint returning many items with the fields you need in one call, (3) field projection / partial responses (fields= / format= style parameters) so you fetch only what you need, (4) pagination sized so one page fits in one tool result. Loop item-by-item only when the official API offers none of these, and say so. A vendor batch endpoint that requires POST is a write here (HITL) even if it only reads — for reads, prefer GET-shaped list/search endpoints.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Connected account id, copied verbatim from composio/list_accounts — never constructed by hand.

input_params.name: toolkit
input_params.description: Toolkit of the account (from composio/list_accounts).

input_params.name: endpoint
input_params.description: Relative path (e.g. /gmail/v1/users/me/profile) ONLY for endpoints on the toolkit's primary host — Composio pins relative paths to that one host. For an endpoint on any other official host of the toolkit (see api_hosts from list_accounts), pass an absolute https URL — e.g. listing Google Docs/Sheets files is a Drive API call: https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files?q=...

input_params.name: parameters
input_params.description: Optional Composio proxy parameter objects; prefer inlining query params in the endpoint URL instead.

actions.description: Write to a connected account's official API (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) via human approval.
actions.long_description: Submits an official-API write for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the request is forwarded to the vendor only after a human approves. Provide method + endpoint + body (e.g. send a Gmail message, append a Google Sheets row, update a Notion page). Get the (account_id, toolkit) from composio/list_accounts; the exact endpoint and body shape come from the provider's official API reference (docs_url, or web_search it) — never guess a write payload.

input_params.name: account_id
input_params.description: Connected account id, copied verbatim from composio/list_accounts — never constructed by hand.

input_params.name: toolkit
input_params.description: Toolkit of the account (from composio/list_accounts).

input_params.name: method
input_params.description: HTTP write method.

input_params.name: endpoint
input_params.description: Relative path ONLY for endpoints on the toolkit's primary host (Composio pins relative paths to that one host); for any other official host of the toolkit (see api_hosts from list_accounts), pass an absolute https URL.

input_params.name: body
input_params.description: JSON request body per the official API.

input_params.name: parameters
input_params.description: Optional Composio proxy parameter objects.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{toolkit, endpoint, method, status, data}

actions.description: Cached GA4 key-event summary for a property over a date range; returns total key events, revenue, and top converting traffic sources.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Conversion summary using GA4 v5+ key_events, together with the top sources that drove those events.

Use when:
- The user asks for total conversions or key events for a period.
- The user asks which sources drove the most conversions.
- You need a quick conversion + revenue snapshot without picking specific events.

Do not use when:
- The user needs event-level counts beyond conversions. Use list_events.
- The user needs a daily key-event time series. Use get_daily_trend and read rows[].key_events.
- The user needs per-page conversion breakdown. Use list_top_pages and read rows[].key_events.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date_start, date_end.

Output semantics:
- overview.total_key_events sums property-level key_events for the range.
- overview.total_revenue_micros is in micros.
- overview.top_converting_sources has the same row shape as list_traffic_sources, sorted by key_events DESC and capped at 10.
- Returns zeros and an empty top_converting_sources when the property has no synced data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

actions.output_shape: {"property": {"property_id": "123456789", "data_freshness": "ISO"}, "overview": {"total_key_events": N, "total_revenue_micros": micros, "top_converting_sources": [{"session_source": "...", "session_medium": "...", "session_campaign_name": "...", "key_events": N, "total_revenue_micros": micros, "sessions": N, "total_users": N, ...}]}}

actions.description: Cached GA4 daily KPI time series for a property over a date range; returns one row per day for charting and anomaly detection.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Per-day GA4 KPI time series for a property.

Use when:
- The user asks for a daily chart, week-over-week or day-over-day comparison, or anomaly check.
- You need report_date x property-level metrics ordered by day.

Do not use when:
- The user needs a single aggregated snapshot. Use get_property_overview.
- The user needs per-page, per-source, or per-event daily breakdown. This action does not split by dimension.
- The user wants daily ad spend. Use ads.query_single_dimension with dimension=date.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date_start, date_end.

Output semantics:
- rows[].report_date is ISO YYYY-MM-DD.
- rows[].total_revenue_micros is in micros; rows[].bounce_rate is a 0-1 ratio; rows[].avg_session_duration_seconds is in seconds.
- rows[].key_events replaces legacy 'conversions' (GA4 v5+).
- Returns [] when the property has no synced data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

actions.output_shape: {"property": {"property_id": "123456789", "data_freshness": "ISO"}, "rows": [{"report_date": "2026-05-01", "total_users": N, "new_users": N, "active_users": N, "sessions": N, "screen_page_views": N, "avg_session_duration_seconds": seconds, "bounce_rate": 0..1, "events_count": N, "key_events": N, "total_revenue_micros": micros, "currency": "USD"}]}

actions.description: Live GA4 Data API metadata discovery for one property; returns queryable dimensions and metrics, including custom definitions when GA4 exposes them.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Discover valid GA4 Data API dimension and metric api_name values before calling ga4.run_report for fields outside the cached action set.

Use when:
- You need a GA4 dimension or metric that is not covered by fixed cached actions.
- ga4.run_report returned ga4_invalid_query and you need valid selectable fields.
- You need custom GA4 definitions exposed by the property metadata endpoint.

Do not use when:
- You only need Soku metric registry semantics. Use ga4.list_metrics.
- You only need standard cached summaries, trends, pages, sources, or events.

Output semantics:
- dimensions[].api_name and metrics[].api_name are the names accepted by run_report.
- custom_definition=true means the field is property-specific.
- This is live GA4 Data API metadata and requires a valid GA4 credential.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

actions.description: Aggregate GA4 KPI snapshot for a property over a date range; returns one overview block with users, sessions, page views, key events, and revenue.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Headline KPIs for a GA4 property over a date range, without dimensional breakdown. Use it for a one-shot snapshot answer.

Use when:
- The user asks for a property-level summary across a window.
- You need total/new/active users, sessions, page views, key events, or revenue across the period.

Do not use when:
- The user needs per-page, per-channel, or per-event breakdown. Use list_top_pages, list_traffic_sources, or list_events.
- The user needs a daily time series. Use get_daily_trend.
- The user needs CPA / ROAS or ad spend. Use ads.query_single_dimension.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date_start, date_end.

Output semantics:
- overview.total_revenue_micros is in micros (1 USD = 1_000_000); pair with overview.currency for display.
- overview.bounce_rate is a 0-1 ratio.
- overview.avg_session_duration_seconds is in seconds.
- overview.key_events is the GA4 v5+ replacement for the legacy 'conversions' concept.
- Returns null overview when the property has no synced data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

actions.output_shape: {"property": {"property_id": "123456789", "data_freshness": "ISO"}, "overview": {"total_users": N, "new_users": N, "active_users": N, "sessions": N, "screen_page_views": N, "avg_session_duration_seconds": seconds, "bounce_rate": 0..1, "events_count": N, "key_events": N, "total_revenue_micros": micros, "currency": "USD"}}

actions.description: Cached GA4 event breakdown for a property over a date range; returns rows of event_name with event_count, event_value_micros, and per-event unique_users (deduplicated users) for funnel / conversion analysis.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Event-level GA4 analytics — not limited to conversion events. Includes per-event unique_users, the building block for user-level funnel and conversion-rate analysis.

Use when:
- The user asks about any GA4 event volume (scroll, session_start, add_to_cart, purchase, etc.).
- You need per-event counts, event value sums, or unique users for a date range.
- You are computing a funnel (people per step) or per-step conversion rate.

Do not use when:
- The user wants only conversion / key-event totals. Use get_conversion_overview.
- The user wants per-page or per-source breakdown. Use list_top_pages or list_traffic_sources.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date range, and optional event_names filter / limit.

Output semantics:
- Default sort is event_count DESC.
- rows[].event_value_micros sums GA4 event_value in micros; events without value report 0.
- rows[].unique_users is deduplicated users for that event over the WHOLE date range (count people, not events) — the metric to use for funnels.
- unique_users_basis qualifies every rows[].unique_users value:
  - 'exact': single-day query (cached daily dedup) or cross-day query resolved via a live GA4 deduplicated lookup.
  - 'approximate': cross-day query whose live dedup lookup was unavailable; unique_users is a daily sum. It can overcount cross-day returning users and can undercount when legacy partitions missing the column contribute no value (unique_users_note explains it). Treat as directional, not precise.
  - null: no unique_users available (e.g. legacy partitions synced before the column existed); rows[].unique_users may be null.
- 'exact' for a single-day query assumes the requested day matches the GA4 property timezone day; for a non-UTC property a day boundary can shift the dedup window slightly.
- A derived per-user rate (event_count / unique_users) is approximate on cross-day queries because count comes from the cached snapshot while unique_users comes from a live lookup.
- When event_names is omitted, returns top events ranked by event_count up to limit.
- Returns [] (and unique_users_basis null) when the property has no synced event data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: event_names
input_params.description: GA4 event_name slugs to filter (string list). Omit to list all events ranked by count. Empty/falsy entries are ignored.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 100, hard cap 500. page_size accepted as an alias.

actions.description: Discover GA4 metric slugs, units, and formulas from the Metric Registry before reading them as field names in other ga4.* outputs.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Metric Registry discovery filtered to source=ga4. Same registry as the metrics block in ads.list_dimensions, but scoped to GA4 metrics only.

Use when:
- You are unsure which metric slug, unit, or formula a GA4 field uses.
- You need to map a metric slug to its dependency chain or platform support.

Do not use when:
- You need a queryable dimension list. ga4 has no list_dimensions equivalent; other ga4.* actions return fixed column sets.
- You need ads-side metric semantics. Use ads.list_dimensions.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. Match returned slugs to columns in get_property_overview, get_daily_trend, list_top_pages, list_traffic_sources, or list_events output.

Output semantics:
- rows[].slug is the canonical metric name (e.g. engagement_rate, total_users).
- rows[].unit is the metric unit class (ratio, micros, seconds, count).
- rows[].available_on lists the platforms exposing this metric; for source=ga4 it always includes 'ga4'.
- This is metadata; no property_id or date range needed and no data is queried.

actions.description: Discover org-bound GA4 properties; returns property_id values required by every other ga4.* action.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Property discovery for the current brand scope. Use the returned property_id values as the only valid property_id inputs for later ga4.* analytics calls. Only properties granted to the caller's brand are returned.

Use when:
- The user did not provide a property_id.
- The user asks which GA4 properties are available.
- You need to choose a GA4 property before querying overviews, trends, pages, sources, or events.

Do not use when:
- You already have a property_id from this response in the same workflow.
- You need ad accounts. Use ads.list_ad_accounts instead.

Workflow:
1. Call this action.
2. Pick one row's property_id.
3. Reuse that property_id (alias account_id is accepted) in any later ga4.* call.

Output semantics:
- rows[].property_id is the external GA4 property id expected by later calls.
- rows[].data_freshness is the latest completed GA4 sync run finish time; null when never synced.

Constraints:
- Rows are scoped to the caller's organization and active ResourceBrandGrant.
- A property absent from this response is not queryable by other ga4 actions.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Max rows. Default 100, hard cap 500. page_size accepted as an alias.

actions.description: Cached GA4 top-page ranking for a date range; returns rows of page_path/page_title with views, users, engagement, and key events.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Page-level ranking over a property's synced GA4 data.

Use when:
- The user asks for most-viewed pages, top landing pages, or best-performing content.
- You need page_path x per-page metrics for a date range.

Do not use when:
- The user needs a per-page time series. This action does not split by day.
- The user needs traffic source breakdown. Use list_traffic_sources.
- The user needs event-level breakdown. Use list_events.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date range, and optional sort_by / limit.

Output semantics:
- Default sort is screen_page_views DESC.
- rows[].bounce_rate is a 0-1 ratio; rows[].avg_session_duration_seconds is in seconds.
- rows[].key_events is page-level conversion count (GA4 v5+ naming).
- Returns [] when the property has no synced page data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 20, hard cap 500. page_size accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: sort_by
input_params.description: Sort metric. One of screen_page_views (default), total_users, avg_session_duration_seconds, bounce_rate, key_events. Unknown values fall back to screen_page_views.

actions.description: Cached GA4 traffic source ranking for a date range; returns rows of session_source/medium/campaign with users, sessions, page views, and revenue.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Acquisition breakdown across session_source x session_medium x session_campaign_name.

Use when:
- The user asks where traffic comes from, top channels, organic vs paid, or campaign-level acquisition.
- You need session-level acquisition metrics for a date range.

Do not use when:
- The user needs page-level data. Use list_top_pages and join client-side.
- The user needs event-level data. Use list_events.
- The user needs ad spend, CPA, or ROAS. Use ads.query_single_dimension.

Workflow:
1. Call list_properties if property_id is unknown.
2. Call this action with property_id, date range, and optional sort_by / limit.

Output semantics:
- Default sort is sessions DESC.
- rows[].total_revenue_micros is in micros; rows[].bounce_rate is a 0-1 ratio; rows[].avg_session_duration_seconds is in seconds.
- rows[].key_events replaces legacy 'conversions' (GA4 v5+).
- Returns [] when the property has no synced source data for the range.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 20, hard cap 500. page_size accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: sort_by
input_params.description: Sort metric. One of sessions (default), total_users, new_users, screen_page_views, bounce_rate, avg_session_duration_seconds, key_events, total_revenue_micros. Unknown values fall back to sessions.

actions.output_shape: {"property": {"property_id": "123456789", "data_freshness": "ISO"}, "rows": [{"session_source": "google", "session_medium": "organic", "session_campaign_name": "...", "total_users": N, "new_users": N, "sessions": N, "screen_page_views": N, "bounce_rate": 0..1, "avg_session_duration_seconds": seconds, "key_events": N, "total_revenue_micros": micros}]}

actions.description: Live GA4 Data API RunReport for ad-hoc custom dimension x metric queries, filters, date ranges, and property-specific fields.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Execute a constrained GA4 RunReport request against one brand-granted property. Prefer cached actions for standard summaries; use this when the user asks for a GA4 breakdown, filter, or custom field outside the fixed cached shapes.

Use when:
- The requested dimensions or metrics are not available in cached actions.
- You need custom GA4 fields discovered through ga4.get_metadata.
- You need live GA4 results instead of the latest synced parquet snapshot.

Do not use when:
- A cached action can answer the question; cached actions are cheaper and more stable.
- You need Google Ads spend, campaign, or keyword data. Use ads.* actions.

Supported DSL:
- property_id plus either date_ranges[] or date_start/date_end.
- dimensions[] and metrics[]. metrics is required; dimensions may be empty.
- dimension_filter and metric_filter support string_filter, in_list_filter, numeric_filter, between_filter, plus and_group/or_group/not_expression.
- order_bys[] entries must reference requested dimensions or metrics.
- limit defaults to 100 and is capped at 1000; offset defaults to 0.

Output semantics:
- rows are row-shaped dictionaries; columns is the same data pivoted by field.
- When multiple date_ranges are requested, rows and columns include dateRange, the zero-based GA4 date range index.
- row_count is the GA4 total available rows, not only the returned page length.
- truncated=true means more rows exist after offset + returned rows.

input_params.name: property_id
input_params.description: Property id from list_properties.rows[].property_id. account_id is accepted as an alias.

input_params.name: date_start
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format when date_ranges is omitted.

input_params.name: date_end
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format when date_ranges is omitted.

input_params.name: date_ranges
input_params.description: Optional GA4 date ranges, each with start_date/end_date. Overrides date_start/date_end.

input_params.name: dimensions
input_params.description: GA4 dimension api_name values from get_metadata.

input_params.name: metrics
input_params.description: GA4 metric api_name values from get_metadata.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 100, hard cap 1000.

input_params.name: offset
input_params.description: Zero-based row offset. Default 0.

actions.description: Daily Google Search organic time series for a property; returns one row per day (ascending) with clicks, impressions, CTR, and position.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Convenience wrapper over searchAnalytics.query with dimensions=[date] — per-day organic performance for charting and anomaly detection.

Use when:
- The user asks for an organic traffic trend, a daily chart, or week-over-week organic comparison.

Do not use when:
- You need per-query or per-page daily breakdowns: use query_search_analytics with dimensions=['date', ...].
- You need on-site daily sessions/users: use ga4.get_daily_trend.

Workflow:
1. Call list_sites if site_url is unknown.
2. Call this action with site_url, start_date, end_date.

Output semantics:
- rows[].date is ISO YYYY-MM-DD; rows are returned in ascending date order.
- Days with zero organic activity are absent, not zero-filled.
- The most recent ~2 days may be missing or partial (Search Console data lag).
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Search analytics data lags ~2 days behind real time.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

actions.description: Inspect one URL's Google index status via the URL Inspection API; returns index verdict, coverage state, canonical, mobile usability, and rich results.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Per-URL diagnosis of how Google sees a page: indexed or not, why, which canonical Google chose, last crawl time, mobile usability, and rich result validation.

Use when:
- The user asks whether a specific page is indexed, why it is not ranking, or what Google's canonical for it is.

Do not use when:
- You need aggregate coverage across many URLs: inspect one URL at a time and mind the quota (below); for bulk health prefer list_sitemaps plus query_search_analytics with dimensions=['page'].
- You want to request (re)indexing: this integration is read-only and the Indexing API is out of scope.

Quota (hard, per Google):
- ~2000 inspections per DAY per property and ~600 per MINUTE. Exhaustion surfaces as gsc_quota_exceeded (HTTP 429); budget calls accordingly and never loop this action over large URL lists.

Constraints:
- inspection_url must belong to the property identified by site_url (same URL-prefix scope or under the sc-domain).
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

Output semantics:
- inspection_result.indexStatusResult.verdict is PASS / FAIL / NEUTRAL; coverageState is the human-readable index coverage (e.g. 'Submitted and indexed').
- googleCanonical vs userCanonical mismatch indicates a canonicalization issue.
- inspection_result.inspectionResultLink opens the same report in the Search Console UI.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

input_params.name: inspection_url
input_params.description: Fully-qualified URL to inspect. Must be inside the site_url property's scope.

actions.description: List sitemaps submitted for a Search Console property; returns per-sitemap status, submitted/indexed counts, warnings, and errors.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Sitemap inventory and health for one property (sitemaps.list).

Use when:
- The user asks which sitemaps are submitted, whether a sitemap has errors/warnings, or how many URLs were submitted vs indexed.

Do not use when:
- You need per-URL index status: use inspect_url.
- You want to submit or delete a sitemap: this integration is read-only.

Workflow:
1. Call list_sites if site_url is unknown.
2. Call this action with site_url.

Output semantics:
- rows[].path is the sitemap URL; rows[].is_pending means Google has not processed it yet; rows[].contents breaks submitted/indexed counts down by content type (web, image, video, ...).
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

actions.output_shape: {"site": {"site_url": "sc-domain:example.com", "permission_level": "siteOwner"}, "rows": [{"path": "https://example.com/sitemap.xml", "last_submitted": "ISO", "last_downloaded": "ISO", "is_pending": false, "is_sitemaps_index": false, "type": "sitemap", "warnings": N, "errors": N, "contents": [{"type": "web", "submitted": N, "indexed": N}]}]}

actions.description: Discover brand-granted Search Console properties; returns site_url values required by every other gsc.* action.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Property discovery for the current brand scope. Use the returned site_url values as the only valid site_url inputs for later gsc.* calls. Only properties granted to the caller's brand are returned.

Use when:
- The user did not provide a site_url.
- The user asks which Search Console properties are available.
- A gsc.* call failed with gsc_site_not_found or gsc_permission_denied and you need to re-check the exact property address.

Do not use when:
- You already have a site_url from this response in the same workflow.
- You need GA4 properties (ga4.list_properties) or ad accounts (ads.list_ad_accounts).

Output semantics:
- rows[].site_url is the exact property identifier Search Console expects: a URL-prefix property ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com'). Pass it verbatim — do not normalize slashes or strip the sc-domain: prefix.
- rows[].permission_level is the connected Google account's access level on the property (siteOwner, siteFullUser, siteRestrictedUser, or siteUnverifiedUser). siteUnverifiedUser rows will fail data reads with gsc_permission_denied.

Constraints:
- Rows are scoped to the caller's organization and active brand grants; a property absent from this response is not queryable by other gsc actions.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Max rows. Default 100, hard cap 500.

actions.description: Top pages by Google Search organic performance for a property over a date range; returns page rows with clicks, impressions, CTR, and position.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Convenience wrapper over searchAnalytics.query with dimensions=[page] — which URLs earned organic impressions and clicks.

Use when:
- The user asks which pages perform best in Google Search, top landing pages from organic, or per-URL organic metrics.

Do not use when:
- You need page x query combinations: use query_search_analytics.
- You need on-site engagement per page (views, bounce): use ga4.list_top_pages.

Workflow:
1. Call list_sites if site_url is unknown.
2. Call this action with site_url, start_date, end_date, optional limit.

Output semantics:
- rows[].page is the full canonical URL; rows are sorted by clicks descending.
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Search analytics data lags ~2 days behind real time.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 100, hard cap 25000.

actions.description: Top Google Search queries for a property over a date range; returns query rows with clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Convenience wrapper over searchAnalytics.query with dimensions=[query] — which search terms drove organic impressions and clicks.

Use when:
- The user asks what people searched to find the site, top keywords, or organic query performance.

Do not use when:
- You need query x page or query x country breakdowns: use query_search_analytics with multiple dimensions.
- You need paid keyword data: use ads.* actions.

Workflow:
1. Call list_sites if site_url is unknown.
2. Call this action with site_url, start_date, end_date, optional limit.

Output semantics:
- Rows are sorted by clicks descending; ctr is a 0-1 ratio; position is the average result position (lower is better).
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Search analytics data lags ~2 days behind real time.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: limit
input_params.description: Row cap. Default 100, hard cap 25000.

actions.description: Generic Search Console searchAnalytics.query for one property; returns clicks, impressions, CTR, and position grouped by any dimension combination.
actions.long_description:
Primary use:
Full-control Google Search organic performance query — the generic pass-through to searchAnalytics.query. Prefer the convenience actions (list_top_queries, list_top_pages, get_daily_trend) for single-dimension questions; use this one for multi-dimension grouping, filters, or non-web search types.

Use when:
- You need multiple dimensions at once (e.g. query x page, date x device).
- You need dimension_filter_groups (e.g. only queries containing a brand term, only one country) or a non-default search_type / data_state / aggregation_type.

Do not use when:
- A convenience action answers the question with fewer knobs.
- You need paid-search data (ads.*) or on-site analytics (ga4.*): Search Console covers Google ORGANIC search only.

Constraints:
- Valid dimensions: country, device, page, query, searchAppearance, date.
- Valid search_type values: web, image, video, news, discover, googleNews (default web).
- data_state 'all' includes fresh (still-consolidating) data; 'final' (default upstream) returns only finalized data.
- row_limit defaults to 100 and is hard-capped at 25000 (the API maximum per request); page beyond that with start_row.
- Requests run through a proxy that cuts a single call at ~30s. Large result sets must be paginated by the caller: keep row_limit bounded and walk pages with start_row instead of asking for everything at once.
- site_url must match a property EXACTLY as Search Console knows it: a URL-prefix property is a full origin like 'https://example.com/' (trailing slash included), while a site verified as a DOMAIN property is addressed as 'sc-domain:example.com' — never as a URL-prefix. When a call fails with gsc_permission_denied or gsc_site_not_found, re-check the exact site_url against gsc.list_sites before retrying.

Output semantics:
- rows[].keys aligns positionally with the requested dimensions.
- ctr is a 0-1 ratio; position is the average result position (lower is better).
- Rows are sorted by clicks descending (API default). Empty rows means no organic data for the range/filters, not an error.

input_params.name: site_url
input_params.description: Property identifier from gsc.list_sites.rows[].site_url — a URL-prefix ('https://example.com/') or a domain property ('sc-domain:example.com').

input_params.name: start_date
input_params.description: Inclusive start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Search analytics data lags ~2 days behind real time.

input_params.name: end_date
input_params.description: Inclusive end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

input_params.name: dimensions
input_params.description: Dimensions to group by, in order. Valid values: country, device, page, query, searchAppearance, date. Omit for property-level totals.

input_params.name: search_type
input_params.description: Search surface to report on. Default web.

input_params.name: row_limit
input_params.description: Rows per page. Default 100, hard cap 25000 (API maximum).

input_params.name: start_row
input_params.description: Zero-based row offset for pagination. Default 0.

input_params.name: data_state
input_params.description: 'all' includes fresh (not yet finalized) data; 'final' returns only finalized data (upstream default).

input_params.name: dimension_filter_groups
input_params.description: searchAnalytics.query dimensionFilterGroups passed through verbatim: a group object (or list of groups) with filters of {dimension, operator, expression}. Operators: equals, notEquals, contains, notContains, includingRegex, excludingRegex.

input_params.name: aggregation_type
input_params.description: How metrics aggregate: auto (default), byProperty, or byPage. byProperty is unavailable for page/searchAppearance dimensions.

actions.description: Execute an authorized BYO MCP read tool through Gateway.
actions.long_description: Execute a BYO MCP tool only when integration/list_tools marks that exact tool dispatch_action as integration/byo_mcp_read_tool. Do not use this for write, unreviewed, unknown, hidden, or drifted tools.

input_params.name: package_slug
input_params.description: Published BYO MCP package slug.

input_params.name: tool_name
input_params.description: BYO MCP tool name.

input_params.name: credential_id
input_params.description: Org credential id.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments.

actions.output_shape: BYO MCP worker result envelope with trace and snapshot binding.

actions.description: Request HITL approval for a BYO MCP write tool.
actions.long_description: Use this for BYO MCP tools that integration/list_tools marks requires_review=true. This creates a HITL approval request and binds the current package snapshot, credential/resource grant, and tool arguments.

input_params.name: package_slug
input_params.description: Published BYO MCP package slug.

input_params.name: tool_name
input_params.description: BYO MCP tool name.

input_params.name: credential_id
input_params.description: Org credential id.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments.

input_params.name: chat_id
input_params.description: Current chat id for HITL review.

input_params.name: _summary
input_params.description: Human-readable review summary.

actions.output_shape: BYO MCP worker result envelope after approved replay.

actions.description: List published BYO MCP packages, tools, and credential grants for this brand.
actions.long_description: DB-only BYO MCP discovery. This action does not call the upstream MCP endpoint and does not execute a tool. Use it before BYO MCP tasks to find the exact package_slug, tool_name, input_schema, dispatch_action, and credential_id/resource grant available to the current brand.

input_params.name: package_slug
input_params.description: Optional published BYO MCP package slug.

input_params.name: tool_name
input_params.description: Optional exact BYO MCP tool name.

actions.output_shape: packages[].tools[] plus credentials[] grant bindings for BYO MCP runtime calls.

actions.description: List the PostHog projects this brand can query.
actions.long_description: Returns the PostHog projects granted to the current brand (project_id + name). START HERE for any PostHog task: every other posthog action requires one of these project_ids. Do NOT ask the user for a project id — this action is the source of truth. (Upstream PostHog project listing is org-wide and not available.)

actions.description: List the PostHog analytics tools available for a project.
actions.long_description: Returns the live PostHog MCP tool catalog for one granted project, each annotated with `requires_review`. The default response is a slim catalog without input schemas. Pass `tool` to fetch one exact tool's full input schema before calling `posthog/query`. Tools with `requires_review=true` (writes, or anything not on our read-only allowlist) must go through `posthog/request_change`, not `posthog/query`.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: PostHog project id (from posthog/list_projects).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact PostHog tool name; when provided, returns that tool's full input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.project_id, data.tools[].{name, description, requires_review[, input_schema when tool is provided]}

actions.description: Run a read-only PostHog analytics tool (trends, funnels, HogQL, etc.).
actions.long_description: Forwards a read-only PostHog tool call for one granted project. Common tools: `insight-query` (trends / funnels / retention), `execute-sql` (HogQL — read-only, validated), `read-data-schema` (use before writing HogQL to get real field names). Get the project_id from `posthog/list_projects` (never ask the user for it). Only allowlisted read tools are accepted here; a write or unvetted tool returns `requires_review` — call `posthog/request_change` for those. The response echoes which project was queried.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: PostHog project id (from posthog/list_projects).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact PostHog tool name from posthog/list_tools.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured, truncated}

actions.description: Request a PostHog write (feature flag / experiment / survey) via human approval.
actions.long_description: Submits a PostHog write or non-allowlisted tool call for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the call is forwarded to PostHog only after a human approves. Use this for anything `posthog/query` rejected as requires_review.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: PostHog project id (from posthog/list_projects).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact PostHog tool name (e.g. a create/update tool).

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured}

actions.description: Delete a file-based SEO page's whole folder (index.html + page.json).
actions.long_description: Removes /brand/seo-hosting/pages/{section}/{slug}/ entirely. The slug frees up for reuse. Page-local files are deleted; CDN assets uploaded via upload_asset are not auto-purged.

input_params.name: section
input_params.description: Content section — must be one of the brand's catalog sections (see get_sections).

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Page slug (the folder name under the section).

actions.description: Read the brand's section catalog (the content sections, i.e. the first URL path segments pages can live under). Returns whether it is established and, if not, discovered seed sections.
actions.long_description: Returns { established, sections: [{key,label,order}], discovered }. When not established (no catalog yet), 'discovered' lists section folders that already hold pages as a seed hint. Establish/replace the catalog with put_sections before authoring pages — put_page/publish_page reject sections not in the catalog.
actions.output_shape: { established, sections: [{ key, label, order }], discovered: [str] }

actions.description: Where this brand's SEO pages will be served: connected custom domains, live state, URL contract (subdomain vs subdirectory), served sections, the asset CDN base URL, and the /brand/seo-hosting workspace dir.
actions.long_description: Call FIRST when authoring SEO pages. Returns each domain connection (hostname/method/status/live/served_sections/public_base_url), the allowed sections (the brand's catalog), sections_established, and where to write files. If sections_established is false, call put_sections to establish the catalog before put_page/publish_page. Pages only go public once a connection is live for their section.
actions.output_shape: { domains: [{ hostname, method, status, live, url_contract, served_sections, public_base_url }], allowed_sections, sections_established, asset_cdn_base_url, workspace_dir, note }

actions.description: List this brand's file-based SEO pages (from page.json), newest first. Use before authoring to avoid duplicates and find internal-link targets.
actions.long_description: Returns page summaries (section/slug/title/status/url_path/published_at) parsed from each page's page.json under /brand/seo-hosting/pages. Filter by 'section' / 'status'.

input_params.name: section
input_params.description: Content section filter (one of the brand's catalog sections).

input_params.name: status
input_params.description: Filter by lifecycle status.

actions.output_shape: { items: [{ section, slug, title, status, url_path, ... }], count }

actions.description: Publish a file-based SEO page after light validation (no custom JS, page.json fields). Flips page.json to published. Returns advisory link_warnings (dead internal links) that do NOT block publishing.
actions.long_description: Reads the index.html + page.json you wrote under /brand/seo-hosting/pages/{section}/{slug}/, validates them, and on success sets status=published (stamps publishedAt on first publish). Returns the public_url + whether it is actually served (depends on a live domain connection). 422 with the issues if validation fails.

input_params.name: section
input_params.description: Content section — must be one of the brand's catalog sections (see get_sections).

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Page slug (the folder name under the section).

actions.description: Create or overwrite a file-based SEO page's HTML + metadata as a draft (API write path for external callers like the Soku CLI; the in-product agent writes files directly). Call publish_page after to go live.
actions.long_description: Writes index.html + page.json under /brand/seo-hosting/pages/{section}/{slug}/. The section must already exist in the brand's catalog (get_sections / put_sections) — otherwise this returns sections_not_established / unknown_section. Always saved as draft — publish_page is the validation gate (no custom JS) before it serves publicly. Reference assets uploaded via upload_asset by absolute URL.

input_params.name: section
input_params.description: Content section — must be one of the brand's catalog sections (see get_sections).

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Page slug (folder name under the section).

input_params.name: title
input_params.description: Page title (used in <title> / listings).

input_params.name: html
input_params.description: The full HTML document (no custom <script>).

input_params.name: description
input_params.description: Meta description.

input_params.name: template
input_params.description: Template name this page was built from.

input_params.name: seo
input_params.description: SEO overrides (canonical / ogImage / keywords).

actions.description: Establish or replace the brand's section catalog (the COMPLETE list of content sections). Required before authoring pages in a new section.
actions.long_description: Writes sections.json (the full catalog). 'sections' is the complete list of { key, label, order? } — keys are lowercase [a-z0-9-], unique, not reserved, max 20. Removing a section that still has PUBLISHED pages requires force=true (otherwise rejected to avoid orphaning live pages). There is no default catalog — a brand has no sections until this is called.

input_params.name: sections
input_params.description: The complete catalog: [{ key, label, order? }]. key is the URL segment (lowercase a-z0-9-), label is the display name.

input_params.name: force
input_params.description: Allow removing a section that still has published pages.

actions.output_shape: { established: true, sections: [{ key, label, order }] }

actions.description: Re-deploy a connected subdirectory (T4 worker/vercel) domain with its CURRENT sections and re-verify — recovers a broken connection or re-applies after an external change. Does NOT change which sections are mounted.
actions.long_description: Re-uploads the Worker (or rebuilds the Vercel rules) and reconciles routes to the connection's current mounted_sections, then runs verification. Idempotent; reuses the stored provider token. Identify the domain by its hostname (from get_status). To change which sections are served, use set_domain_sections instead.

input_params.name: hostname
input_params.description: The connected domain hostname (from get_status).

actions.description: Set which sections a connected subdirectory (T4 worker/vercel) domain serves, then re-provision + re-verify so the live worker/routes match. Use after adding a catalog section that an already-connected T4 domain should start serving (T3/CNAME serves the whole catalog automatically).
actions.long_description: Updates the connection's mounted_sections to the given set and re-deploys (worker: re-upload script + add/prune routes; vercel: rebuild rules), then runs verification. Sections must be a subset of the brand's catalog (see get_sections). Reuses the domain's stored provider token — no token needed. Identify the domain by its hostname (from get_status). CNAME subdomains have no per-section mounts. To re-deploy without changing sections, use reprovision_domain.

input_params.name: hostname
input_params.description: The connected domain hostname (from get_status).

input_params.name: sections
input_params.description: The complete set of catalog sections this domain should serve.

actions.description: Unpublish a file-based SEO page (back to draft; stops serving).
actions.long_description: Sets the page's page.json status back to 'draft'. The page stops being served publicly but its files remain for further editing. Reversible via publish_page.

input_params.name: section
input_params.description: Content section — must be one of the brand's catalog sections (see get_sections).

input_params.name: slug
input_params.description: Page slug (the folder name under the section).

actions.description: Upload an image / theme.css / font to the public CDN and get a stable absolute URL to reference from page HTML (HTML+CSS only; no JS assets).
actions.long_description: Writes asset bytes to the public asset CDN under the brand's namespace and returns { url }. Provide the bytes via EITHER 'source_path' (a file you already wrote under your /brand/seo-hosting/ mount, e.g. 'pages/blog/best-crm/hero.png' — preferred in-sandbox, no base64) OR 'content_base64' (inline base64, for callers without the mount). 'path' is the relative CDN path like 'blog/best-crm/hero.png' (page asset) or '_templates/article/theme.css' (shared template asset). Allowed types: images / css / fonts. Reference the returned URL absolutely in your HTML. Content-Type is derived from the file extension. Assets are not auto-deleted with pages.

input_params.name: path
input_params.description: Relative CDN asset path, e.g. 'blog/{slug}/hero.png'.

input_params.name: source_path
input_params.description: Path to a file already written under your /brand/seo-hosting/ mount (e.g. 'pages/blog/x/hero.png'). Avoids base64. Provide this OR 'content_base64'.

input_params.name: content_base64
input_params.description: Base64-encoded file bytes. Provide this OR 'source_path'.

actions.description: List the Stripe accounts this brand can query.
actions.long_description: Returns the Stripe accounts granted to the current brand (account_id + name). START HERE for any Stripe task: every other stripe action requires one of these account_ids. Do NOT ask the user for an account id — this action is the source of truth.

actions.description: List the Stripe payment/billing tools available for an account.
actions.long_description: Returns the live Stripe MCP tool catalog for one granted account, each annotated with `requires_review`. The default response is a slim catalog without input schemas. Pass `tool` to fetch one exact tool's full input schema before calling `stripe/query`. Tools with `requires_review=true` (writes like refunds or payment links, or anything not on our read-only allowlist) must go through `stripe/request_change`, not `stripe/query`. Data is live from Stripe (realtime, no cached pipeline).

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Stripe account id (from stripe/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Stripe tool name; when provided, returns that tool's full input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.project_id, data.tools[].{name, description, requires_review[, input_schema when tool is provided]}

actions.description: Run a read-only Stripe tool (balance, customers, invoices, subscriptions, etc.).
actions.long_description: Forwards a read-only Stripe tool call for one granted account and returns live data (realtime — there is no cached sync pipeline). Current hosted MCP exposes generic API reads through `stripe_api_read` (for example GET `/v1/balance`, `/v1/customers`, `/v1/invoices`, `/v1/subscriptions`, `/v1/payment_intents`) plus `get_stripe_account_info` and `search_stripe_resources`. Get the account_id from `stripe/list_accounts` (never ask the user for it). Only allowlisted read tools run immediately; a write or unvetted tool returns `requires_review` — call `stripe/request_change` for those. The response echoes which account was queried.

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Stripe account id (from stripe/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Stripe tool name from stripe/list_tools.

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured, truncated}

actions.description: Request a Stripe write (refund / payment link / coupon) via human approval.
actions.long_description: Submits a Stripe write or non-allowlisted tool call for human approval (HITL). Returns a pending_review_id immediately; the call is forwarded to Stripe only after a human approves. Use this for anything `stripe/query` rejected as requires_review (e.g. `create_refund`, `create_payment_link`).

input_params.name: project_id
input_params.description: Stripe account id (from stripe/list_accounts).

input_params.name: tool
input_params.description: Exact Stripe tool name (e.g. a create/update tool).

input_params.name: arguments
input_params.description: Tool arguments object per the tool's input schema.

actions.output_shape: 202 -> {ok, pending_review_id}; on approval data.{project_id, tool, is_error, content[], structured}
